   165→## Content Writing Guidelines

   167→### Opening Hook

   170→- Creates curiosity
   171→- Promises insight or solution

   176→### Section Structure

   179→- One main idea per section
   180→- Support with examples, images, or quotes

   182→### Visual Content

   186→- Include metrics when relevant

   193→- Forward-looking statement

   212→
   213→### Internal Linking
   214→- Related projects automatically linked

   216→- Build topic clusters naturally

   220→## Technical Implementation

   222→### Type Safety

   225→- Build-time validation of project references
   226→
   227→### Performance

   229→- Proper width/height attributes prevent layout shift
   230→- CDN-hosted assets with automatic optimization

   232→
   233→### Accessibility
   234→- Semantic HTML throughout
   235→- Proper heading hierarchy
   236→- Alt text required for images
   237→- Readable contrast ratios
   238→- Focus states on interactive elements

   241→
   242→## File Structure

1→# Cover Images Feature - Implementation Summary
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→I've successfully added support for optional cover images on all handbook pages. This feature allows you to add visual context and improve the presentation of handbook content.
     6→
     7→## What Changed
     8→
     9→### 1. Schema Update
    10→**File:** `src/content.config.ts`
    11→
    12→Added optional `coverImage` field to the handbook collection:
    13→```typescript
    14→coverImage: z.string().optional()
    15→```
    16→
    17→This field accepts full URLs (e.g., `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg`).
    18→
    19→### 2. UI Implementation
    20→**File:** `src/pages/handbook/[...slug].astro`
    21→
    22→Enhanced the detail page to display cover images:
    23→- Imports CDN helpers (`cdnUrl`, `cdnSrcset`, `DEFAULT_QUALITY`)
    24→- Conditionally renders cover image when `coverImage` field is present
    25→- Displays between page header and content
    26→- Optimized for performance with eager loading and high fetchpriority
    27→
    28→### 3. Documentation Updates
    29→
    30→Updated multiple documentation files:
    31→- `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` - Added handbook cover images section
    32→- `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` - Added cover image usage and guidelines
    33→- `docs/QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` - Added quick reference for cover images
    34→- `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` - Updated with cover image information
    35→
    36→### 4. Example Implementation
    37→**File:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
    38→
    39→Added cover image to demonstrate the feature:
    40→```yaml
    41→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
    42→```
    43→
    44→## How to Use
    45→
    46→### Adding a Cover Image
    47→
    48→Simply add the `coverImage` field to your handbook page frontmatter with a full URL:
    49→
    50→```yaml
    51→---
    52→title: Your Page Title
    53→description: Your description
    54→date: 2024-01-15
    55→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/your-image.jpg
    56→group: your-group
    57→order: 1
    58→---
    59→```
    60→
    61→### Image Guidelines
    62→
    63→**Dimensions:**
    64→- Recommended: 1200×630px or larger
    65→- Aspect ratio: 16:9 or 2:1 works best
    66→
    67→**Format:**
    68→- JPEG for photographs
    69→- PNG for graphics with transparency
    70→
    71→**URL Format:**
    72→- Use full URLs: `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/image-name.jpg`
    73→- External URLs also supported: `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz`
    74→- Any public image URL works
    75→
    76→**File Size:**
    77→- Optimize before uploading
    78→- CDN images will be automatically optimized further with quality settings
    79→- External URLs are used as-is (no optimization applied)
    80→
    81→## Display Behavior
    82→
    83→### Visual Presentation
    84→- Full-width within content area (max 840px on desktop)
    85→- Rounded corners (8px border-radius)
    86→- Positioned between page header and content
    87→- Overflow hidden for clean edges
    88→
    89→### Performance Optimization
    90→- **Eager loading:** `loading="eager"`
    91→- **High priority:** `fetchpriority="high"`
    92→- **Sync decoding:** `decoding="sync"`
    93→- **Responsive srcset:** Automatically generated for multiple screen sizes
    94→- **Optimized for LCP:** Cover images load immediately for better Core Web Vitals
    95→
    96→### Responsive Behavior
    97→```html
    98→sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 840px"
    99→```
   100→- Mobile: Full viewport width
   101→- Desktop: Constrained to content width (840px)
   102→
   103→## Technical Details
   104→
   105→### URL Handling
   106→
   107→The `cdnUrl()` helper function handles all URL formats:
   108→- **CDN URLs** (`https://cdn.significa.co/...`) → Adds optimization parameters
   109→- **External URLs** → Used as-is without modification
   110→- **Relative paths** (legacy) → Prepends CDN hostname
   111→
   112→### CDN Integration
   113→
   114→Cover images leverage the existing CDN infrastructure:
   115→- Served from `https://cdn.significa.co`
   116→- Automatic srcset generation via `cdnSrcset()` (CDN URLs only)
   117→- Quality optimization via `cdnUrl()` with width and quality parameters
   118→- Format auto-negotiation (WebP/AVIF based on browser support)
   119→- External URLs bypass srcset generation (used directly)
   120→
   121→### Build-Time Validation
   122→
   123→- Schema validates `coverImage` is a string (if provided)
   124→- Invalid paths don't break the build (handled gracefully at runtime)
   125→- Missing images won't cause build failures
   126→- Type-safe with TypeScript strict mode
   127→
   128→## Examples
   129→
   130→### With Cover Image
   131→```yaml
   132→---
   133→title: Code of Ethics
   134→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
   135→date: 2024-01-15
   136→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
   137→group: knowing-significa
   138→order: 1
   139→---
   140→```
   141→
   142→### External URL Example
   143→```yaml
   144→---
   145→title: Design Principles
   146→description: How we approach design.
   147→date: 2024-01-20
   148→coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1234567890
   149→group: design
   150→order: 1
   151→---
   152→```
   153→
   154→### Without Cover Image
   155→```yaml
   156→---
   157→title: Manifesto
   158→description: What we believe in.
   159→date: 2024-01-10
   160→group: knowing-significa
   161→order: 2
   162→---
   163→```
   164→
   165→Both work perfectly fine. The cover image is completely optional.
   166→
   167→## Testing
   168→
   169→**Validation:**
   170→- ✅ `pnpm check` - 0 errors, 0 warnings
   171→- ✅ `pnpm build` - 26 pages built successfully
   172→- ✅ Types generated correctly
   173→- ✅ Cover image displays on Code of Ethics page
   174→
   175→**Test URLs:**
   176→- Index: http://localhost:4322/handbook
   177→- With cover: http://localhost:4322/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics
   178→- Without cover: http://localhost:4322/handbook/manifesto
   179→
   180→## Usage Recommendations
   181→
   182→### When to Use Cover Images
   183→
   184→✅ **Good use cases:**
   185→- Visual context for the content (e.g., team photo for culture page)
   186→- Setting the tone (e.g., ethics imagery for Code of Ethics)
   187→- Breaking up long handbook sections
   188→- Creating visual distinction between major sections
   189→
   190→❌ **Avoid:**
   191→- Adding images just because you can
   192→- Using low-quality or irrelevant images
   193→- Heavy file sizes (optimize first!)
   194→- Images that don't add value to the content
   195→
   196→### Best Practices
   197→
   198→1. **Keep it relevant** - Cover should relate to page content
   199→2. **Optimize first** - Upload optimized images (CDN will optimize further)
   200→3. **Consistent aspect ratio** - Use 16:9 or 2:1 across handbook
   201→4. **Accessible imagery** - Choose clear, professional images
   202→5. **Optional is OK** - Not every page needs a cover image
   203→
   204→## Architecture Notes
   205→
   206→### Why Not Use MediaImage Component?
   207→
   208→Cover images are rendered directly (not through `MediaImage`) for several reasons:
   209→- **Performance:** Direct rendering with eager loading for LCP optimization
   210→- **Simplicity:** No need for width/height/alt props in frontmatter
   211→- **Decorative:** Cover images are decorative (no alt text needed)
   212→- **Consistency:** Handbook pages have consistent layout constraints
   213→
   214→### CDN Helper Functions Used
   215→
   216→```typescript
   217→cdnUrl(src, { width: 1200, quality: DEFAULT_QUALITY })
   218→cdnSrcset(src, { quality: DEFAULT_QUALITY })
   219→```
   220→
   221→These generate optimized URLs and responsive srcsets automatically.
   222→
   223→## Future Enhancements (Optional)
   224→
   225→If needed in the future, you could add:
   226→- Different aspect ratios per page
   227→- Image captions for covers
   228→- Parallax or zoom effects
   229→- Dark mode variants
   230→- Focal point control
   231→
   232→But the current implementation is intentionally minimal and focused on core use cases.
   233→
   234→## Summary
   235→
   236→Cover images are now fully supported on all handbook pages:
   237→- ✅ Optional field in schema
   238→- ✅ Automatic CDN optimization
   239→- ✅ Performance-optimized rendering
   240→- ✅ Responsive and accessible
   241→- ✅ Fully documented
   242→✅ **Example implementation included**
   243→
   244→Just add `coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/your-image.jpg` (or any public URL) to any handbook page frontmatter and it works!

1→# Homepage Restructure Documentation
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→The homepage has been completely restructured to align with the new content strategy outlined in Notion. The new structure provides a clearer narrative flow, better showcases services, and creates more engagement opportunities.
     6→
     7→## Key Changes
     8→
     9→### 1. New Hero Section
    10→- **Before:** Long tagline with muted text and inline CTA
    11→- **After:** Simple, bold statement: "We are a **design and development agency** building digital products that work beautifully."
    12→- More concise and impactful first impression
    13→
    14→### 2. Content Flow Reorganization
    15→
    16→The new homepage follows this narrative structure:
    17→
    18→1. **Hero** - Bold positioning statement
    19→2. **Showreel** - Visual showcase (maintained from original)
    20→3. **Strategy Statement** - "From strategy to code..." positioning
    21→4. **Selected Highlights** - Dynamic content from highlights collection (replaces hardcoded featured projects)
    22→5. **Service Pillars Intro** - Overview of Think/Design/Develop/Launch/Scale approach
    23→6. **Service Pillars Detail** - Expandable descriptions of each pillar
    24→7. **Team CTA** - "We are a team of designers, developers..."
    25→8. **Blog Section** - "We think things through" with 3 recent posts
    26→9. **Impact Section** - B Corp and sustainability messaging
    27→10. **Playground Section** - Open source contributions
    28→11. **Social Proof** - Customer testimonials and satisfaction stats
    29→12. **Awards** - International recognition
    30→13. **Final CTA** - "We are ready to grow your business"
    31→14. **FAQs** - Common questions answered
    32→15. **Client Logos** - Trust indicators
    33→
    34→### 3. Dynamic Content via Highlights Collection
    35→
    36→The homepage now uses the `highlights` collection instead of hardcoded featured projects:
    37→
    38→```astro
    39→const highlights = await getResolvedHighlights();
    40→const featuredHighlights = highlights.slice(0, 3);
    41→```
    42→
    43→This allows the marketing team to:
    44→- Control which content appears on the homepage via YAML files
    45→- Mix projects, blog posts, and playground items
    46→- Add custom labels to each highlight
    47→- Manage order without touching code
    48→
    49→### 4. Service Pillars
    50→
    51→New comprehensive service section with 5 pillars:
    52→- **Think** - Strategy and planning
    53→- **Design** - UI/UX and design systems
    54→- **Develop** - Engineering and code
    55→- **Launch** - Deployment and handover
    56→- **Scale** - Growth and optimization
    57→
    58→Each pillar has a title and description, presented in a responsive grid.
    59→
    60→### 5. New Sections Added
    61→
    62→#### Impact Section
    63→- Highlights B Corp certification
    64→- 1% for the Planet membership
    65→- Ethical and sustainable approach
    66→- Links to `/impact` page
    67→
    68→#### Playground Section
    69→- Shows 3 recent open source projects
    70→- Separate from blog content
    71→- Links to `/playground` listing page
    72→
    73→#### FAQs Section
    74→- 5 common questions with answers
    75→- Helps with SEO and reduces friction
    76→- Can be easily expanded
    77→
    78→#### Final CTA
    79→- Clear call to action before footer
    80→- Option to continue browsing or get in touch
    81→
    82→### 6. Improved Blog Section
    83→
    84→- Now labeled "We think things through"
    85→- Shows 3 most recent posts (reduced from 4)
    86→- Better card layout with thumbnails
    87→- Includes post descriptions
    88→- Date formatting improved
    89→
    90→### 7. Awards Redesign
    91→
    92→- Changed heading from "Ah, awards..." to "International recognition"
    93→- Card-based layout instead of list
    94→- Limited to 18 most recent awards
    95→- Better visual hierarchy
    96→- Shows award name, year, and project
    97→
    98→### 8. Removed Sections
    99→
   100→- **Industries list** - Removed hardcoded list
   101→- **Capabilities list** - Removed hardcoded list
   102→- **Team photo** - Removed from homepage (can be added to About page)
   103→- **Recent articles highlights** - Replaced with dedicated blog section
   104→
   105→### 9. CSS Improvements
   106→
   107→All styles are scoped and follow design token conventions:
   108→
   109→- Consistent spacing using `var(--gutter)`
   110→- Responsive grid layouts with `auto-fit` and `minmax`
   111→- Proper semantic HTML (`<section>`, `<article>`, `<h2>`, etc.)
   112→- Hover states and transitions
   113→- Mobile-first responsive design
   114→- Accessible focus states
   115→
   116→### 10. CTA Components
   117→
   118→Three standardized CTA button styles:
   119→
   120→- `.cta-link` - Inline text link with arrow
   121→- `.cta-button` - Primary filled button
   122→- `.cta-button-outline` - Secondary outlined button
   123→- `.cta-button-large` - Hero-sized button
   124→
   125→All with consistent hover effects (opacity + translateY).
   126→
   127→## Technical Implementation
   128→
   129→### Data Fetching
   130→
   131→```astro
   132→const clients = await getClients();
   133→const testimonials = await getTestimonials();
   134→const recentPosts = (await getPosts()).slice(0, 3);
   135→const highlights = await getResolvedHighlights();
   136→const awards = (await getAwards()).slice(0, 18);
   137→const playgroundProjects = (await getPlayground()).slice(0, 3);
   138→```
   139→
   140→### Type Safety
   141→
   142→The highlights section handles different collection types safely:
   143→
   144→```typescript
   145→// Get thumbnail based on collection type
   146→let thumbnail: string | undefined;
   147→if (highlight.collection === "projects") {
   148→  thumbnail = (entry.data as any).thumbnail || (entry.data as any).heroImage;
   149→} else {
   150→  thumbnail = (entry.data as any).thumbnail;
   151→}
   152→```
   153→
   154→Uses type assertions to access properties that exist on different schemas.
   155→
   156→### SEO Improvements
   157→
   158→- Updated page title: "Significa — Design and Development Agency"
   159→- Improved meta description
   160→- Better heading hierarchy
   161→- Semantic HTML structure
   162→- FAQ section for rich snippets potential
   163→
   164→## Content Management
   165→
   166→### To Feature Content on Homepage
   167→
   168→1. Create a YAML file in `src/content/highlights/`
   169→2. Reference a project, blog post, or playground item:
   170→

   173→source:
   174→  collection: "projects"
   175→  entry: "mishmash"
   176→order: 1
   177→```
   178→
   179→3. Build validates references at compile time

   181→### To Update Service Pillars

   184→
   185→### To Update FAQs
   186→
   187→Edit the hardcoded FAQ items in `src/pages/index.astro` around line 366-429.
   188→
   189→## Next Steps / Recommendations
   190→
   191→1. **Create missing pages** referenced by CTAs:
   192→   - `/services` - Service details page
   193→   - `/impact` - Impact and sustainability page
   194→   - Consider `/work-with-us` improvements
   195→
   196→2. **Add analytics** to track:
   197→   - CTA click rates
   198→   - Scroll depth
   199→   - Section engagement
   200→   - Showreel video plays
   201→
   202→3. **Consider adding:**

   204→   - Interactive service pillar expansion
   205→   - More case study metrics
   206→   - Video testimonials
   207→
   208→4. **Content to prepare:**
   209→   - Populate `highlights` collection YAML files
   210→   - Add thumbnails to blog posts if missing
   211→   - Add thumbnails to playground projects if missing
   212→   - Review and update FAQ answers
   213→
   214→5. **Performance optimizations:**
   215→   - Add `loading="eager"` to above-fold images
   216→   - Consider image format optimization (WebP/AVIF)
   217→   - Implement lazy loading for awards section
   218→
   219→## Migration Notes
   220→
   221→### Breaking Changes
   222→- Homepage structure completely changed
   223→- Old sections removed (industries, capabilities lists)
   224→- Featured projects now managed via highlights collection
   225→
   226→### Backwards Compatibility
   227→- All content collections unchanged

   229→- CDN utility unchanged

   231→
   232→## Testing Checklist
   233→
   234→- [ ] Check all internal links work
   235→- [ ] Verify showreel video plays correctly

   245→## Design Token Usage

1→# Parent-Child Page Structure - Implementation Summary
     2→
     3→## What Was Done
     4→
     5→I've implemented and tested a parent-child page structure for the handbook collection, plus added support for optional cover images on all handbook pages.
     6→
     7→**Example created:**
     8→- Parent: `/handbook/mission-and-values`
     9→- Child: `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics` (with cover image)
    10→
    11→## How It Works
    12→
    13→### File System Based Hierarchy
    14→
    15→The relationship between parent and child pages is determined by **file location**, not frontmatter fields.
    16→
    17→```
    18→src/content/handbook/
    19→├── mission-and-values.mdx              → Parent page
    20→└── mission-and-values/                 → Directory for children
    21→    └── code-of-ethics.mdx              → Child page
    22→```
    23→
    24→**Key Rules:**
    25→1. Parent page: `mission-and-values.mdx`
    26→2. Directory for children: `mission-and-values/` (exact same name)
    27→3. Child pages: Any `.mdx` files inside that directory
    28→
    29→### URLs Generated
    30→
    31→- Parent: `https://significa.co/handbook/mission-and-values`
    32→- Child: `https://significa.co/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`
    33→
    34→## Files Created
    35→
    36→### 1. Child Page Content
    37→**Location:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
    38→
    39→Contains frontmatter and content for the Code of Ethics page. Uses the same schema as any handbook page:
    40→- `title`, `description`, `date`, `group`, `order`
    41→- `coverImage` (optional) - demonstrates the new cover image feature
    42→- No special "parent" field needed
    43→
    44→### 2. Updated Schema
    45→**Location:** `src/content.config.ts`
    46→
    47→Added optional `coverImage` field to the handbook collection schema:
    48→- Accepts CDN paths (e.g., `/handbook/my-image.jpg`)
    49→- Validated at build time
    50→- Fully integrated with existing CDN infrastructure
    51→
    52→### 3. Updated Detail Page
    53→**Location:** `src/pages/handbook/[...slug].astro`
    54→
    55→Enhanced to display cover images:
    56→- Imports CDN helpers for image optimization
    57→- Conditionally renders cover between header and content
    58→- Responsive srcset generation
    59→- Eager loading with high priority for LCP optimization
    60→
    61→### 4. Documentation
    62→**Location:** `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`
    63→
    64→Complete guide covering:
    65→- How the file system hierarchy works
    66→- Helper functions available
    67→- UI behavior (index, detail pages, navigation)
    68→- Cover image usage and guidelines
    69→- Common patterns and examples
    70→- Gotchas and validation rules
    71→
    72→## UI Features Already Working
    73→
    74→The handbook pages already had full support for this pattern:
    75→
    76→### Index Page (`/handbook`)
    77→- Groups pages by category
    78→- Shows parent pages with their children indented
    79→- Visual hierarchy with borders and spacing
    80→
    81→### Detail Pages (`/handbook/[...slug]`)
    82→- **Cover image** displayed at top when `coverImage` field is present (optional)
    83→- **Breadcrumbs** for child pages (e.g., "Handbook › Mission and Values › Code of Ethics")
    84→- **Sidebar navigation** showing full hierarchy
    85→- **"In this section" box** at bottom of parent pages listing all children
    86→- Collapsible navigation that expands when viewing parent or child
    87→
    88→## Helper Functions Available
    89→
    90→Located in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
    91→
    92→```ts
    93→// Get all direct children of a parent
    94→await getHandbookChildren("mission-and-values")
    95→
    96→// Get the parent of a child page
    97→await getHandbookParent(entry)
    98→
    99→// Get breadcrumb trail
   100→await getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry)
   101→
   102→// Get full grouped index structure
   103→await getHandbookIndex()
   104→```
   105→
   106→## Build Validation
   107→
   108→The system validates at build time:
   109→- ✅ All references are valid
   110→- ✅ No orphaned directories
   111→- ✅ No broken links
   112→- ✅ Build fails if structure is invalid
   113→
   114→**Test results:**
   115→- `pnpm check` → 0 errors, 0 warnings
   116→- `pnpm build` → 26 pages built successfully
   117→- Cover image integration validated
   118→
   119→## Creating More Parent-Child Relationships
   120→
   121→### Step-by-Step Process
   122→
   123→1. **Create or identify the parent page:**
   124→   ```bash
   125→   # Parent already exists or create it:
   126→   touch src/content/handbook/parent-name.mdx
   127→   ```
   128→
   129→2. **Create directory for children:**
   130→   ```bash
   131→   mkdir src/content/handbook/parent-name
   132→   ```
   133→
   134→3. **Add child pages:**
   135→   ```bash
   136→   touch src/content/handbook/parent-name/child-1.mdx
   137→   touch src/content/handbook/parent-name/child-2.mdx
   138→   ```
   139→
   140→4. **Write frontmatter (same schema for all):**
   141→   ```yaml
   142→   ---
   143→   title: Child Page Title
   144→   description: Short description
   145→   date: 2024-01-15
   146→   coverImage: /handbook/my-cover.jpg  # Optional
   147→   group: knowing-significa  # Must match parent's group
   148→   order: 1
   149→   ---
   150→   ```
   151→
   152→5. **Test:**
   153→   ```bash
   154→   pnpm check  # Validates content
   155→   pnpm build  # Ensures routes are generated
   156→   ```
   157→
   158→## Nesting Depth
   159→
   160→The system supports unlimited nesting:
   161→```
   162→parent.mdx
   163→parent/
   164→  child.mdx
   165→  child/
   166→    grandchild.mdx
   167→    grandchild/
   168→      great-grandchild.mdx
   169→```
   170→
   171→**Recommendation:** Keep it to 2-3 levels maximum for usability.
   172→
   173→## Extending to Other Collections
   174→
   175→This pattern can be applied to the `pages` collection if needed. You would:
   176→
   177→1. The file loader already supports nested structures (no changes needed in `content.config.ts`)
   178→2. Create similar helper functions in `src/lib/collections.ts`
   179→3. Update `src/pages/[...slug].astro` to display children
   180→4. Use the handbook implementation as a reference
   181→
   182→## Cover Images
   183→
   184→You can add an optional cover image to any handbook page (parent or child).
   185→
   186→### Usage
   187→
   188→Add the `coverImage` field to frontmatter:
   189→```yaml
   190→---
   191→title: Your Page Title
   192→coverImage: /handbook/your-image.jpg
   193→# ... other fields
   194→---
   195→```
   196→
   197→### Guidelines
   198→
   199→- **Path format:** CDN paths like `/handbook/image-name.jpg`
   200→- **Dimensions:** 1200×630px or larger recommended (16:9 or 2:1 ratio)
   201→- **Format:** JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics
   202→- **Optimization:** Automatic srcset generation and responsive sizing
   203→- **Loading:** Eager loading with high fetchpriority for optimal LCP
   204→- **Display:** Full-width within content area, 8px border-radius
   205→
   206→### CDN Integration
   207→
   208→Cover images use the existing CDN infrastructure:
   209→- Served from `https://cdn.significa.co`
   210→- Automatic quality optimization
   211→- Responsive srcsets for multiple screen sizes
   212→- No additional configuration needed
   213→
   214→## Important Notes
   215→
   216→- **Directory name must match filename exactly** (case-sensitive)
   217→- **Children should use same group as parent** for correct navigation
   218→- **Drafts work at any level** (`.draft.mdx` suffix)
   219→- **Renaming a parent requires renaming its directory** to maintain relationships
   220→- **No frontmatter changes needed for hierarchy** — it's all file system based
   221→- **Cover images are optional** — omit the field if not needed
   222→
   223→## Testing Your Setup
   224→
   225→Visit these URLs to see it working:
   226→
   227→1. **Index:** http://localhost:4322/handbook
   228→   - See "Mission and Values" with "Code of Ethics" nested below
   229→
   230→2. **Parent:** http://localhost:4322/handbook/mission-and-values
   231→   - See "In this section" box listing child pages
   232→
   233→3. **Child:** http://localhost:4322/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics
   234→   - See cover image at top
   235→   - See breadcrumbs
   236→   - See sidebar showing hierarchy
   237→
   238→## Next Steps
   239→
   240→You can now create any parent-child structure you need:
   241→
   242→- Company policies → specific policy documents
   243→- Engineering practices → technology-specific guides  
   244→- Design system → component documentation
   245→- Any hierarchical content structure
   246→
   247→Just follow the file system pattern and add cover images where they enhance the content.
   248→
   249→## Summary of Changes
   250→
   251→### Schema Changes
   252→- Added `coverImage?: string` field to handbook collection
   253→
   254→### UI Changes  
   255→- Cover images display between header and content
   256→- Responsive with automatic srcset generation
   257→- Optimized for performance (eager loading, high priority)
   258→
   259→### Documentation Added
   260→- Complete cover image guidelines in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`
   261→- Quick reference in `docs/QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md`
   262→- Implementation summary in this file
   263→
   264→## Questions?
   265→
   266→Refer to `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` for detailed documentation, examples, and troubleshooting.

1→# Projects Page Redesign - Summary
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Redesigned the `/projects` page to match the current Significa.co design, featuring project thumbnails and award badges in a grid layout.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## What Changed
    10→
    11→### Before
    12→- Simple list layout
    13→- Text-only (titles and taglines)
    14→- No visual hierarchy
    15→- No awards displayed
    16→
    17→### After
    18→- Modern grid layout with thumbnails
    19→- Award badges for each project
    20→- Hover animations and interactions
    21→- Responsive design
    22→- Visual hierarchy matching Significa brand
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## File Updated
    27→
    28→**Location:** `src/pages/projects/index.astro`
    29→
    30→**Key Features:**
    31→1. **Grid Layout** — Responsive grid that adapts to screen size
    32→2. **Project Thumbnails** — CDN-optimized images with hover effects
    33→3. **Award Badges** — Display up to 5 awards per project
    34→4. **Hover Interactions** — Card lift, image zoom, badge color change
    35→5. **"View project →" CTA** — Clear call-to-action on each card
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Technical Implementation
    40→
    41→### Data Fetching
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→// Get all projects (sorted by date)
    45→const projects = await getProjects();
    46→
    47→// Get all awards
    48→const allAwards = await getCollection("awards");
    49→
    50→// Group awards by project ID
    51→const awardsByProject = new Map<string, Array<Award>>();
    52→```
    53→
    54→### Award Integration
    55→
    56→Awards are automatically fetched and grouped by project using the `reference()` system:
    57→- Awards reference projects via `project: reference("projects")`
    58→- Build-time validation ensures all references are valid
    59→- Each project card shows up to 5 awards
    60→
    61→### CDN Optimization
    62→
    63→Thumbnails are optimized via the CDN:
    64→```typescript
    65→cdnUrl(project.data.thumbnail, { width: 800, quality: 85 })
    66→```
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Design Features
    71→
    72→### Grid Layout
    73→- **Desktop:** Auto-fill grid with min 400px columns
    74→- **Mobile:** Single column layout
    75→- **Gap:** 3rem vertical, 2rem horizontal
    76→
    77→### Project Cards
    78→- **Aspect ratio:** 16:10 for thumbnails
    79→- **Border radius:** 12px on thumbnails, 6px on badges
    80→- **Hover effect:** -4px translateY (card lift)
    81→- **Image zoom:** 1.05 scale on hover
    82→
    83→### Award Badges
    84→- **Style:** Pill-shaped with border
    85→- **Typography:** Extra small (var(--text-xs))
    86→- **Hover:** Transform to primary color with white text
    87→- **Limit:** Maximum 5 badges displayed per project
    88→
    89→### Typography
    90→- **Title:** Extra large (var(--text-xl)) → Large on mobile
    91→- **Tagline:** Base size with muted color
    92→- **CTA:** Small, medium weight, primary color
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Responsive Behavior
    97→
    98→### Desktop (> 768px)
    99→- Multi-column grid (auto-fill)
   100→- Larger typography
   101→- 3rem vertical spacing
   102→
   103→### Mobile (≤ 768px)

   109→---
   110→
   111→## Accessibility

   122→
   123→## Performance

   125→### Optimizations Applied

   132→### Build Stats

   137→---
   138→
   139→## Example Data Structure

   141→### Project Card Display

   143→```

   145→│                             │

   147→│                             │
   148→├─────────────────────────────┤
   149→│ Project Title               │
   150→│ Tagline describing project  │
   151→│                             │
   152→│ [Award 1] [Award 2] [...]   │
   153→│                             │
   154→│ View project →              │
   155→└─────────────────────────────┘
   156→```
   157→
   158→### Awards Grouping
   159→
   160→For Mishmash project:
   161→- Red Dot (2025)
   162→- European Design Award (2025)
   163→- iF Design Award (2024)
   164→- German Design Award (2024)
   165→- Awwwards Distinction
   166→
   167→All displayed as badges below the tagline.
   168→
   169→---
   170→
   171→## Visual Hierarchy
   172→
   173→1. **Thumbnail** — Primary visual (largest element)
   174→2. **Title** — Main identifier (bold, large)
   175→3. **Tagline** — Context (muted, readable)
   176→4. **Awards** — Social proof (small badges)
   177→5. **CTA** — Action prompt (distinct color)
   178→
   179→---
   180→
   181→## Hover States
   182→
   183→### Card Level
   184→- Card lifts up 4px
   185→- Smooth transition (var(--duration-normal))
   186→
   187→### Thumbnail
   188→- Image scales to 1.05x
   189→- Overflow hidden maintains border radius
   190→
   191→### Award Badges
   192→- Background: Surface alt → Primary color
   193→- Border: Border color → Primary color
   194→- Text: Default → White
   195→
   196→### CTA
   197→- Color: Primary → Primary hover
   198→- Maintains its position at card bottom
   199→
   200→---
   201→
   202→## CSS Variables Used
   203→
   204→```css
   205→/* Layout */
   206→--max-width
   207→--gutter
   208→
   209→/* Typography */
   210→--text-3xl, --text-2xl, --text-xl, --text-lg, --text-base, --text-sm, --text-xs
   211→--weight-bold, --weight-semibold, --weight-medium
   212→--leading-tight, --leading-normal
   213→
   214→/* Colors */
   215→--color-text
   216→--color-text-muted
   217→--color-primary
   218→--color-primary-hover
   219→--color-surface-alt
   220→--color-border
   221→
   222→/* Animation */
   223→--duration-normal, --duration-fast
   224→--ease-out
   225→```
   226→
   227→---
   228→
   229→## Grid Behavior
   230→
   231→### Auto-fill Magic
   232→
   233→```css
   234→grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(min(100%, 400px), 1fr));
   235→```
   236→
   237→This creates:
   238→- **Wide screens:** Multiple columns (as many as fit)
   239→- **Medium screens:** 2-3 columns
   240→- **Narrow screens:** Gracefully reduces columns
   241→- **Mobile:** Overridden to single column
   242→
   243→No media queries needed for the grid itself!
   244→
   245→---
   246→
   247→## Integration with Content Collections
   248→
   249→### Projects
   250→- Automatically fetched via `getProjects()`
   251→- Sorted by date (descending)
   252→- All project data available (title, tagline, thumbnail, etc.)
   253→
   254→### Awards
   255→- Fetched via `getCollection("awards")`
   256→- Grouped by project ID using Map
   257→- Referenced projects validated at build time
   258→- Optional (projects without awards display fine)
   259→
   260→### Build-Time Safety
   261→- Missing thumbnails handled gracefully (no image shown)
   262→- Missing awards handled (no badges shown)
   263→- Invalid project references caught by build
   264→
   265→---
   266→
   267→## Testing
   268→
   269→**Access the page:**
   270→```bash
   271→pnpm dev
   272→# Visit: http://localhost:4322/projects
   273→```
   274→
   275→**What you'll see:**
   276→- Grid of project cards
   277→- Mishmash with 5 award badges
   278→- Planit App without badges (no awards yet)
   279→- Hover effects on all cards
   280→- Responsive layout
   281→
   282→---
   283→
   284→## Next Steps
   285→
   286→### To enhance further (optional):
   287→1. **Filters** — Add tag-based filtering
   288→2. **Search** — Add project search
   289→3. **Pagination** — If project count grows large
   290→4. **Sorting** — Allow sorting by date/title/awards
   291→5. **Featured section** — Highlight specific projects
   292→6. **View modes** — Grid vs. list toggle
   293→
   294→Current implementation covers the core requirements from Significa.co!
   295→
   296→---
   297→
   298→## Summary
   299→
   300→✅ **Grid layout** — Responsive with auto-fill  
   301→✅ **Thumbnails** — CDN-optimized images  
   302→✅ **Award badges** — Up to 5 per project  
   303→✅ **Hover effects** — Card lift, zoom, badge color  
   304→✅ **Responsive** — Mobile-first approach  
   305→✅ **Accessible** — Semantic HTML and ARIA  
   306→✅ **Performant** — Lazy loading, optimized images

   309→The projects page now matches the Significa.co design and provides an excellent showcase for the portfolio! 🎉

1→# Cover Images - URL Format Confirmed ✅ 2→ 3→## Overview 4→ 5→The cover image feature for handbook pages is **fully compatible with URL-based images**. You can paste any public image URL directly into the `coverImage` field and it will work perfectly. 6→ 7→## Supported URL Formats 8→ 9→### ✅ Significa CDN (Recommended) 10→```yaml 11→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg 12→``` 13→**Benefits:** 14→- Automatic image optimization 15→- Responsive srcset generation 16→- Quality and format conversion (WebP/AVIF) 17→- Width-based transforms 18→ 19→### ✅ External URLs (Unsplash, etc.) 20→```yaml 21→coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1234567890 22→``` 23→**Behavior:** 24→- Used as-is without modification 25→- No optimization applied 26→- No srcset generation 27→- Still displays perfectly 28→ 29→### ✅ Any Public Image URL 30→```yaml 31→coverImage: https://example.com/images/my-cover.jpg 32→``` 33→**Works with:** 34→- Image hosting services 35→- Asset managers 36→- Direct S3 links 37→- Any publicly accessible image 38→ 39→## How It Works 40→ 41→The `cdnUrl()` helper function in `src/lib/cdn.ts` intelligently handles all URL formats: 42→ 43→```typescript 44→// CDN URLs → Adds optimization parameters 45→if (src.startsWith("https://cdn.significa.co")) { 46→ return "https://cdn.significa.co/path?width=1200&quality=80"; 47→} 48→ 49→// External URLs → Used directly 50→if (isAbsoluteUrl(src)) { 51→ return src; // No modification 52→} 53→``` 54→ 55→## Usage Example 56→ 57→Simply paste your image URL into the frontmatter: 58→ 59→```yaml 60→--- 61→title: Code of Ethics 62→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles. 63→date: 2024-01-15 64→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg 65→group: knowing-significa 66→order: 1 67→--- 68→ 69→## Your content here... 70→``` 71→ 72→## Real-World Scenarios 73→ 74→### Scenario 1: Marketing Team Uses Asset Manager 75→```yaml 76→# Marketing uploads to Significa's asset manager 77→# Gets back: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/ethics.jpg 78→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/ethics.jpg 79→``` 80→✅ **Result:** Fully optimized with responsive images 81→ 82→### Scenario 2: Designer Uses Unsplash 83→```yaml 84→# Designer finds perfect image on Unsplash 85→# Copies URL: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz 86→coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz 87→``` 88→✅ **Result:** Displays perfectly, no optimization needed 89→ 90→### Scenario 3: External Asset Service 91→```yaml 92→# Company uses external DAM system 93→# URL: https://assets.company.com/handbook-covers/image.jpg 94→coverImage: https://assets.company.com/handbook-covers/image.jpg 95→``` 96→✅ **Result:** Works as expected 97→ 98→## Advantages of URL-Based Approach 99→ 100→1. **Flexible:** Works with any image hosting solution 101→2. **Simple:** Just copy-paste the URL 102→3. **No Migration:** Existing URLs continue working 103→4. **Team-Friendly:** Non-technical team members can add images easily 104→5. **Future-Proof:** Change hosting providers without code changes 105→ 106→## Performance Considerations 107→ 108→### CDN URLs (Optimal) 109→- ✅ Automatic optimization 110→- ✅ Responsive srcsets 111→- ✅ Format negotiation (WebP/AVIF) 112→- ✅ Edge caching 113→ 114→### External URLs (Good) 115→- ⚠️ No optimization applied 116→- ⚠️ No srcset generation 117→- ✅ Still fast if source is optimized 118→- ✅ Eager loading applied 119→ 120→**Recommendation:** Use Significa CDN URLs when possible for best performance. 121→ 122→## Documentation Updated 123→ 124→All documentation has been updated to reflect URL-based usage: 125→- ✅ `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` - Full URL examples 126→- ✅ `docs/QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` - Quick reference with URLs 127→- ✅ `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` - CDN integration details 128→- ✅ `COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md` - Complete technical reference 129→ 130→## Validation 131→ 132→**Build Status:** ✅ All tests passing 133→```bash 134→pnpm check # 0 errors, 0 warnings 135→pnpm build # 26 pages built successfully 136→``` 137→ 138→**Example Page:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx` 139→```yaml 140→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg 141→``` 142→ 143→## Workflow for Content Authors 144→ 145→1. **Get image URL** from your preferred source (asset manager, Unsplash, etc.) 146→2. **Copy the full URL** (must start with `https://`) 147→3. **Paste into frontmatter:** 148→ ```yaml 149→ coverImage: https://your-url-here.jpg 150→ ``` 151→4. **Save and commit** - that's it! 152→ 153→## Technical Details 154→ 155→### Schema 156→```typescript 157→coverImage: z.string().optional() 158→``` 159→- Accepts any string 160→- No URL validation (flexible) 161→- Optional field (can be omitted) 162→ 163→### Rendering 164→```astro 165→{page.data.coverImage && ( 166→ 173→)} 174→``` 175→ 176→### URL Processing 177→- **Full URLs:** Used directly or with optimization params 178→- **No validation:** Won't break build if URL is invalid 179→- **Graceful handling:** Missing images won't crash the page 180→ 181→## Summary 182→ 183→✅ **URL format is fully supported and recommended** 184→✅ **Works with any public image URL** 185→✅ **CDN URLs get automatic optimization** 186→✅ **External URLs work as-is** 187→✅ **Simple copy-paste workflow for content authors** 188→✅ **Production-ready and tested** 189→ 190→Your team can now add cover images by simply pasting image URLs into the handbook pages. No complex file management required!

1→# Color Palettes OKLCH: Design Document
     2→
     3→## Goal
     4→
     5→Generate 9 OKLCH color palettes (15 shades each) + 1 grayscale palette from Figma reference colors. Apply to the homepage layout from Figma design node `4990:97134`.
     6→
     7→## Source Colors
     8→
     9→### 9 Chromatic Palettes
    10→
    11→Each has two Figma anchors: light (shade 50) and dark (shade 950).
    12→
    13→| Palette  | Light (shade 50) | Dark (shade 950) |
    14→|----------|-----------------|-----------------|
    15→| Beige    | `#d1cec7`       | `#2b261d`       |
    16→| Green    | `#d0cfb9`       | `#2b2a1d`       |
    17→| Marine   | `#c4cac9`       | `#202726`       |
    18→| Orange   | `#dec5a6`       | `#251f18`       |
    19→| Purple   | `#c3b8cf`       | `#231d2b`       |
    20→| Yellow   | `#ddcca3`       | `#252118`       |
    21→| Sea      | `#b6c8d2`       | `#1d262b`       |
    22→| Red      | `#e0bcb2`       | `#251b18`       |
    23→| Pink     | `#dab9cb`       | `#25181f`       |
    24→
    25→### Grayscale Palette
    26→
    27→Base color: `#1e1a15` (warm near-black) with opacity steps over white.
    28→
    29→| Shade | Opacity |
    30→|-------|---------|
    31→| 950   | 100%    |
    32→| 925   | 96%     |
    33→| 900   | 88%     |
    34→| 850   | 80%     |
    35→| 800   | 72%     |
    36→| 700   | 64%     |
    37→| 600   | 56%     |
    38→| 500   | 48%     |
    39→| 400   | 40%     |
    40→| 300   | 32%     |
    41→| 200   | 24%     |
    42→| 150   | 16%     |
    43→| 100   | 8%      |
    44→| 50    | 4%      |
    45→| 25    | 2%      |
    46→
    47→Converted to solid OKLCH values (composited over white) for use as standalone tokens.
    48→
    49→## Shade Naming (Tailwind-style)
    50→
    51→15 shades per palette:
    52→
    53→```
    54→25, 50, 100, 150, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 850, 900, 925, 950
    55→```
    56→
    57→- **25** = new extra-light shade (lighter than Figma light, near-white with tint)
    58→- **50** = Figma light anchor
    59→- **100-925** = interpolated shades
    60→- **950** = Figma dark anchor (extreme)
    61→
    62→## OKLCH Generation Strategy
    63→
    64→**Lightness (L):**
    65→- shade 25: L of shade 50 + 3-5% (near white)
    66→- shade 50 to 950: smooth curve, denser in mid-range for UI utility
    67→- Not linear: slight easing for perceptual evenness
    68→
    69→**Chroma (C):**
    70→- Bell curve: low at extremes, peak around 400-600
    71→- shade 25: very low C (near-neutral tint)
    72→- shade 950: low C (rich dark without muddiness)
    73→
    74→**Hue (H):**
    75→- Constant within each palette, max drift of 3 degrees
    76→- Derived from the midpoint between both Figma anchors
    77→
    78→## Fonts
    79→
    80→- **IBM Plex Sans** (variable): body text. Loaded from Google Fonts.
    81→- **N27** (local OTF): headings/display. Weights: Thin, ExtraLight, Light, Regular, Medium, Bold. Located at `/Users/andrefurt/Library/Fonts/N27-*.otf`.
    82→
    83→## Homepage Layout
    84→
    85→Based on Figma node `4990:97134` (1512x6156px). Sections:
    86→
    87→1. **Hero**: title + highlight rows
    88→2. **Showreel**: large media area (replaced by palette color block)
    89→3. **Intro**: centered text block
    90→4. **Prizes**: 2x2 grid of project cards (bg: palette light, fg: palette dark)
    91→5. **Services**: two-column layout with service lists
    92→6. **Highlights**: large + small card layout
    93→7. **Blog**: 4 post cards in a row
    94→
    95→Images replaced by palette color backgrounds. Cards use `--{palette}-50` as bg, `--{palette}-950` as fg.
    96→
    97→## Files to Modify
    98→
    99→- `src/styles/tokens.css`: add 10 palettes (9 chromatic + grayscale) as CSS custom properties
   100→- `src/styles/global.css`: add `@font-face` for N27, import IBM Plex Sans variable
   101→- `src/pages/index.astro`: adapt homepage to match Figma layout with palette colors
   102→
   103→## Out of Scope
   104→
   105→- Responsive design (focus on 1512px desktop as in Figma)
   106→- Palette switcher (user changes colors manually in code)
   107→- Dark mode
   108→

1→# Color Palettes OKLCH Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.
     4→
     5→**Goal:** Generate 10 OKLCH color palettes (9 chromatic + 1 grayscale, 15 shades each), integrate them as tokens following the project's design system, and adapt the homepage to the Figma layout using reusable Astro components.
     6→
     7→**Architecture:** Palette tokens in `tokens.css` as CSS custom properties. Semantic color aliases reference palette shades. Homepage sections extracted as composable Astro components with typed props and scoped styles. No CSS framework. Fonts via `@font-face` (N27 local) and Google Fonts (IBM Plex Sans variable).
     8→
     9→**Tech Stack:** Astro v5, CSS custom properties (OKLCH), TypeScript strict, Node.js (generation script only)
    10→
    11→**Branch:** `design` in `code/significa.co`
    12→
    13→---
    14→
    15→## Project Conventions (must follow)
    16→
    17→- **OKLCH only.** No hex, no rgb. All colors as CSS custom properties.
    18→- **Semantic HTML.** `<section>`, `<article>`, `<nav>`, `<dl>`, `<figure>`. Not everything is a `<div>`.
    19→- **Scoped styles.** `<style>` in each Astro component. No global classes for section styling.
    20→- **kebab-case filenames.** Enforced by ESLint.
    21→- **TypeScript strict.** `interface Props` for every component. No `any`.
    22→- **Tokens for everything.** Colors, typography, transitions via `var()`. Raw values for spacing (4px grid, rem vertical, px horizontal).
    23→- **Breakpoints:** sm 640px, md 768px, lg 1024px, xl 1280px.
    24→- **No dependencies without approval.**
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Source Data
    29→
    30→### 9 Chromatic Palettes (hex anchors from Figma)
    31→
    32→| Name   | Light (shade 50) | Dark (shade 950) |
    33→|--------|-----------------|-----------------|
    34→| beige  | #d1cec7         | #2b261d         |
    35→| green  | #d0cfb9         | #2b2a1d         |
    36→| marine | #c4cac9         | #202726         |
    37→| orange | #dec5a6         | #251f18         |
    38→| purple | #c3b8cf         | #231d2b         |
    39→| yellow | #ddcca3         | #252118         |
    40→| sea    | #b6c8d2         | #1d262b         |
    41→| red    | #e0bcb2         | #251b18         |
    42→| pink   | #dab9cb         | #25181f         |
    43→
    44→### Grayscale Palette
    45→
    46→Base: `#1e1a15` (warm near-black) with opacity steps over white, converted to solid OKLCH.
    47→
    48→Opacities: 100%, 96%, 88%, 80%, 72%, 64%, 56%, 48%, 40%, 32%, 24%, 16%, 8%, 4%, 2%
    49→
    50→### Shade Naming (15 per palette, Tailwind-style)
    51→
    52→```
    53→25, 50, 100, 150, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 850, 900, 925, 950
    54→```
    55→
    56→- **25** = new extra-light (lighter than Figma light, near-white with tint)
    57→- **50** = Figma light anchor
    58→- **100-925** = interpolated shades
    59→- **950** = Figma dark anchor (darkest extreme)
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Task 1: Create OKLCH generation script
    64→
    65→**Files:**
    66→- Create: `scripts/generate-palettes.mjs`
    67→
    68→**What it does:**
    69→- `hexToOklch(hex)`: sRGB -> linear RGB -> XYZ D65 -> Oklab -> OKLCH
    70→- `generateChromaticPalette(name, lightHex, darkHex)`: 15 shades per palette
    71→  - shade 50 = Figma light OKLCH (anchor, exact match)
    72→  - shade 950 = Figma dark OKLCH (anchor, exact match)
    73→  - shade 25 = L of shade 50 + ~4%, C * 0.4, same H (near-white tint)
    74→  - shades 100-925: L interpolated linearly between shade 50 and 950 (OKLCH is perceptually uniform, linear works)
    75→  - C follows bell curve: `C_peak * exp(-((t - 0.35)^2) / (2 * 0.3^2))` where C_peak = max(C_light, C_dark) * 1.3
    76→  - H constant (average of both anchors)
    77→- `generateGrayscale(baseHex)`: composite base at each opacity over white, convert to OKLCH
    78→- Outputs CSS custom properties ready for tokens.css
    79→
    80→**Run:** `node scripts/generate-palettes.mjs`
    81→
    82→**Verify:** L values decrease monotonically 25->950. C forms bell curve. H consistent per palette. Grayscale C near 0.
    83→
    84→**Commit:** `git commit -m "add OKLCH palette generation script"`
    85→
    86→---
    87→
    88→## Task 2: Add palette tokens and update semantic colors

   168→---
   169→
   170→## Task 4: Create reusable section components
   171→
   172→**Files to create** (all in `src/components/`):
   173→
   174→### 4a: `src/components/section-wrapper.astro`
   175→
   176→Generic section wrapper following the existing pattern, but as a reusable component:

## Complete Documentation Audit

**docs/ directory (23 files total):**

| `07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md` | 500 | Half redundant, half historical |
| `08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` | 46 | Thin stub, mostly redundant |
| `09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` | 37 | Lean, useful |
| `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` | 367 | 70% overlap with PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md and QUICK guide |
| `QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` | 135 | Redundant with 10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md |
| `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` | 475 | Content guide for authors, very verbose |
| `11-BLOG-INDEX.md` | 620 | 80% redundant documentation of existing code |
| `BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md` | 356 | Changelog-style, no ongoing value |
| `COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md` | 243 | Redundant with 10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md + 04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md |
| `HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md` | 256 | Changelog/migration notes, no ongoing value |
| `MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md` | 330 | Migration log, no ongoing value |
| `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` | 265 | Implementation summary, mostly redundant |
| `PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md` | 310 | Implementation log, no ongoing value |
| `URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md` | 189 | Fully redundant with 04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md |
| `plans/2026-03-02-color-palettes-design.md` | 108 | Active plan/spec, relevant |
| `plans/2026-03-02-color-palettes.md` | 343 | Active implementation plan, relevant |

**memory/ directory (6 files):**

| File | Lines (approx) | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `MEMORY.md` | 30 | Index only, useful but has stale CDN hostname |
| `architecture.md` | 50 | Dense, high signal, current |
| `design-tokens.md` | 77 | Redundant with 08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md but more complete |
| `content-system.md` | 75 | Redundant with 02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md but denser |
| `components.md` | 54 | Redundant with 03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md but denser |
| `routing.md` | 48 | Unique, no equivalent in docs/ |

---

### Critical Stale/Incorrect Data

1. **MEMORY.md line 4:** Says `Media: S3 + Bunny CDN (significa.b-cdn.net)` but the actual CDN is `cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare -> Fly.io -> S3). Every doc in `docs/` says `cdn.significa.co`. This is wrong and will mislead agents.

2. **content-system.md line 66:** `CDN_HOSTNAME: https://significa.b-cdn.net` — same stale Bunny CDN reference.

3. **MEMORY.md:** Says "8 collections" but `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` and `architecture.md` both show 10 collections (adding `handbook-groups` and `handbook`).

4. **MEMORY.md:** `No test framework` — accurate. `No dark mode` — accurate.

5. **07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md line 32:** Says "Astro v6 (latest stable)" as a decision, but the actual codebase and all other docs consistently say v5. This is a known inconsistency (the decision was made before v6 reached stable, so the team stayed on v5). Confusing to encounter mid-document.

---

### Overlap and Redundancy Analysis

**Extreme redundancy (4 files covering handbook cover images):**
- `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` section "Handbook Cover Images"
- `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` section "Cover Images" (exhaustive, ~80 lines)
- `docs/QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` section "Cover Images (Optional)"
- `docs/COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md` (entire file, 243 lines)
- `docs/URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md` (entire file, 189 lines)

All five contain the same information: `coverImage` is optional, accepts full URLs, CDN URLs get optimization, external URLs used as-is, 1200x630 recommended. This could be two paragraphs in `04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`.

**Extreme redundancy (parent-child pages):**
- `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` — full reference, 367 lines
- `docs/QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` — summary, 135 lines
- `docs/PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` — implementation log of the same feature, 265 lines

These three files cover identical concepts: directory-based hierarchy, same helper functions, same gotchas, same frontmatter schema. The QUICK guide explicitly defers to the full guide. The SETUP file is just a past-tense duplicate.

**docs/ vs memory/ overlap:**
- `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` (545 lines) and `memory/content-system.md` (75 lines) cover the same ground. The memory file is the compressed version.
- `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` (142 lines) and `memory/components.md` (54 lines) — same.
- `08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` (46 lines) is actually *less complete* than `memory/design-tokens.md` (77 lines), which includes actual token values. A doc that defers to a memory file is backwards.

**06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md is near-entirely redundant:**
This 559-line file teaches Astro fundamentals (what a frontmatter block is, what `client:load` does, how `getStaticPaths` works). An AI agent already knows Astro. The project-specific gotchas in the "Common Gotchas" section are already duplicated in `05-RULES.md`. The only unique content is the integration checklist and the View Transitions rename note (`ViewTransitions` → `ClientRouter`). The rest is Astro docs rewrite.

**07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md is 60% historical noise:**
The discussion simulation (4 fictional agents debating), the Devil's Advocate Report Card, competitor comparison table, the content workflow debate — none of this is useful reference material. The actual decisions are summarized in the appendix table at the bottom (22 lines). The valuable unique content is the Hybrid page architecture section and the decisions summary table.

**Implementation/changelog files with no ongoing value:**
- `BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md` — describes what changed during a past sprint. The current state is already in `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` and the actual code.
- `HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md` — describes what changed during restructure. The result is the current `index.astro`.
- `MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md` — migration log. The migrated content is already in the codebase.
- `PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md` — implementation log. The result is the current `projects/index.astro`.

These four files collectively consume ~1,250 lines describing the history of changes rather than the current state.

---

### Token Waste by Category

**Worst offenders (approximate line savings if removed/merged):**

| Action | Lines saved |
|---|---|
| Delete 4 changelog files (BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS, HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE, MISHMASH-MIGRATION, PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN) | ~1,250 |
| Delete COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md and URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md | ~430 |
| Delete PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md and QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md (merge essentials into 10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md) | ~350 |
| Strip 06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md to ~50 lines (project-specific gotchas only) | ~510 |
| Strip 07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md to the appendix decisions table + hybrid architecture section | ~400 |
| Strip 11-BLOG-INDEX.md to ~50 lines (current structure only, drop implementation details) | ~570 |
| **Total** | **~3,510 lines** |

That's roughly 70% of the total docs/ word count. What remains would be: a lean, accurate reference corpus.

---

### Essential vs Nice-to-Have by File

**Essential (keep, possibly trim):**
- `00-README.md` — good index
- `01-PROJECT-BOOTSTRAP.md` — stack, commands, key principles
- `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` — authoritative schema reference, keep in full
- `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` — component catalog, keep
- `04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` — CDN pipeline, keep (remove handbook cover images section, already duplicated)
- `05-RULES.md` — rules, keep in full
- `08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` — should be expanded with actual token values (currently worse than the memory file)
- `09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` — checklists, keep
- `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` — keep as sole reference, trim cover image repetition
- `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` — genuinely useful for content authors, but 475 lines is too long; the writing style guide sections could be cut to 20 lines
- `plans/` — keep both, they are active specs

**Essential (memory/):**
- `memory/architecture.md` — densest, most signal-to-noise of all files
- `memory/routing.md` — no equivalent elsewhere
- `memory/MEMORY.md` — fix the stale CDN hostname

**Nice-to-have (trim heavily or remove):**
- `06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md` — reduce to project-specific gotchas only (~50 lines)
- `07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md` — reduce to appendix table + hybrid architecture section (~60 lines)
- `11-BLOG-INDEX.md` — reduce to current structure description only (~50 lines)

**Delete entirely:**
- `BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md`
- `HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md`
- `MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md`
- `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md`
- `PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md`
- `COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md`
- `URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md`
- `QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md`

---

### Structural Issues

**The CLAUDE.md table of contents is outdated.** It lists docs 01-09. Files 10, 11, and all the UPPERCASE files don't appear in the table. An agent following CLAUDE.md's "read the docs relevant to your task" instruction would miss the handbook parent-child docs entirely.

**The memory/ files and docs/ files serve different audiences but aren't coordinated.** The memory files are denser and often more accurate (e.g., the components list is more up-to-date in `components.md` than in `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`). There's no guidance on which source to trust when they differ. The stale CDN hostname in MEMORY.md is a consequence of this drift.

**Files with `10-` prefix conflict.** There are two files starting with `10-`: `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` and `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md`. Neither appears in the CLAUDE.md table that stops at `09`.

- `BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md`
- `HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md`
- `MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md`
- `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md`
- `PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md`
- `COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md`
- `URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md`
- `QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md`

---

### Structural Issues

**The CLAUDE.md table of contents is outdated.** It lists docs 01-09. Files 10, 11, and all the UPPERCASE files don't appear in the table. An agent following CLAUDE.md's "read the docs relevant to your task" instruction would miss the handbook parent-child docs entirely.

**The memory/ files and docs/ files serve different audiences but aren't coordinated.** The memory files are denser and often more accurate (e.g., the components list is more up-to-date in `components.md` than in `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`). There's no guidance on which source to trust when they differ. The stale CDN hostname in MEMORY.md is a consequence of this drift.

**Files with `10-` prefix conflict.** There are two files starting with `10-`: `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` and `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md`. Neither appears in the CLAUDE.md table that stops at `09`.

content.text:
Análise completa. Aqui está o diagnóstico e o plano.

---

## Diagnóstico

**Total atual:** ~5,500 linhas em 23 ficheiros docs/ + 6 ficheiros memory/ + 2 CLAUDE.md
**Desperdício estimado:** ~3,500 linhas (64%) são redundância, changelogs históricos, ou reescrita dos docs do Astro.

### Problemas principais

1. **8 ficheiros são lixo histórico** (changelogs de migrações passadas, duplicados de features já implementadas). ~1,700 linhas sem valor para agentes.

2. **Redundância extrema no tema "parent-child pages":** 4 ficheiros cobrem o mesmo. Cover images aparece em 5 sítios diferentes.

3. **`06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md`** (559 linhas) ensina o que é frontmatter e `client:load` a um LLM que já sabe Astro. Só ~50 linhas são project-specific.

4. **`07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md`** (500 linhas) simula uma discussão entre 4 agentes fictícios. As decisões reais cabem em 22 linhas.

5. **memory/ vs docs/ não coordenados.** Memory files são mais densos e por vezes mais correctos, mas o CDN hostname está errado em 2 sítios (`significa.b-cdn.net` devia ser `cdn.significa.co`).

6. **CLAUDE.md table of contents pára no doc 09.** Os docs 10, 11 e todos os ficheiros UPPERCASE não aparecem.

---

## Plano de Síntese

### Fase 1: Limpar (eliminar ruído)

**Apagar 8 ficheiros** (~1,700 linhas):
- `BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md` - changelog passado
- `HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md` - changelog passado
- `MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md` - log de migração
- `PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md` - log de implementação
- `COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md` - duplicado de `04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`
- `URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md` - duplicado de `04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`
- `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` - duplicado de `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`
- `QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` - duplicado de `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`

### Fase 2: Comprimir (reduzir verbosidade)

| Ficheiro | Agora | Target | Acção |
|---|---|---|---|
| `06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md` | 559 | ~60 | Manter só gotchas project-specific + ViewTransitions rename |
| `07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md` | 500 | ~60 | Manter tabela de decisões + hybrid architecture |
| `11-BLOG-INDEX.md` | 620 | ~60 | Manter só estrutura actual, drop implementation narrative |
| `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` | 475 | ~150 | Comprimir writing guide, manter frontmatter reference |
| `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` | 367 | ~120 | Absorver essenciais do QUICK guide, remover repetição de cover images |

**Saving estimado:** ~2,100 linhas adicionais.

### Fase 3: Unificar memory/ e docs/

**Decisão de arquitectura:** memory/ é para o LLM (denso, sem narrativa). docs/ é referência completa (para humanos e LLMs que precisam de profundidade). Mas não podem contradizer-se.

Acções:
- Corrigir CDN hostname em `MEMORY.md` e `content-system.md` para `cdn.significa.co`
- Corrigir contagem de collections em `MEMORY.md` (8 → 10)
- Fazer `08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` absorver o conteúdo de `memory/design-tokens.md` (que é mais completo)
- Adicionar nota no `00-README.md` sobre o papel de cada camada

### Fase 4: Actualizar CLAUDE.md

- Actualizar tabela de docs para incluir `10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` e `10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md`
- Remover referências a ficheiros apagados
- Renumerar para eliminar conflito de dois `10-`

### Fase 5: Regras de routing por modelo

Adicionar ao CLAUDE.md uma secção que orienta o agente a escolher modelo por task:

```
## Model Routing

Tasks that can use Haiku (fast, cheap):
- File lookups, glob/grep searches
- Simple content edits (typos, frontmatter updates)
- Git operations (status, diff, log)
- Formatting and linting
- Reading/summarizing single files

Tasks that should use Sonnet:
- Multi-file edits with clear instructions
- Component implementation from existing patterns
- Content schema changes
- Bug fixes with known root cause
- Code review of small PRs

Tasks that require Opus:
- Architectural decisions
- New feature design and implementation
- Complex debugging (unknown root cause)
- Documentation synthesis and planning
- Refactoring across 3+ files
```

---

## Resultado esperado

| Métrica | Antes | Depois |
|---|---|---|
| Ficheiros em docs/ | 23 | 15 |
| Linhas totais (docs/) | ~5,500 | ~1,900 |
| Redundância | ~64% | <5% |
| Dados stale | 4 instâncias | 0 |

**Redução de ~65% no consumo de tokens** para qualquer agente que carregue os docs.

---

Queres que avance com este plano? Posso executar fase a fase com review entre cada uma, ou todas de uma vez se preferires.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/andrefurt/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

## Overview

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
3. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
4. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
5. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
6. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill";
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**
- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**
- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**
- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

## After the Design

**Documentation:**
- Write the validated design to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Implementation:**
- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

   303→---
   304→
   305→## Best Practices Summary
   306→
   307→✅ **Do:**
   308→- Write compelling opening hooks
   309→- Use concrete examples and stories
   310→- Include images and visual breaks
   311→- Show your work (process, diagrams)
   312→- End with actionable takeaways
   313→- Link to related work
   314→- Use MDX components strategically
   315→- Write in active voice
   316→- Keep paragraphs short (2-4 sentences)
   317→
   318→❌ **Don't:**
   319→- Start with abstract generalities
   320→- Create walls of text
   321→- Skip image alt text
   322→- Forget SEO metadata
   323→- Use corporate jargon
   324→- Hardcode image paths (use CDN)
   325→- Overcomplicate heading structure
   326→- Write in passive voice
   327→
   328→---
   329→
   330→## Next Steps
   331→
   332→1. **Review existing blog posts** - Update to match new structure
   333→2. **Add author bios** - Create author content collection if needed
   334→3. **Populate related projects** - Ensure cross-linking between content
   335→4. **Create content calendar** - Plan upcoming posts
   336→5. **Add analytics** - Track reading time, scroll depth, engagement
   337→6. **Consider additions**:
   338→   - Author pages with bio and all their posts
   339→   - Tag archive pages
   340→   - Search functionality
   341→   - Related posts algorithm (beyond manual linking)
   342→   - Newsletter signup integration
   343→
   344→---
   345→
   346→## Resources
   347→
   348→- **Live example**: `/blog/designing-complex-products`
   349→- **Documentation**: `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md`
   350→- **MDX components**: `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`
   351→- **Design tokens**: `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`
   352→- **SEO guidelines**: `docs/09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md`
   353→
   354→---
   355→
   356→This blog structure now matches professional standards with real content, comprehensive styling, and clear guidelines for the team to create consistent, high-quality posts going forward.

1→# Homepage Restructure Documentation
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→The homepage has been completely restructured to align with the new content strategy outlined in Notion. The new structure provides a clearer narrative flow, better showcases services, and creates more engagement opportunities.
     6→
     7→## Key Changes
     8→
     9→### 1. New Hero Section
    10→- **Before:** Long tagline with muted text and inline CTA
    11→- **After:** Simple, bold statement: "We are a **design and development agency** building digital products that work beautifully."
    12→- More concise and impactful first impression
    13→
    14→### 2. Content Flow Reorganization
    15→
    16→The new homepage follows this narrative structure:
    17→
    18→1. **Hero** - Bold positioning statement
    19→2. **Showreel** - Visual showcase (maintained from original)
    20→3. **Strategy Statement** - "From strategy to code..." positioning
    21→4. **Selected Highlights** - Dynamic content from highlights collection (replaces hardcoded featured projects)
    22→5. **Service Pillars Intro** - Overview of Think/Design/Develop/Launch/Scale approach
    23→6. **Service Pillars Detail** - Expandable descriptions of each pillar
    24→7. **Team CTA** - "We are a team of designers, developers..."
    25→8. **Blog Section** - "We think things through" with 3 recent posts
    26→9. **Impact Section** - B Corp and sustainability messaging
    27→10. **Playground Section** - Open source contributions
    28→11. **Social Proof** - Customer testimonials and satisfaction stats
    29→12. **Awards** - International recognition
    30→13. **Final CTA** - "We are ready to grow your business"
    31→14. **FAQs** - Common questions answered
    32→15. **Client Logos** - Trust indicators
    33→
    34→### 3. Dynamic Content via Highlights Collection
    35→
    36→The homepage now uses the `highlights` collection instead of hardcoded featured projects:
    37→
    38→```astro
    39→const highlights = await getResolvedHighlights();
    40→const featuredHighlights = highlights.slice(0, 3);
    41→```
    42→
    43→This allows the marketing team to:
    44→- Control which content appears on the homepage via YAML files

    48→
    49→### 4. Service Pillars

    51→New comprehensive service section with 5 pillars:
    52→- **Think** - Strategy and planning
    53→- **Design** - UI/UX and design systems
    54→- **Develop** - Engineering and code
    55→- **Launch** - Deployment and handover
    56→- **Scale** - Growth and optimization
    57→
    58→Each pillar has a title and description, presented in a responsive grid.
    59→
    60→### 5. New Sections Added
    61→
    62→#### Impact Section

    65→- Ethical and sustainable approach
    66→- Links to `/impact` page

    68→#### Playground Section

    78→#### Final CTA

    81→
    82→### 6. Improved Blog Section
    83→
    84→- Now labeled "We think things through"
    85→- Shows 3 most recent posts (reduced from 4)
    86→- Better card layout with thumbnails
    87→- Includes post descriptions
    88→- Date formatting improved
    89→
    90→### 7. Awards Redesign
    91→
    92→- Changed heading from "Ah, awards..." to "International recognition"
    93→- Card-based layout instead of list
    94→- Limited to 18 most recent awards
    95→- Better visual hierarchy
    96→- Shows award name, year, and project
    97→
    98→### 8. Removed Sections
    99→
   100→- **Industries list** - Removed hardcoded list
   101→- **Capabilities list** - Removed hardcoded list
   102→- **Team photo** - Removed from homepage (can be added to About page)
   103→- **Recent articles highlights** - Replaced with dedicated blog section
   104→
   105→### 9. CSS Improvements
   106→
   107→All styles are scoped and follow design token conventions:
   108→
   109→- Consistent spacing using `var(--gutter)`
   110→- Responsive grid layouts with `auto-fit` and `minmax`
   111→- Proper semantic HTML (`<section>`, `<article>`, `<h2>`, etc.)
   112→- Hover states and transitions
   113→- Mobile-first responsive design
   114→- Accessible focus states
   115→
   116→### 10. CTA Components
   117→
   118→Three standardized CTA button styles:
   119→
   120→- `.cta-link` - Inline text link with arrow
   121→- `.cta-button` - Primary filled button
   122→- `.cta-button-outline` - Secondary outlined button
   123→- `.cta-button-large` - Hero-sized button
   124→
   125→All with consistent hover effects (opacity + translateY).
   126→
   127→## Technical Implementation
   128→
   129→### Data Fetching
   130→
   131→```astro
   132→const clients = await getClients();
   133→const testimonials = await getTestimonials();

   140→### Type Safety

   143→
   144→```typescript
   145→// Get thumbnail based on collection type
   146→let thumbnail: string | undefined;
   147→if (highlight.collection === "projects") {
   148→  thumbnail = (entry.data as any).thumbnail || (entry.data as any).heroImage;
   149→} else {
   150→  thumbnail = (entry.data as any).thumbnail;
   151→}
   152→```
   153→
   154→Uses type assertions to access properties that exist on different schemas.

1→# Projects Page Redesign - Summary
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Redesigned the `/projects` page to match the current Significa.co design, featuring project thumbnails and award badges in a grid layout.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## What Changed
    10→
    11→### Before
    12→- Simple list layout
    13→- Text-only (titles and taglines)
    14→- No visual hierarchy
    15→- No awards displayed
    16→
    17→### After
    18→- Modern grid layout with thumbnails
    19→- Award badges for each project
    20→- Hover animations and interactions
    21→- Responsive design
    22→- Visual hierarchy matching Significa brand
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## File Updated
    27→
    28→**Location:** `src/pages/projects/index.astro`
    29→
    30→**Key Features:**
    31→1. **Grid Layout** — Responsive grid that adapts to screen size
    32→2. **Project Thumbnails** — CDN-optimized images with hover effects
    33→3. **Award Badges** — Display up to 5 awards per project
    34→4. **Hover Interactions** — Card lift, image zoom, badge color change
    35→5. **"View project →" CTA** — Clear call-to-action on each card
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Technical Implementation
    40→
    41→### Data Fetching
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→// Get all projects (sorted by date)
    45→const projects = await getProjects();
    46→
    47→// Get all awards
    48→const allAwards = await getCollection("awards");
    49→
    50→// Group awards by project ID
    51→const awardsByProject = new Map<string, Array<Award>>();
    52→```
    53→
    54→### Award Integration
    55→
    56→Awards are automatically fetched and grouped by project using the `reference()` system:
    57→- Awards reference projects via `project: reference("projects")`
    58→- Build-time validation ensures all references are valid
    59→- Each project card shows up to 5 awards
    60→
    61→### CDN Optimization
    62→
    63→Thumbnails are optimized via the CDN:
    64→```typescript
