1→# Significa Website: Team Discussion & Exploration 2→ 3→> Senior team review of the Significa website rebuild plan. 4→> 4 agents, 4 perspectives, 1 synthesis. 5→> Date: 2026-02-09 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Participants 10→ 11→| Role | Focus | Experience | 12→|---|---|---| 13→| **Technical Architect** | Architecture, build, deploy, performance | 15+ years | 14→| **UX/UI Designer** | Storytelling, components, IA, interactions | 12+ years | 15→| **Marketing/Content Director** | Lead gen, SEO, workflow, content strategy | 15+ years | 16→| **Devil's Advocate** | Stress-testing every decision | 15+ years | 17→ 18→--- 19→ 20→## Consensus: The Plan Is Solid 21→ 22→All 4 participants agree: the technical foundation is well-designed. Astro, static output, Content Collections with Zod, MDX. No one recommended changing the framework or fundamental approach. 23→ 24→Verdict: **ship it, but fix these gaps first.** 25→ 26→--- 27→ 28→## Team Decisions (Post-Discussion) 29→ 30→After reviewing all 4 perspectives, the following decisions were made: 31→ 32→1. **Astro v6 (latest stable).** We go with the latest stable version to avoid near-future breaking changes. No pinning to v5. 33→2. **Media in `public/` for now.** External CDN/image service (ImageKit, Cloudinary, or similar with S3) will be evaluated later. Not blocking for development. 34→3. **No CMS. Marketing uses git + AI.** The marketing team will have the project set up locally. MDX is learnable. AI assistance fills any gaps. Good documentation and content templates are key. 35→4. **Components: start lean, grow with content.** The 5 starting components are sufficient. New components are added when content demands them, not eagerly pre-built. 36→5. **Conversion architecture evolves with data.** CTAs and conversion points will be designed and tested against real metrics. Marketing owns this. 37→6. **SEO infra from inception.** RSS, sitemap, canonical URLs, robots.txt, 404 page, and SEO component should be set up from the start. 38→7. **CSS token system: no magic values.** Tokens evolve with the design but the rule is absolute: no hardcoded pixels, no hex colors, no arbitrary spacing. Agents must follow this strictly. 39→8. **Slug collision check: included.** Maximize compile-time validations. `reference()` over plain slugs, always. Non-technical content editors need the build to catch their mistakes. 40→9. **`reference()` is non-negotiable.** The Astro lock-in is worth the build-time validation. Runtime errors are unacceptable for a site managed by non-developers. 41→ 42→--- 43→ 44→## 5 Critical Gaps (Unanimous or Near-Unanimous) 45→ 46→### 1. Media Pipeline Is Underspecified 47→ 48→**Raised by:** All 4 participants. 49→ 50→The plan says "CDN handles it" but doesn't define the actual pipeline. Cloudflare R2 is object storage, not an image CDN. 51→ 52→**What's missing:** 53→- URL patterns for responsive images (`srcset`, format=webp/avif) 54→- Upload workflow (Figma to CDN to MDX) 55→- `width` and `height` as required props on `MediaImage` (prevents CLS) 56→- `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"` for hero images (LCP) 57→- Cloudflare Image Resizing or Workers for transformation 58→- Local development experience with CDN images 59→ 60→**Tech Architect's solution:** 61→ 62→```astro 63→--- 64→// MediaImage with responsive images via Cloudflare Image Resizing 65→interface Props { 66→ src: string; 67→ alt: string; 68→ width: number; 69→ height: number; 70→ eager?: boolean; 71→ sizes?: string; 72→} 73→ 74→const { src, alt, width, height, eager = false, sizes = '100vw' } = Astro.props; 75→const cdnBase = 'https://media.significa.co'; 76→const transforms = (w: number) => 77→ `${cdnBase}/cdn-cgi/image/width=${w},format=auto,quality=80/${src}`; 78→--- 79→ 80→ 81→ 85→ 94→ 95→``` 96→ 97→**Recommendation:** Define the image pipeline before writing any content. Build a CLI tool or script that uploads to R2 and generates the MDX snippet. 98→ 99→**Team Decision (Updated):** Media is managed via the internal asset manager app (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads to S3. A self-hosted image transform service on Fly.io serves media through `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare edge cache → Fly.io → S3) with sharp-based image transforms (resize, quality, format conversion via URL query parameters). See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` for the full technical reference. 100→ 101→--- 102→ 103→### 2. Content Workflow Without CMS: Realistic But Limited 104→ 105→**Raised by:** Marketing, Devil's Advocate, Tech Architect. 106→ 107→Git + MDX works if 1-2 technical people control the content. The breaking point comes when non-technical team members need to publish. 108→ 109→**Devil's Advocate scenario:** 110→> Tuesday afternoon. A marketing manager wants to fix a typo. They need to: open the repo, find the .mdx file, edit without breaking YAML, commit to a branch, open a PR, wait for build, get it merged, wait for deployment. In a CMS: click, edit, save. 30 seconds. 111→ 112→**When it works:** 113→- 1-2 people write content AND are comfortable with git 114→- Publishing cadence is low (2-4 posts/month) 115→- No external contributors 116→ 117→**When it breaks:** 118→- Non-technical team members need to contribute 119→- Publishing frequency increases 120→- Blog posts sit in PRs because the dev who merges is busy 121→ 122→**Recommendation:** Don't add CMS now, but: 123→1. Create MDX skeleton templates for each content type (copy-paste-edit) 124→2. Document "create new blog post" in 5 steps 125→3. Assign one content owner (not "the team") 126→4. Document Decap CMS as the Phase 2 escape hatch (git-backed, zero lock-in) 127→ 128→**Marketing Director's phased approach:** 129→1. **Phase 1 (now):** File-based MDX. Ship the site. 130→2. **Phase 2 (6-12 months):** Decap CMS or Tina CMS (git-backed visual editor) 131→3. **Phase 3 (if needed):** Headless CMS (Sanity, Contentful, Payload) 132→ 133→**Team Decision:** No CMS. The marketing team will have the project set up on their computers with AI assistance. Changing markdown files is not rocket science. Even with some learning curve, the team should be sufficiently familiar within a week or two. AI fills any remaining gaps, given correct skills and documentation in-project. 134→ 135→--- 136→ 137→### 3. MDX Components Insufficient for Case Studies 138→ 139→**Raised by:** UX/UI (strongly), Marketing, Devil's Advocate. 140→ 141→5 components produce linear, monotonous case studies. The UX/UI Designer was unequivocal: the gap between "portfolio site" and "design studio experience" is in the component toolkit. 142→ 143→**Current 5 components:** 144→ 145→| Component | Verdict | 146→|---|---| 147→| MediaImage | Essential. Needs `size` variant and `eager` prop. | 148→| MediaVideo | Essential. Needs autoplay-on-scroll for showreels. | 149→| ComparisonBlock | Good. Limited use but high impact. | 150→| Metrics | Important. Needs careful design. | 151→| ProjectCrossSell | Necessary. Should support blog/labs too. | 152→ 153→**What a case study looks like with 5 components:** 154→``` 155→Title (markdown) 156→Intro paragraph (markdown) 157→ 158→More text (markdown) 159→ 160→ 161→ 162→Results paragraph (markdown) 163→ 164→``` 165→This is a **linear, single-column scroll**. No rhythm, no visual chapters, no surprise. 166→ 167→**What a compelling case study needs:** 168→``` 169→[Hero: full-bleed image/video, title overlay] 170→[Brief: 2-3 sentences, large type] 171→[Challenge with client quote] 172→[Process: timeline/steps] 173→[Design exploration: image grid] 174→[Key decision: callout block] 175→[Solution: full-bleed dark section with device frames] 176→[Details: comparison, color palette, type specimen] 177→[Results: metrics with context] 178→[Impact: client testimonial] 179→[Next: related project cross-sell] 180→``` 181→ 182→**Components to add (by priority):** 183→ 184→| Component | Justification | Priority | 185→|---|---|---| 186→| `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have | 187→| `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have | 188→| `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have | 189→| `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have | 190→| `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have | 191→| `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have | 192→| `ProcessTimeline` | Visualize design process | Should-have | 193→| `ContentCrossSell` | Generic version of ProjectCrossSell (blog/labs too) | Should-have | 194→| `ColorPalette` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | 195→| `TypeSpecimen` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | 196→| `Embed` | Figma prototypes, CodeSandbox | Nice-to-have | 197→ 198→**Devil's Advocate concern:** Every new component requires a code change and deployment. Document the component addition process so it's a 15-minute task, not a research project. 199→ 200→**UX/UI insight:** Consider using `.md` for blog posts (no custom components needed) and `.mdx` only for case studies. Saves MDX compilation overhead on content that doesn't need it. 201→ 202→**Team Decision:** The 5 starting components are a starting point. New components will be created as content needs arise. We won't eagerly build components that might not be needed. Start simple and lean, grow with the content. 203→ 204→--- 205→ 206→### 4. Conversion Architecture Is Nonexistent 207→ 208→**Raised by:** Marketing (strongly), UX/UI. 209→ 210→The plan has zero conversion points. After reading a brilliant case study, the visitor encounters nothing saying "let's work together." 211→ 212→**What's missing:** 213→- `CTA` component embeddable in MDX (headline, body, button, link) 214→- Contact form (Formspree, Netlify Forms, or similar) 215→- Newsletter signup (Buttondown, Resend) 216→- Contextual CTAs at the end of case studies and blog posts 217→- Strategic CTA placement on homepage 218→ 219→**Marketing Director's math:** 220→> 5,000 monthly visitors at 1% contact rate = 50 leads. With proper conversion architecture (CTAs, newsletter, social proof) = 2-3% = 100-150 leads. For a studio, that's predictable pipeline vs. relying on referrals. 221→ 222→**Recommended CTA placements:** 223→- **Case studies:** "Want results like these? Let's talk." at the bottom 224→- **Blog posts:** Newsletter signup mid-article and at bottom 225→- **Labs:** "Built by Significa. See our client work." crosslink 226→- **Homepage:** Primary CTA above the fold, secondary after work section 227→ 228→**Team Decision:** The final structure will have conversion points, but this is something to try and test against real data and metrics. Not a worry for the initial build. Marketing people own this and will iterate based on performance. 229→ 230→--- 231→ 232→### 5. SEO and Technical Infrastructure Missing 233→ 234→**Raised by:** Marketing, Tech Architect. 235→ 236→Elements required for launch day: 237→ 238→| Item | Status | Priority | Effort | 239→|---|---|---|---| 240→| RSS Feed (`@astrojs/rss`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 241→| Sitemap (`@astrojs/sitemap`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 242→| 404 page | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 243→| Canonical URLs | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 244→| Robots.txt | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 245→| `` component (OG, Twitter, meta) | Partial | Must-have | Medium | 246→| JSON-LD structured data | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | 247→| Tag archive pages | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | 248→| View Transitions | Mentioned, not planned | Should-have | Medium | 249→| Pagefind (search) | Not mentioned | Nice-to-have | Low | 250→ 251→**Tech Architect's SEO component:** 252→ 253→```astro 254→--- 255→interface Props { 256→ title: string; 257→ description: string; 258→ ogImage?: string; 259→ type?: 'website' | 'article'; 260→ publishedAt?: Date; 261→} 262→ 263→const { 264→ title, 265→ description, 266→ ogImage = 'https://media.significa.co/og-default.jpg', 267→ type = 'website', 268→ publishedAt 269→} = Astro.props; 270→ 271→const canonicalURL = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site); 272→--- 273→ 274→{title} 275→ 276→ 277→ 278→ 279→ 280→ 281→ 282→ 283→ 284→ 285→ 286→{type === 'article' && publishedAt && ( 287→ 288→)} 289→``` 290→ 291→--- 292→ 293→## Secondary Gaps (Important but Not Blockers) 294→ 295→### Content Types Missing 296→ 297→**Team/People collection** (raised by UX/UI, Marketing): 298→- Name, role, photo, bio, social links 299→- Feeds into: author cards on blog, project credits, about page 300→- A design studio sells people. Without this, the studio feels faceless. 301→ 302→**Testimonials** as structured data (raised by Marketing, UX/UI): 303→- Client name, role, company, quote, associated project 304→- Embeddable in case studies AND aggregatable on homepage 305→- Single highest-ROI missing content type per Marketing Director 306→ 307→**Careers collection** (raised by Marketing): 308→- Title, department, location, type, description, requirements 309→- Enables Google Jobs structured data 310→- Static "careers page" is insufficient if attracting talent is a goal 311→ 312→### Analytics (Raised by Marketing, Tech) 313→ 314→Nothing mentioned in the plan. Unanimous recommendation: 315→- **Plausible or Fathom** (privacy-first, no GDPR cookie banner needed) 316→- **Google Search Console** (free, non-negotiable for SEO) 317→- Track: page views, traffic sources, conversions, content performance 318→ 319→### Version Pinning: Astro v5 Now, v6 When Stable 320→ 321→**Team Decision:** Use latest stable Astro. Currently on **v5** — Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs). Upgrade to v6 as soon as it reaches stable. Until then, keep dependencies up to date within the v5 range. 322→ 323→### CSS Token System (Raised by Devil's Advocate, UX/UI) 324→ 325→**Team Decision:** Confirmed needed. The token system will be documented and developed alongside the design. No magic numbers and no magic colors must be enforced by all agents. The system grows with the project but the rule is absolute from day one. 326→ 327→### Slug Collision Check (Raised by Tech Architect) 328→ 329→**Team Decision:** Include this. We should include as many compile-time validations as possible. A website managed by non-tech and marketing people should error as soon as possible instead of relying on runtime errors. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→const reserved = ['blog', 'projects', 'labs', 'index']; 333→for (const page of pages) { 334→ const topSegment = page.id.split('/')[0]; 335→ if (reserved.includes(topSegment)) { 336→ throw new Error(`Page "${page.id}" collides with reserved route /${topSegment}.`); 337→ } 338→} 339→``` 340→ 341→This philosophy extends to all content validation: use `reference()` instead of plain slugs, require fields that must exist, throw on missing data. Build-time errors are features, not annoyances. 342→ 343→--- 344→ 345→## Plan Strengths (Full Consensus) 346→ 347→What all 4 participants praised: 348→ 349→1. **Build-time validation** with `reference()` and Zod: broken references fail the build, not production 350→2. **Zero JS by default**: performance is excellent out of the box 351→3. **Highlights with discriminatedUnion**: elegant, extensible homepage curation 352→4. **Documentation quality**: clear, opinionated, well-organized (rare for a project plan) 353→5. **MDX components as Astro (not React)**: correct decision, zero client JS overhead 354→6. **Media on CDN, not in git**: repo stays lean, deploys stay fast 355→7. **Content Collections as the "database"**: typed, validated, portable content 356→ 357→--- 358→ 359→## Devil's Advocate: Report Card 360→ 361→| Aspect | Grade | Note | 362→|---|---|---| 363→| Framework choice (Astro) | B+ | Good fit, but version uncertainty is real | 364→| Content strategy (MDX in git) | B- | Works for dev teams, risky for mixed teams | 365→| Schema design | A- | Well-structured, good references and validation | 366→| Component architecture | B | Solid start, needs growth strategy | 367→| CSS approach | B- | Works at small scale, needs token system | 368→| Media strategy | C+ | Too vague. "CDN handles it" isn't a plan | 369→| Build/deploy | B | Fine for now, needs monitoring | 370→| Documentation | A- | Clear, opinionated, well-organized | 371→| Migration risk | B | Content is portable, glue code is not | 372→| Team scalability | C+ | Works for devs, walls off everyone else | 373→ 374→--- 375→ 376→## Interactions and Islands (UX/UI Perspective) 377→ 378→### Where Islands Are Required 379→1. **Mobile navigation**: `client:load` (immediate) 380→2. **ComparisonBlock slider**: `client:visible` (in viewport) 381→3. **Project filtering**: `client:load` (tag-based, immediate results) 382→4. **Image lightbox/zoom**: `client:visible` (portfolio detail viewing) 383→5. **Contact form**: `client:load` (validation + submission) 384→6. **Video player controls**: `client:visible` (CDN-hosted showreels) 385→ 386→### Where Islands Are NOT Needed 387→- Scroll animations: CSS `@scroll-timeline` or Intersection Observer via `

1→# Blog Index Page Documentation 2→ 3→This document explains the structure, features, and implementation of the blog index page at `/blog`. 4→ 5→--- 6→ 7→## Overview 8→ 9→The blog index page serves as the main entry point for all blog content. It features: 10→ 11→- **Hero section** with headline and description 12→- **Featured post** (most recent) with large visual treatment 13→- **Tag filtering** (client-side JavaScript enhancement) 14→- **Blog grid** with card-based layout 15→- **Newsletter CTA** for engagement 16→ 17→--- 18→ 19→## Page Structure 20→ 21→### 1. Blog Header 22→ 23→```astro 24→
25→

We think things through.

26→

Insights on design, development, and building products...

27→
28→``` 29→ 30→**Purpose:** 31→- Sets the tone and context 32→- Clearly communicates what the blog is about 33→- Simple, centered layout 34→ 35→**Styling:** 36→- Large heading (--text-4xl) 37→- Centered text 38→- Generous padding (8rem top) 39→- Neutral background 40→ 41→--- 42→ 43→### 2. Featured Post 44→ 45→The most recent blog post gets special treatment: 46→ 47→**Features:** 48→- Large two-column layout (image + content) 49→- "Featured" label badge 50→- Date and reading time 51→- Full description 52→- Tag badges 53→- Hover effects 54→ 55→**Layout:** 56→- Desktop: 1fr 1fr grid (50/50 split) 57→- Tablet/Mobile: Stacks vertically 58→- Image on left, content on right (desktop) 59→ 60→**Visual treatment:** 61→- Larger font sizes 62→- Border on hover 63→- Image zoom on hover 64→- Clear hierarchy 65→ 66→--- 67→ 68→### 3. Tag Filter 69→ 70→Client-side filtering system for blog posts: 71→ 72→**Features:** 73→- "All" button (default active) 74→- Individual tag buttons 75→- Active state styling 76→- Smooth fade-in animation when filtering 77→ 78→**How it works:** 79→1. Extract all unique tags from posts 80→2. Render as buttons 81→3. JavaScript listens for clicks 82→4. Filters cards by matching `data-tags` attribute 83→5. Animates filtered results 84→ 85→**Implementation:** 86→```javascript 87→// Cards have data-tags attribute 88→
89→ 90→// Filter on button click 91→if (selectedTag === "all" || cardTags.includes(selectedTag)) { 92→ card.style.display = ""; 93→} else { 94→ card.style.display = "none"; 95→} 96→``` 97→ 98→--- 99→ 100→### 4. Blog Grid 101→ 102→Card-based layout for all non-featured posts: 103→ 104→**Features:** 105→- Responsive grid (auto-fill, minmax 320px) 106→- Thumbnail image (600x400px) 107→- Date and reading time 108→- Title and description 109→- Tag badges 110→- Hover effects 111→ 112→**Card anatomy:** 113→``` 114→┌─────────────────┐ 115→│ │ 116→│ Image │ 117→│ │ 118→├─────────────────┤ 119→│ Date • 5 min │ 120→│ │ 121→│ Post Title │ 122→│ │ 123→│ Description... │ 124→│ │ 125→│ [tag] [tag] │ 126→└─────────────────┘ 127→``` 128→ 129→**Behavior:** 130→- Border color change on hover 131→- Image scales 1.05x on hover 132→- Entire card is clickable 133→- Smooth transitions 134→ 135→--- 136→ 137→### 5. Newsletter CTA 138→ 139→Bottom section encouraging engagement: 140→ 141→**Features:** 142→- Centered layout 143→- Clear headline and description 144→- Primary CTA button 145→- Alternative background color 146→ 147→**Can be customized to:** 148→- Link to newsletter signup 149→- Link to RSS feed 150→- Link to contact form 151→- Any engagement action 152→ 153→--- 154→ 155→## Data Processing 156→ 157→### Post Fetching 158→ 159→```astro 160→const allPosts = await getPosts(); // Sorted by date desc 161→const featuredPost = allPosts[0]; // Most recent 162→const recentPosts = allPosts.slice(1); // Rest 163→``` 164→ 165→### Tag Extraction 166→ 167→```astro 168→const allTags = [ 169→ ...new Set(allPosts.flatMap((post) => post.data.tags || [])) 170→].sort(); 171→``` 172→ 173→**Result:** Unique, sorted array of all tags used across posts. 174→ 175→### Reading Time Calculation 176→ 177→```typescript 178→function calculateReadingTime(post): number { 179→ const wordCount = post.body?.split(/\s+/).length ?? 0; 180→ return wordCount > 0 ? Math.ceil(wordCount / 200) : 5; 181→} 182→``` 183→ 184→**Formula:** Word count ÷ 200 words per minute (industry standard). 185→ 186→### Date Formatting 187→ 188→```typescript 189→function formatDate(date: Date): string { 190→ return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { 191→ year: "numeric", 192→ month: "long", 193→ day: "numeric", 194→ }); 195→} 196→``` 197→ 198→**Output:** "January 22, 2026" 199→ 200→--- 201→ 202→## JavaScript Functionality 203→ 204→### Tag Filtering 205→ 206→**Purpose:** Filter posts without page reload. 207→ 208→**How it works:** 209→1. Store tags in `data-tags` attribute as JSON 210→2. Button click updates active state 211→3. Parse each card's tags 212→4. Show/hide based on match 213→5. Animate appearance 214→ 215→**Progressive enhancement:** 216→- Works without JavaScript (all posts visible) 217→- Enhances experience when JS available 218→- Respects View Transitions API 219→ 220→**Code:** 221→```javascript 222→function initTagFilter() { 223→ const buttons = document.querySelectorAll(".tag-button"); 224→ const cards = document.querySelectorAll(".blog-card"); 225→ 226→ buttons.forEach((button) => { 227→ button.addEventListener("click", () => { 228→ const selectedTag = button.dataset.tag; 229→ 230→ // Update active state 231→ buttons.forEach((btn) => btn.classList.remove("active")); 232→ button.classList.add("active"); 233→ 234→ // Filter cards 235→ cards.forEach((card) => { 236→ const cardTags = JSON.parse(card.dataset.tags); 237→ 238→ if (selectedTag === "all" || cardTags.includes(selectedTag)) { 239→ card.style.display = ""; 240→ card.style.animation = "fadeIn 0.3s ease-out"; 241→ } else { 242→ card.style.display = "none"; 243→ } 244→ }); 245→ }); 246→ }); 247→} 248→ 249→// Run on load and after navigation 250→initTagFilter(); 251→document.addEventListener("astro:after-swap", initTagFilter); 252→``` 253→ 254→--- 255→ 256→## Styling Details 257→ 258→### Layout Widths 259→ 260→- **Header/CTA:** `--max-width-narrow` (centered, readable) 261→- **Featured post:** `--max-width` (wider for visual impact) 262→- **Blog grid:** `--max-width` (wider for cards) 263→ 264→### Grid Behavior 265→ 266→```css 267→.blog-grid-inner { 268→ display: grid; 269→ grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr)); 270→ gap: 2rem; 271→} 272→``` 273→ 274→**Result:** 275→- Desktop: 3 columns 276→- Tablet: 2 columns 277→- Mobile: 1 column 278→- Automatic, responsive 279→ 280→### Card Heights 281→ 282→Cards have flexible height: 283→- `.card-link { height: 100%; }` - Fill parent 284→- `.card-content { flex-grow: 1; }` - Expand content 285→- `.card-description { flex-grow: 1; }` - Push tags to bottom 286→- `.card-tags { margin-top: auto; }` - Stick to bottom 287→ 288→**Result:** Aligned cards even with varying content lengths. 289→ 290→### Color Usage 291→ 292→- **Surface:** Main background (white/dark) 293→- **Surface-alt:** Alternating sections, cards 294→- **Border:** Subtle borders on cards, tags 295→- **Text:** Primary content 296→- **Text-muted:** Secondary info (dates, descriptions) 297→ 298→All from design token system. 299→ 300→--- 301→ 302→## Responsive Design 303→ 304→### Breakpoints 305→ 306→**Featured Post (968px):** 307→```css 308→@media (max-width: 968px) { 309→ .featured-link { 310→ grid-template-columns: 1fr; /* Stack vertically */ 311→ } 312→ 313→ .featured-image { 314→ height: 300px; /* Fixed height for stacked layout */ 315→ } 316→} 317→``` 318→ 319→**Mobile (768px):** 320→```css 321→@media (max-width: 768px) { 322→ .blog-title { 323→ font-size: var(--text-3xl); /* Smaller heading */ 324→ } 325→} 326→``` 327→ 328→**Grid:** 329→- Auto-adjusts based on `minmax(320px, 1fr)` 330→- No explicit breakpoints needed 331→- Fluid, responsive by default 332→ 333→--- 334→ 335→## SEO Optimization 336→ 337→### Page Metadata 338→ 339→```astro 340→ 344→``` 345→ 346→### Structured Content 347→ 348→- Semantic HTML (`
`, ``, `
`) 349→- Proper heading hierarchy (H1 → H2) 350→- Alt text on all images 351→- Descriptive link text 352→ 353→### Internal Linking 354→ 355→- Each card links to full post 356→- Related posts visible in grid 357→- Tag-based organization 358→- Clear navigation 359→ 360→--- 361→ 362→## Performance Considerations 363→ 364→### Image Loading 365→ 366→**Featured post:** 367→```astro 368→loading="eager" // Above fold, load immediately 369→``` 370→ 371→**Grid cards:** 372→```astro 373→loading="lazy" // Below fold, lazy load 374→``` 375→ 376→### CDN Usage 377→ 378→All images served via CDN with optimization: 379→```astro 380→src={cdnUrl(post.data.thumbnail, { width: 600, quality: 80 })} 381→``` 382→ 383→**Benefits:** 384→- Automatic resizing 385→- Format optimization 386→- Caching 387→- Fast delivery 388→ 389→### JavaScript 390→ 391→- **Minimal:** ~40 lines for filtering 392→- **Progressive:** Works without JS 393→- **Efficient:** Event delegation, no loops 394→- **Cached:** Static, changes rarely 395→ 396→--- 397→ 398→## Accessibility 399→ 400→### Keyboard Navigation 401→ 402→- All interactive elements focusable 403→- Clear focus states 404→- Logical tab order 405→ 406→### Screen Readers 407→ 408→- Semantic HTML throughout 409→- `` elements with `datetime` attribute 410→- Alt text on all images 411→- Clear link purposes 412→ 413→### Color Contrast 414→ 415→- Text meets WCAG AA standards 416→- Sufficient contrast ratios 417→- No color-only indicators 418→ 419→--- 420→ 421→## Content Requirements 422→ 423→### For Posts to Appear Correctly 424→ 425→**Required:** 426→- `title` - Display in cards 427→- `date` - Sorting and display 428→ 429→**Recommended:** 430→- `thumbnail` - Visual interest (600x400px minimum) 431→- `description` - Preview text 432→- `tags` - Categorization and filtering 433→- `author` - Attribution 434→ 435→**Optional:** 436→- `seo.*` - Override defaults 437→ 438→### Missing Content Handling 439→ 440→**No thumbnail:** 441→- Card still displays with placeholder background 442→- Content fills available space 443→- No broken image 444→ 445→**No description:** 446→- Card shows title and meta only 447→- Still looks complete 448→ 449→**No tags:** 450→- No tag badges shown 451→- Card still functional 452→ 453→--- 454→ 455→## Customization Guide 456→ 457→### Change Featured Post Logic 458→ 459→Currently shows most recent. To change: 460→ 461→```astro 462→// Example: Feature specific post 463→const featuredPost = allPosts.find(p => p.data.featured); 464→ 465→// Example: Feature random post 466→const featuredPost = allPosts[Math.floor(Math.random() * allPosts.length)]; 467→``` 468→ 469→### Add More Filters 470→ 471→Beyond tags, you could add: 472→ 473→```astro 474→// Author filter 475→const authors = [...new Set(allPosts.map(p => p.data.author))]; 476→ 477→// Year filter 478→const years = [...new Set(allPosts.map(p => p.data.date.getFullYear()))]; 479→ 480→// Category filter (if you add categories) 481→const categories = [...new Set(allPosts.flatMap(p => p.data.categories))]; 482→``` 483→ 484→### Modify Card Layout 485→ 486→Change grid columns: 487→ 488→```css 489→/* 4 columns on large screens */ 490→grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr)); 491→ 492→/* 2 columns max */ 493→grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(400px, 1fr)); 494→``` 495→ 496→### Add Pagination 497→ 498→For many posts: 499→ 500→```astro 501→const POSTS_PER_PAGE = 12; 502→const page = Number(Astro.url.searchParams.get('page')) || 1; 503→const paginatedPosts = recentPosts.slice( 504→ (page - 1) * POSTS_PER_PAGE, 505→ page * POSTS_PER_PAGE 506→); 507→``` 508→ 509→--- 510→ 511→## Best Practices 512→ 513→### Image Specifications 514→ 515→**Featured post:** 516→- Size: 1200x675px (16:9) 517→- Format: JPG 518→- Quality: 85% 519→- Loading: eager 520→ 521→**Grid cards:** 522→- Size: 600x400px (3:2) 523→- Format: JPG 524→- Quality: 80% 525→- Loading: lazy 526→ 527→### Tag Naming 528→ 529→- **lowercase** - "design" not "Design" 530→- **hyphenated** - "case-study" not "case study" 531→- **specific** - "ui-design" not just "design" 532→- **consistent** - Use same tags across posts 533→ 534→### Writing Card Content 535→ 536→**Titles:** 537→- 50-70 characters ideal 538→- Front-load important words 539→- No clickbait 540→ 541→**Descriptions:** 542→- 100-150 characters 543→- Complete sentence 544→- Entice without spoiling 545→ 546→--- 547→ 548→## Analytics Recommendations 549→ 550→Track these events: 551→ 552→1. **Featured post clicks** - Measure effectiveness 553→2. **Tag filter usage** - Which topics are popular 554→3. **Card clicks** - Which posts get attention 555→4. **Scroll depth** - How far users browse 556→5. **Newsletter CTA clicks** - Conversion rate 557→ 558→--- 559→ 560→## Future Enhancements 561→ 562→Consider adding: 563→ 564→1. **Search functionality** - Full-text search 565→2. **Load more button** - Instead of pagination 566→3. **Related posts** - "You might also like..." 567→4. **Author pages** - Filter by author 568→5. **RSS feed** - Already exists at `/blog/rss.xml` 569→6. **Reading progress** - Show how much user has read 570→7. **Share buttons** - Social sharing 571→8. **Bookmarking** - Save for later 572→9. **Comments** - Discussion system 573→10. **View count** - Popular posts indicator 574→ 575→--- 576→ 577→## Troubleshooting 578→ 579→### Tags not filtering 580→ 581→Check: 582→1. Posts have `tags` in frontmatter 583→2. JavaScript is enabled 584→3. `data-tags` attribute is valid JSON 585→4. Console for errors 586→ 587→### Cards have different heights 588→ 589→Ensure: 590→1. `.card-link { height: 100%; }` 591→2. `.card-description { flex-grow: 1; }` 592→3. `.card-tags { margin-top: auto; }` 593→ 594→### Featured post not showing 595→ 596→Verify: 597→1. At least one post exists 598→2. Post has all required fields 599→3. Array is not empty: `allPosts.length > 0` 600→ 601→### Images not loading 602→ 603→Check: 604→1. CDN path is correct 605→2. Image exists at path 606→3. CDN service is running 607→4. No CORS issues 608→ 609→--- 610→ 611→## Related Documentation 612→ 613→- **Blog post template:** `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` 614→- **MDX components:** `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` 615→- **Design tokens:** `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` 616→- **SEO guidelines:** `docs/09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` 617→ 618→--- 619→ 620→This blog index provides a modern, performant, and user-friendly way to discover and filter blog content while maintaining the design system and accessibility standards.

1→# Blog Post Template & Guidelines
     2→
     3→This document explains the structure, content, and best practices for creating blog posts on the Significa website.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Quick Start
     8→
     9→1. Create a new `.mdx` or `.md` file in `src/content/blog/`
    10→2. Use kebab-case for the filename: `designing-complex-products.mdx`
    11→3. Copy the frontmatter template below
    12→4. Write your content using MDX components
    13→5. Add images via CDN (`https://cdn.significa.co/blog/...`)
    14→6. Reference related projects using `relatedProjects` array
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Frontmatter Template
    19→
    20→```yaml
    21→---
    22→title: Your Post Title Here
    23→description: A compelling one-sentence summary that appears in previews and meta descriptions
    24→date: 2026-01-22
    25→author: Author Name
    26→thumbnail: https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/hero.jpg
    27→tags:
    28→  - design
    29→  - guide
    30→  - case-study
    31→relatedProjects:
    32→  - project-slug
    33→seo:
    34→  metaTitle: Your Post Title — Significa
    35→  metaDescription: Extended description for search engines (150-160 characters)
    36→  ogImage: https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/og-image.jpg
    37→---
    38→```
    39→
    40→### Frontmatter Fields
    41→
    42→| Field | Required | Type | Description |
    43→|---|---|---|---|
    44→| `title` | Yes | string | Post title (50-60 characters ideal) |
    45→| `description` | No | string | One-sentence summary for previews |
    46→| `date` | Yes | date | Publication date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
    47→| `author` | No | string | Author's full name |
    48→| `thumbnail` | No | string | CDN path to hero image (16:9 ratio, 1600x900px) |
    49→| `tags` | No | array | Content categories (lowercase, hyphenated) |
    50→| `relatedProjects` | No | array | Project slugs (validated at build time) |
    51→| `seo.metaTitle` | No | string | Override default page title |
    52→| `seo.metaDescription` | No | string | Override default description |
    53→| `seo.ogImage` | No | string | Social sharing image (1200x630px) |
    54→
    55→### Common Tags
    56→
    57→- `design` - Design process, UI/UX, visual design
    58→- `development` - Engineering, code, technical
    59→- `strategy` - Product strategy, planning
    60→- `guide` - How-to articles, tutorials
    61→- `case-study` - Project retrospectives
    62→- `team` - Culture, hiring, team posts
    63→- `learning` - Lessons learned, insights
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## Content Structure
    68→
    69→### Opening Hook
    70→
    71→Start with a compelling hook that establishes context and creates curiosity:
    72→
    73→```mdx
    74→I recently designed an insurance management product. My knowledge of insurance? Zero.
    75→
    76→I could barely tell you the difference between premiums and brokers, let alone design a platform that finance professionals would use daily to reconcile thousands of payments. But here's the thing: designers rarely start projects as domain experts, and we don't need to be.
    77→```
    78→
    79→**Why this works:**
    80→- Vulnerable opening (relatable)
    81→- Specific problem stated
    82→- Promise of insight/solution
    83→
    84→### Project Cross-Sell (Optional)
    85→
    86→Link to a related project early in the post:
    87→
    88→```mdx
    89→<ProjectCrossSell
    90→  project="mishmash"
    91→  title="Streamlining insurance payments"
    92→  description="Before you uncover all the insights from this article, discover the project behind it."
    93→/>
    94→```
    95→
    96→**Best practices:**
    97→- Place after intro, before first H2
    98→- Use when post relates to a specific project
    99→- Keep description focused on the project, not the post
   100→
   101→### Body Structure
   102→
   103→Use clear hierarchy with H2 and H3 headings:
   104→
   105→```mdx
   106→## Main Section Heading
   107→
   108→Opening paragraph for the section.
   109→
   110→### Subsection (if needed)
   111→
   112→More specific content.
   113→```
   114→
   115→**Heading guidelines:**
   116→- H2 for main sections (actionable, clear)
   117→- H3 for subsections (rare, use sparingly)
   118→- No H4 or deeper (simplify instead)
   119→- Keep headings short (3-7 words)
   120→- Use sentence case, not title case
   121→
   122→### Visual Content
   123→
   124→Use MDX components to add rich media:
   125→
   126→#### Images
   127→
   128→```mdx
   129→<MediaImage
   130→  src="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/image-name.jpg"
   131→  alt="Descriptive alt text for accessibility"
   132→  width={1260}
   133→  height={840}
   134→  caption="Optional caption explaining the image"
   135→/>
   136→```
   137→
   138→**Image specifications:**
   139→- Format: JPG for photos, PNG for screenshots/diagrams
   140→- Max width: 1600px (for retina displays)
   141→- Aspect ratio: 3:2 or 16:9 preferred
   142→- File size: <500KB per image
   143→- CDN path: `https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg`
   144→
   145→#### Video
   146→
   147→```mdx
   148→<MediaVideo
   149→  src="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/video-name.mp4"
   150→  width={1260}
   151→  height={840}
   152→  poster="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/video-poster.jpg"
   153→  caption="Optional video caption"
   154→/>
   155→```
   156→
   157→#### Comparison Blocks
   158→
   159→```mdx
   160→<ComparisonBlock
   161→  before={{
   162→    src: "https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/before.jpg",
   163→    label: "Before — Immediate confirmation dialog",
   164→  }}
   165→  after={{
   166→    src: "https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/after.jpg",
   167→    label: "After — Undo toast with 10s grace period",
   168→  }}
   169→/>
   170→```
   171→
   172→**Use for:**
   173→- Before/after redesigns
   174→- Problem/solution comparisons
   175→- Old vs new approach
   176→
   177→#### Metrics
   178→
   179→```mdx
   180→<Metrics
   181→  items={[
   182→    { value: "+12%", label: "Engagement increase" },
   183→    { value: "3x", label: "Faster task completion" },
   184→    { value: "−28%", label: "Support tickets reduced" },
   185→  ]}
   186→/>
   187→```
   188→
   189→**Best practices:**
   190→- 3-5 metrics maximum
   191→- Use real data when possible
   192→- Include context in labels
   193→- Format consistently (+/−/x)
   194→
   195→### Quotes
   196→
   197→Use blockquotes for testimonials or key insights:
   198→
   199→```mdx
   200→> "From the outside, it might not seem like the most exciting area, but I was genuinely surprised by how intricate and intellectually stimulating it is. Insurance payments involve a huge amount of nuance, edge cases, and interconnected rules."
   201→>
   202→> **Julieta Frade** — Front-end developer
   203→```
   204→
   205→**Formatting:**
   206→- Use `>` for blockquotes
   207→- Add attribution on a new line
   208→- Bold the name with `**Name**`
   209→- Use em dash (—) before role/title
   210→
   211→### Lists
   212→
   213→Use lists for clarity:
   214→
   215→**Bullet lists:**
   216→```mdx
   217→- **Choose what to "buy"** → Select which premiums to process
   218→- **Review your cart** → Double-check selections
   219→- **Complete checkout** → Process the payout
   220→```
   221→
   222→**Numbered lists:**
   223→```mdx
   224→1. Create a domain model early
   225→2. Run structured workshops
   226→3. Keep feedback continuous
   227→4. Validate with real users
   228→```
   229→
   230→**Best practices:**
   231→- Bold key terms at the start
   232→- Keep items parallel in structure
   233→- Use 3-7 items (never just 2)
   234→- Add context after em dash (→) if needed
   235→
   236→### Closing
   237→
   238→End with a clear takeaway:
   239→
   240→```mdx
   241→## Lessons learned.
   242→
   243→Every project is a crash course in something new. You don't need to become an industry expert to design a great product, but you do need to collaborate effectively, ask the right questions, simplify complexity when you can, and validate your assumptions with real users.
   244→
   245→The best work doesn't come from designers working in isolation or clients dictating solutions. They come from genuine collaboration where both sides bring what they know best and build something better together.
   246→
   247→That's what made this project work… and that's what I'll carry into the next unfamiliar industry I encounter.
   248→```
   249→
   250→**Why this works:**
   251→- Summarizes key insights
   252→- Actionable takeaway
   253→- Personal reflection
   254→- Forward-looking
   255→
   256→---
   257→
   258→## Writing Style Guide
   259→
   260→### Voice & Tone
   261→
   262→- **Conversational but professional** - Write like you're explaining to a colleague
   263→- **First person for personal posts** - "I designed..." not "We designed..."
   264→- **Active voice** - "We tested the prototype" not "The prototype was tested"
   265→- **Specific over vague** - "Reduced support tickets by 28%" not "Improved efficiency"
   266→
   267→### Sentence Structure
   268→
   269→- **Vary sentence length** - Mix short punchy sentences with longer explanatory ones
   270→- **One idea per sentence** - Complex ideas deserve their own space
   271→- **Start strong** - Put the main point early in the sentence
   272→- **Cut filler words** - "really", "very", "just", "actually" rarely add value
   273→
   274→### Paragraphs
   275→
   276→- **2-4 sentences per paragraph** - Easier to scan online
   277→- **One idea per paragraph** - New point = new paragraph
   278→- **Add breathing room** - White space improves readability
   279→
   280→### Common Mistakes
   281→
   282→❌ **Don't:**
   283→- Start sentences with "So..."
   284→- Overuse em dashes (—) for dramatic effect
   285→- Write in abstract generalities
   286→- Use corporate jargon
   287→- Apologize for opinions ("In my humble opinion...")
   288→
   289→✅ **Do:**
   290→- Use concrete examples
   291→- Show your work (screenshots, diagrams)
   292→- Tell stories
   293→- Share failures alongside wins
   294→- Link to related work
   295→
   296→---
   297→
   298→## Technical Considerations
   299→
   300→### MDX vs MD
   301→
   302→- Use `.mdx` if you need MDX components (MediaImage, Metrics, etc.)
   303→- Use `.md` for simple text-only posts
   304→- Both support frontmatter and markdown syntax
   305→
   306→### Links
   307→
   308→**Internal links:**
   309→```mdx
   310→[related project](/projects/mishmash)
   311→[our services](/services)
   312→```
   313→
   314→**External links:**
   315→```mdx
   316→[external resource](https://example.com)
   317→```
   318→
   319→**Project references:**
   320→```yaml
   321→relatedProjects:
   322→  - mishmash
   323→  - planit-app
   324→```
   325→
   326→Build validates these at compile time. Typos break the build.
   327→
   328→### Images via CDN
   329→
   330→All images must be served from `https://cdn.significa.co`:
   331→
   332→```
   333→https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg
   334→```
   335→
   336→**Folder structure:**
   337→```
   338→cdn.significa.co/
   339→└── blog/
   340→    └── designing-complex-products/
   341→        ├── hero.jpg
   342→        ├── london-workshop.jpg
   343→        ├── domain-model.jpg
   344→        └── og-image.jpg
   345→```
   346→
   347→**Image optimization:**
   348→- Use CDN's automatic resizing
   349→- Specify `width` and `height` attributes
   350→- Always include descriptive `alt` text
   351→- Use `loading="lazy"` for below-fold images
   352→
   353→### Reading Time
   354→
   355→Automatically calculated based on word count (200 words per minute).
   356→
   357→### URL Structure
   358→
   359→Posts live at `/blog/{filename-without-extension}`:
   360→
   361→```
   362→src/content/blog/designing-complex-products.mdx
   363→→ https://significa.co/blog/designing-complex-products
   364→```
   365→
   366→---

   375→- Add brand suffix: "— Significa"

   382→- Match article tone

   384→### OG Image
   385→
   386→Social sharing image specifications:
   387→
   388→- Size: 1200x630px (Facebook/LinkedIn ratio)
   389→- Format: JPG (better compression)
   390→- Text overlay: Large, readable from thumbnail
   391→- Include Significa branding
   392→
   393→### Internal Linking
   394→
   395→- Link to 2-3 related projects or posts
   396→- Use descriptive anchor text
   397→- Add related projects in frontmatter
   398→- Link naturally within content

1→# Parent-Child Page Structure
     2→
     3→This document explains how to create hierarchical page relationships in the handbook and pages collections.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## How It Works
     8→
     9→The parent-child structure is based on **file system hierarchy**. No frontmatter field is needed — the directory structure determines the relationship.
    10→
    11→### File System Rules
    12→
    13→```
    14→src/content/handbook/
    15→├── mission-and-values.mdx              → /handbook/mission-and-values (parent)
    16→└── mission-and-values/
    17→    ├── code-of-ethics.mdx              → /handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics (child)
    18→    └── diversity-and-inclusion.mdx     → /handbook/mission-and-values/diversity-and-inclusion (child)
    19→```
    20→
    21→**Key principle:** A page's ID is its file path (without extension). Children have IDs that start with their parent's ID + `/`.
    22→
    23→- Parent page: `mission-and-values` (from `mission-and-values.mdx`)
    24→- Child page: `mission-and-values/code-of-ethics` (from `mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`)
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Creating a Parent-Child Relationship
    29→
    30→### Step 1: Create the Parent Page
    31→
    32→Create a top-level `.mdx` file:
    33→
    34→```mdx
    35→---
    36→title: Mission and Values
    37→description: Our mission and the core values that guide everything we do.
    38→date: 2024-09-06
    39→group: knowing-significa
    40→order: 3
    41→---
    42→
    43→## Content of the parent page...
    44→```
    45→
    46→This becomes `/handbook/mission-and-values`.
    47→
    48→### Step 2: Create a Directory for Children
    49→
    50→Create a directory with the **exact same name** as the parent file (without extension):
    51→
    52→```bash
    53→mkdir src/content/handbook/mission-and-values
    54→```
    55→
    56→### Step 3: Add Child Pages
    57→
    58→Create `.mdx` files inside the directory:
    59→
    60→```mdx
    61→---
    62→title: Code of Ethics
    63→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
    64→date: 2024-01-15
    65→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
    66→group: knowing-significa
    67→order: 1
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Content of the child page...
    71→```
    72→
    73→This becomes `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`.
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## Helper Functions
    78→
    79→These functions in `src/lib/collections.ts` handle parent-child relationships:
    80→
    81→### `getHandbookChildren(parentId: string)`
    82→
    83→Returns all direct children of a parent page.
    84→
    85→```ts
    86→const children = await getHandbookChildren("mission-and-values");
    87→// Returns: [code-of-ethics, diversity-and-inclusion]
    88→```
    89→
    90→### `getHandbookParent(entry: HandbookEntry)`
    91→
    92→Returns the parent page of a child, or `null` for top-level pages.
    93→
    94→```ts
    95→const parent = await getHandbookParent(codeOfEthicsEntry);
    96→// Returns: mission-and-values entry
    97→```
    98→
    99→### `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry: HandbookEntry)`
   100→
   101→Returns the full breadcrumb trail from root to the current page (exclusive).
   102→
   103→```ts
   104→const breadcrumbs = await getHandbookBreadcrumbs(codeOfEthicsEntry);
   105→// Returns: [mission-and-values]
   106→```
   107→
   108→### `getHandbookIndex()`
   109→
   110→Returns the full grouped structure with children attached. Used by the index page.
   111→
   112→```ts
   113→const groups = await getHandbookIndex();
   114→// Returns: [
   115→//   {
   116→//     group: { id: "knowing-significa", data: { title: "Knowing Significa" } },
   117→//     pages: [
   118→//       {
   119→//         page: mission-and-values,
   120→//         children: [code-of-ethics, diversity-and-inclusion]
   121→//       }
   122→//     ]
   123→//   }
   124→// ]
   125→```
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→## UI Behavior
   130→
   131→### Index Page (`/handbook`)
   132→
   133→- Shows top-level pages grouped by `group`
   134→- If a parent has children, they appear indented below it
   135→- Clicking a parent or child navigates to that page
   136→
   137→### Detail Page (`/handbook/[...slug]`)
   138→
   139→**Parent pages:**
   140→- Show their content
   141→- Display an "In this section" box listing all children at the bottom
   142→
   143→**Child pages:**
   144→- Show breadcrumbs at the top (Handbook › Parent › Child)
   145→- Show their content
   146→- If they have children, display "In this section" box
   147→
   148→**Sidebar navigation:**
   149→- Shows full hierarchy with collapsible sections
   150→- Children only visible when parent is active or current
   151→- Active page highlighted in primary color
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## Nesting Depth
   156→
   157→The system supports **unlimited nesting depth**:
   158→
   159→```
   160→how-we-build-software.mdx                          (parent)
   161→how-we-build-software/
   162→  front-end-development.mdx                        (child of how-we-build-software)
   163→  front-end-development/
   164→    react-best-practices.mdx                       (child of front-end-development)
   165→    react-best-practices/
   166→      hooks-and-state-management.mdx               (child of react-best-practices)
   167→```
   168→
   169→However, **2-3 levels maximum is recommended** for usability. Deeper nesting becomes hard to navigate.
   170→
   171→---
   172→
   173→## Build-Time Validation
   174→
   175→The system validates relationships at build time:
   176→
   177→1. **No orphaned directories** — If `mission-and-values/` exists, `mission-and-values.mdx` must exist as the parent.
   178→2. **No ID collisions** — File names must be unique within their directory level.
   179→3. **Group references** — All pages must reference a valid `handbook-groups` entry (validated via `reference()`).
   180→
   181→If validation fails, **the build breaks**. This prevents broken links in production.
   182→
   183→---
   184→
   185→## Common Patterns
   186→
   187→### Creating a Section with Multiple Children
   188→
   189→```
   190→culture.mdx                      → /handbook/culture
   191→culture/
   192→  diversity.mdx                  → /handbook/culture/diversity
   193→  remote-work.mdx                → /handbook/culture/remote-work
   194→  team-rituals.mdx               → /handbook/culture/team-rituals
   195→```
   196→
   197→### Standalone Pages (No Children)
   198→
   199→Just create the `.mdx` file without a matching directory:
   200→
   201→```
   202→changelog.mdx                    → /handbook/changelog (no children)
   203→```
   204→
   205→### Adding Children to an Existing Page
   206→
   207→1. Create the directory: `mkdir src/content/handbook/existing-page`
   208→2. Add child files inside the directory
   209→3. The parent page automatically displays them in "In this section"
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Frontmatter Reference
   214→
   215→All pages use the same schema, whether parent or child:
   216→
   217→```yaml
   218→title: string                    # Required. Page title
   219→description: string              # Optional. Short description
   220→date: Date                       # Required. Last updated date (YYYY-MM-DD)
   221→coverImage: string               # Optional. Cover image (full URL)
   222→group: reference("handbook-groups")  # Required. Which group it belongs to
   223→order: number                    # Optional. Sort order within group (default: 0)
   224→seo:                             # Optional. SEO overrides
   225→  metaTitle: string
   226→  metaDescription: string
   227→  ogImage: string
   228→```
   229→
   230→**Note:** There is no `parent` field. The relationship is determined by file location.
   231→
   232→---
   233→
   234→## Extending to Other Collections
   235→
   236→This pattern can be applied to any collection. For example, if you wanted parent-child pages in the `pages` collection:
   237→
   238→1. Update `content.config.ts` to allow nested file structure
   239→2. Create helper functions similar to `getHandbookChildren()` in `src/lib/collections.ts`
   240→3. Update `src/pages/[...slug].astro` to handle children
   241→4. Add UI for displaying child pages
   242→
   243→The handbook implementation can serve as a reference.
   244→
   245→---
   246→
   247→## Examples
   248→
   249→### Example 1: Mission and Values → Code of Ethics
   250→
   251→**Parent:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values.mdx`
   252→- URL: `/handbook/mission-and-values`
   253→- Group: `knowing-significa`
   254→
   255→**Child:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
   256→- URL: `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`
   257→- Group: `knowing-significa` (must match parent's group)
   258→
   259→### Example 2: How We Build Software → Front-End Development
   260→
   261→**Parent:** `src/content/handbook/how-we-build-software.mdx`
   262→- URL: `/handbook/how-we-build-software`
   263→- Group: `engineering`
   264→
   265→**Child:** `src/content/handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development.mdx`
   266→- URL: `/handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development`
   267→- Group: `engineering` (must match parent's group)
   268→
   269→---
   270→
   271→## Gotchas
   272→
   273→1. **Directory name must match file name exactly** (case-sensitive, including hyphens)
   274→   - ✅ `mission-and-values.mdx` + `mission-and-values/`
   275→   - ❌ `mission-and-values.mdx` + `mission_and_values/`
   276→
   277→2. **Children must belong to the same group as their parent** — Otherwise they won't appear correctly in the navigation.
   278→
   279→3. **The `.draft` suffix works for parent and child pages** — `mission-and-values.draft.mdx` or `code-of-ethics.draft.mdx` are excluded in production.
   280→
   281→4. **Changing a parent's filename breaks children's URLs** — Renaming `mission-and-values.mdx` to `values.mdx` requires renaming the directory and all child IDs.
   282→
   283→5. **Cover images are served from CDN** — Use paths like `/handbook/my-image.jpg` which resolve to `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`. The system automatically generates responsive srcsets.
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## Cover Images
   288→
   289→You can add an optional cover image to any handbook page (parent or child).
   290→
   291→### Adding a Cover Image
   292→
   293→Add the `coverImage` field to your frontmatter with a full URL:
   294→
   295→```yaml
   296→---
   297→title: Code of Ethics
   298→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
   299→date: 2024-01-15
   300→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
   301→group: knowing-significa
   302→order: 1
   303→---
   304→```
   305→
   306→### URL Format
   307→
   308→The `coverImage` field accepts **full URLs**. You can use:
   309→
   310→- **CDN URLs:** `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`
   311→- **External URLs:** `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz`
   312→- **Any public image URL**
   313→
   314→The system handles all URL formats correctly:
   315→- CDN URLs get automatic optimization parameters
   316→- External URLs are used as-is
   317→- No need to worry about paths vs URLs
   318→
   319→### Image Guidelines
   320→
   321→- **URL format:** Full URL (e.g., `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`)
   322→- **Recommended dimensions:** 1200×630px or larger (16:9 or 2:1 aspect ratio)
   323→- **File format:** JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics with transparency
   324→- **File size:** Optimize before uploading (CDN images will be further optimized)
   325→- **Alt text:** Cover images are decorative, so no alt text is added
   326→
   327→### Display Behavior
   328→
   329→- Cover image appears between the page header and content
   330→- Full-width within the content area (max 840px on desktop)
   331→- Rounded corners (8px border-radius)
   332→- Responsive srcset generated automatically
   333→- Loaded eagerly with high priority (optimized for LCP)
   334→
   335→### Example
   336→
   337→**Code of Ethics page** with cover:
   338→```
   339→/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics
   340→```
   341→
   342→The cover image displays prominently at the top, setting the visual tone for the ethical guidelines content.
   343→
   344→### URL Examples
   345→
   346→```yaml
   347→# Significa CDN (recommended)
   348→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/ethics-cover.jpg
   349→
   350→# External service
   351→coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1234567890
   352→
   353→# Any public URL
   354→coverImage: https://example.com/images/cover.jpg
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→## Summary
   360→
   361→- **Parent-child relationships are defined by file structure**, not frontmatter.
   362→- **Create a directory matching the parent filename** to add children.

---

## 1. `/docs/06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md`

**Project-specific info worth keeping:**

- Gotcha 6 is project-specific: `.draft` filename suffix is handled by a custom `contentLoader()` helper at the glob level, not by query-time filtering. No `getCollection()` filter is needed.
- Gotcha 7: this project uses `src/content.config.ts` (not `src/content/content.config.ts`).
- The integration checklist mentions `cdn.significa.co` (self-hosted sharp transform service) as the image optimization strategy, which contradicts the generic Astro docs advice to use `astro:assets`.
- `ViewTransitions` was renamed to `ClientRouter` in v5; the old name is deprecated and will break in v6.
- Analytics integration is explicitly listed as NOT done (unchecked item).

**Pure padding:**

Everything else. The entire file is a generic Astro tutorial: component anatomy, loaders, `getStaticPaths`, MDX setup, island directives, scoped styles, View Transitions API, performance patterns, build commands. All of this is in the official Astro docs and adds zero project-specific value. This file is almost entirely safe to remove or replace with a link to `docs.astro.build`.

**Verdict:** Keep only the 4 bullet points above. The rest is generic docs padding.

---

## 2. `/docs/07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md`

This is the most information-dense file. Almost nothing is padding.

**Essential decisions that must survive compression:**

- Hybrid page architecture: bespoke `.astro` files in `src/pages/` for marketing pages (homepage, who-we-are, what-we-do, work-with-us); MDX only for prose content. Rationale: MDX fights grid/full-bleed layouts.
- Reserved routes that `[...slug].astro` checks against: `blog`, `projects`, `labs`, `who-we-are`, `what-we-do`, `work-with-us`.
- `clients` collection: YAML, used for logo strip. Fields: `name`, `logo`, `url`, `order`.
- `testimonials` collection: YAML. Fields: `quote`, `author`, `role`, `company`, `avatar`, `order`.
- `awards` collection: YAML, each entry references a project via `reference()`.
- Project `metrics` field: optional array with `value` + `label`, shown on homepage showcase cards.
- Project `heroImage` field: optional, falls back to `thumbnail` for homepage showcase.
- Showreel cursor: vanilla `<script>` with `requestAnimationFrame` lerp, not a React island. Re-inits via `astro:after-swap`. Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`. Falls back to static button on touch.
- Industries/capabilities: hardcoded arrays on the homepage, not collections. Rarely change, used in one place only.
- Draft system: `.draft` filename suffix, excluded at glob level by `contentLoader()`, included in dev. Publishing = rename.
- Media pipeline: S3 + Fly.io sharp transforms + Cloudflare edge at `cdn.significa.co`. Internal upload tool at `assets.significa.co`.
- No CMS decision, with explicit rationale: marketing uses git + AI. Phase 2 escape hatch = Decap or Tina CMS.
- `reference()` is non-negotiable. Build-time validation beats runtime errors for a non-developer-managed site.
- Slug collision check code: throws if a `pages` collection entry's top segment matches reserved routes.

**What is padding:**

- The "Participants" table and their bios.
- The "Plan Strengths" section (praises things already documented elsewhere).
- The "Devil's Advocate Report Card" table.
- The "Competitor Comparison" table.
- The "Secondary Gaps: Analytics" section (decision deferred, no concrete action taken).

## 3. `/docs/11-BLOG-INDEX.md`

**Project-specific info:**

- Reading time formula: word count divided by 200 wpm, with a fallback of 5 minutes. Uses `post.body?.split(/\s+/).length`.
- Tag filtering uses vanilla JS, `data-tags` attribute with JSON, and re-inits on `astro:after-swap` for View Transitions compatibility.
- Featured post is `allPosts[0]` (most recent). The rest go into the grid.
- Grid: `repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr))`, gap `2rem`.
- Featured post breakpoint: 968px stacks to single column. Mobile heading shrinks at 768px.
- Image dimensions: featured = 1200x675px (16:9), cards = 600x400px (3:2).

**Pure padding:**

- The "Future Enhancements" section (10 items, none implemented).
- The "Troubleshooting" section (debugging tips for generic issues).
- The "Best Practices: Writing Card Content" section (title length advice).
- Accessibility and SEO sections are generic web best practices, not project-specific.

**Verdict:** About 60% padding. The unique info is the reading time formula, the `astro:after-swap` re-init pattern, the CDN call signature, and the image dimension specs.

---

## 4. `/docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md`

**Essential project-specific rules:**

- Frontmatter schema: `title` (required), `date` (required, YYYY-MM-DD), `description`, `author`, `thumbnail` (CDN URL, 16:9, 1600x900px), `tags` (lowercase hyphenated), `relatedProjects` (array of slugs, validated at build time), `seo.metaTitle`, `seo.metaDescription`, `seo.ogImage`.
- `relatedProjects` slugs are validated at build time. A typo breaks the build.
- CDN path convention for blog images: `https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg`.
- OG image spec: 1200x630px, JPG.
- Use `.mdx` if you need MDX components; `.md` for text-only posts.
- `ProjectCrossSell` placement: after intro, before first H2.
- Canonical tag convention: add `— Significa` suffix to meta title.

**Essential writing rules (project voice, not generic):**

- The "Common Tags" list is the actual taxonomy to follow: `design`, `development`, `strategy`, `guide`, `case-study`, `team`, `learning`.
- Real example file exists: `src/content/blog/designing-complex-products.mdx`.

**Pure padding:**

- The "Content Structure" section with the example prose is illustrative but not a rule.
- The "Writing Style Guide" is generic editorial advice (vary sentence length, active voice, cut filler words). None of it is Significa-specific.
- The three "Example Post Structures" are aspirational templates, not enforced by code.
- The "Getting Help" section is stale routing to other docs.
- The "Publishing Checklist" duplicates the schema reference.
- The SEO section mostly repeats the frontmatter field descriptions.

**Verdict:** The frontmatter schema, CDN path convention, `relatedProjects` build-time validation warning, tag taxonomy, and `.mdx` vs `.md` guidance are the only things that must survive.

---

## 5. `/docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`

**Yes, it mentions the rename gotcha.** Line 281, explicitly:

> "Changing a parent's filename breaks children's URLs — Renaming `mission-and-values.mdx` to `values.mdx` requires renaming the directory and all child IDs."

**Other essential project-specific rules:**

- Parent-child is determined purely by filesystem. No frontmatter field. A child's ID starts with the parent's ID plus `/`.
- To create children: directory name must match the parent filename exactly (case-sensitive, hyphens included).
- Orphaned directory validation: if `mission-and-values/` exists, `mission-and-values.mdx` must exist. Build breaks otherwise.
- Children must belong to the same `group` as their parent, or navigation breaks.
- Helper functions in `src/lib/collections.ts`: `getHandbookChildren(parentId)`, `getHandbookParent(entry)`, `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry)`, `getHandbookIndex()`.
- `group` field uses `reference("handbook-groups")`, not a plain string.
- `coverImage` field accepts full URLs. CDN URLs get optimization params; external URLs used as-is.
- The draft suffix works for both parent and child pages.
- This pattern is handbook-specific currently, but the doc explains how to extend it to other collections via `content.config.ts` + new helper functions.

**Pure padding:**

- The "Examples" section at the bottom (lines 248-268) repeats information already covered in the "How It Works" section.
- The "Cover Images" section is largely implementation detail that could be a one-liner note.
- The "Extending to Other Collections" section is speculative.
- The "Summary" box at the end repeats what was already said.

**Verdict:** The rename gotcha is present and documented. The three essential rules are: filesystem determines hierarchy, directory name must exactly match parent filename, and renaming a parent requires renaming the directory and all children. Everything else is elaboration.

content.content:
1→# Significa Website: Documentation
     2→
     3→Reference documentation for the Significa website project. For AI agent instructions, see `CLAUDE.md` in the project root.
     4→
     5→| File | What it covers |
     6→|------|---------------|
     7→| `01-PROJECT-BOOTSTRAP.md` | Stack, commands, project structure, key principles |
     8→| `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` | Content collections, Zod schemas, relationships, draft system |
     9→| `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` | Custom components for use inside MDX files |
    10→| `04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` | S3 + CDN pipeline, MediaImage/MediaVideo, image transform parameters |
    11→| `05-RULES.md` | Do's, don'ts, build-time validation, common mistakes |
    12→| `06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md` | Astro framework deep reference (components, collections, islands, transitions) |
    13→| `07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md` | Team discussion log and architectural decisions |
    14→| `08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` | CSS custom properties: spacing, typography, colors, breakpoints |
    15→| `09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` | SEO checklist and accessibility baseline |
    16→
    17→## Quick Reminders
    18→
    19→1. **Static site.** No server, no API, no database. HTML generated at build time.
    20→2. **Astro v5 (latest stable).** Astro 6 is in beta — upgrade when it reaches stable. Stay on latest v5 until then.
    21→3. **Content Collections are the database.** Use `getCollection()` and `getEntry()`. Never `import.meta.glob`.
    22→4. **References use `reference()`.** Build-time validated cross-collection links. Broken slugs break the build, not production.
    23→5. **Media via S3 + self-hosted CDN.** Uploaded via asset manager, served from `https://cdn.significa.co` with sharp-based image transforms.
    24→6. **Astro components first.** React only for interactive islands.
    25→7. **When unsure, read the Astro docs.** Do not guess. https://docs.astro.build

1→# Project Bootstrap
     2→
     3→## Stack
     4→
     5→- **Astro v5** (latest stable): static site generator, zero JS by default. Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs) — do not upgrade until stable.
     6→- **MDX**: markdown with custom components for case studies and rich content
     7→- **TypeScript**: strict mode, no `any`
     8→- **Content Collections**: Astro's built-in content layer with Zod schemas
     9→- **React**: only for interactive islands (mobile nav, form validation). Keep it minimal.
    10→- **pnpm**: package manager
    11→- **Cloudflare Pages**: static deploy target
    12→
    13→## Init
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→pnpm create astro@latest significa-website -- --template minimal --typescript strict
    17→cd significa-website
    18→pnpm astro add mdx react
    19→```
    20→
    21→## Project Structure
    22→
    23→```
    24→src/
    25→├── components/

    81→
    82→### Hybrid page architecture
    83→
    84→Some pages are hardcoded Astro files (`who-we-are.astro`, `what-we-do.astro`, `work-with-us.astro`) because they have complex layouts that don't fit the MDX pattern. Other pages (about, services, etc.) live as MDX in `src/content/pages/` and are rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.
    85→
    86→The reserved routes list in `[...slug].astro` prevents collisions between the two approaches.
    87→
    88→## Key Principles
    89→
    90→1. **No database.** Content lives in MDX/YAML files with typed frontmatter. Content Collections are the database.
    91→2. **No client-side JS by default.** Astro renders everything at build time. Only add `client:load` or `client:visible` to React components that genuinely need interactivity.
    92→3. **Media via S3 + self-hosted CDN.** Images and videos are uploaded through the internal asset manager to S3 and served via `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare → Fly.io transform service → S3) with sharp-based image optimization. See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`.
    93→4. **Relationships via `reference()`.** Collections reference each other using Astro's `reference()` for build-time validation. Broken slugs break the build, not production.
    94→5. **Drafts via `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. The shared `contentLoader()` excludes `*.draft.*` files in production builds at the glob level. In development, drafts are included for preview. Publishing is a file rename — no frontmatter field needed.
    95→6. **Keep it simple.** If you're reaching for a library, stop and check if plain HTML/CSS or an Astro component can do it.
    96→7. **Fail at build time.** The site is managed by marketing and non-technical people. Every error caught at build time is one less bug in production.
    97→8. **Centralized MDX registration.** All MDX components are registered in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`. Add new components there — all slug pages pick them up automatically.
    98→
    99→## Build & Deploy
   100→
   101→```bash
   102→pnpm dev          # Development server with HMR
   103→pnpm build        # Build static site to dist/
   104→pnpm preview      # Preview built site locally
   105→pnpm check        # TypeScript + content validation (astro check)
   106→pnpm lint         # Full lint pipeline: Prettier + type check + ESLint
   107→pnpm lint:check   # ESLint only
   108→pnpm lint:fix     # ESLint auto-fix
   109→pnpm format       # Prettier auto-format src/
   110→pnpm format:check # Prettier check (no write)
   111→```
   112→
   113→### CI Pipeline
   114→
   115→```bash
   116→pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   117→pnpm lint       # Prettier + type check + ESLint (catches all code quality issues)
   118→pnpm build      # Full build (catches MDX errors, broken references)
   119→```
   120→
   121→### Deploy
   122→
   123→Cloudflare Pages: push to main triggers build and deploy of `dist/`. Branch pushes create preview deployments.
   124→
   125→### Pre-Push Checklist
   126→
   127→Before pushing, run `pnpm lint && pnpm build` locally. This runs Prettier, type checking, and ESLint before building. Content errors caught locally are 10x faster than waiting for CI.
   128→

   132→- **Content Collections:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/content-collections/
   133→- **MDX in Astro:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/markdown-content/
   134→- **View Transitions:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/view-transitions/

   136→
   137→**Do not hallucinate APIs.** If you're unsure whether a method or config option exists, check the docs. Astro's API surface is small.

1→# Content Schema & Relationships
     2→
     3→Defines the "database": all content types, their fields, and how they relate to each other.
     4→
     5→## Schema Definition
     6→
     7→File: `src/content.config.ts` (at the root of `src/`, not inside `src/content/`)
     8→
     9→### Shared Schemas
    10→
    11→```ts
    12→import { defineCollection, reference, z } from "astro:content";
    13→import { glob, type Loader } from "astro/loaders";
    14→
    15→const seoSchema = z.object({
    16→  metaTitle: z.string().optional(),
    17→  metaDescription: z.string().optional(),
    18→  ogImage: z.string().optional(),
    19→});
    20→
    21→const metricSchema = z.object({
    22→  value: z.string(),
    23→  label: z.string(),
    24→});
    25→```
    26→
    27→### Content Loader
    28→
    29→All collections use a shared `contentLoader()` helper that centralizes glob patterns, ID generation, and **draft filtering**:
    30→
    31→```ts
    32→const contentLoader = ({ base, extensions }: { extensions: string[]; base: string }): Loader => {
    33→  const stripExtension = ({ entry }: { entry: string }) => {
    34→    return entry.replace(/\.(mdx?|yaml)$/, "");
    35→  };
    36→
    37→  // In development: include all files (drafts visible for preview)
    38→  // In production: exclude *.draft.* files at the glob level
    39→  const draftFilter = import.meta.env.MODE === "development" ? "**" : "!(*.draft)";
    40→  const extensionFilter = extensions.length > 1 ? `{${extensions.join(",")}}` : extensions[0];
    41→
    42→  return glob({
    43→    pattern: `**/${draftFilter}.${extensionFilter}`,
    44→    base,
    45→    generateId: stripExtension,
    46→  });
    47→};
    48→```
    49→
    50→This means:
    51→
    52→- **Development** (`pnpm dev`): all files are loaded, including `*.draft.*` files, so authors can preview drafts locally.
    53→- **Production** (`pnpm build`): files matching `*.draft.*` are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer.
    54→- **Publishing a draft** is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`.
    55→- **ID generation** strips file extensions for clean URL slugs: `example-project.mdx` → `example-project`.
    56→
    57→## Collections
    58→
    59→### Projects (MDX)
    60→
    61→Case studies. The richest content type.
    62→
    63→```ts
    64→const projects = defineCollection({
    65→  loader: contentLoader({
    66→    base: "src/content/projects",
    67→    extensions: ["mdx"],
    68→  }),
    69→  schema: z.object({
    70→    title: z.string(),
    71→    tagline: z.string().optional(),
    72→    client: z.string().optional(),
    73→    date: z.coerce.date(),
    74→    thumbnail: z.string(),
    75→    heroImage: z.string().optional(),
    76→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    77→    metrics: z.array(metricSchema).default([]),
    78→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
    79→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
    80→  }),
    81→});
    82→```
    83→
    84→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
    85→|---|---|---|---|
    86→| `title` | string | yes | Project name |
    87→| `tagline` | string | no | Short description for cards |
    88→| `client` | string | no | Client company name |
    89→| `date` | date | yes | Project date (used for sorting) |
    90→| `thumbnail` | string | yes | CDN path for listing cards |
    91→| `heroImage` | string | no | CDN path for showcase/detail header |
    92→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
    93→| `metrics` | metric[] | no | Key metrics shown on project cards (e.g. `{ value: "55%↑", label: "Conversion" }`) |
    94→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
    95→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Build-time validated slugs of related projects |
    96→
    97→### Blog (MD/MDX)
    98→
    99→Blog posts. Supports both `.md` (no components) and `.mdx` (with components).
   100→
   101→```ts
   102→const blog = defineCollection({
   103→  loader: contentLoader({
   104→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   105→    base: "src/content/blog",
   106→  }),
   107→  schema: z.object({
   108→    title: z.string(),
   109→    description: z.string().optional(),
   110→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   111→    author: z.string().optional(),
   112→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   113→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   114→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   115→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
   116→  }),
   117→});
   118→```
   119→
   120→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   121→|---|---|---|---|
   122→| `title` | string | yes | Post title |
   123→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and RSS. Falls back to title-based string if missing |
   124→| `date` | date | yes | Publication date |
   125→| `author` | string | no | Author name |
   126→| `thumbnail` | string | no | CDN path for listing cards |
   127→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
   128→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   129→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Cross-sell to project case studies |
   130→

   133→Open source projects and experiments.

   136→const labs = defineCollection({

   152→
   153→### Pages (MDX)
   154→
   155→Miscellaneous content pages (about, services, etc.) rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.
   156→
   157→```ts
   158→const pages = defineCollection({
   159→  loader: contentLoader({
   160→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   161→    base: "src/content/pages",
   162→  }),
   163→  schema: z.object({
   164→    title: z.string(),
   165→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   166→  }),

   169→
   170→### Highlights (YAML)
   171→
   172→Homepage curation. Each entry references an item in another collection using a discriminated union. Astro validates every slug at build time.
   173→
   174→```ts
   175→const highlights = defineCollection({
   176→  loader: contentLoader({
   177→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   178→    base: "src/content/highlights",
   179→  }),
   180→  schema: z.object({
   181→    label: z.string().optional(),
   182→    source: z.discriminatedUnion("collection", [
   183→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("projects"), entry: reference("projects") }),
   184→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("blog"), entry: reference("blog") }),
   185→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("labs"), entry: reference("labs") }),
   186→    ]),
   187→    order: z.number(),
   188→  }),
   189→});
   190→```
   191→
   192→Example YAML:
   193→
   194→```yaml
   195→# src/content/highlights/01-cool-project.yaml
   196→order: 1
   197→label: Featured Project
   198→source:
   199→  collection: projects
   200→  entry: example-project
   201→```
   202→
   203→If `example-project` doesn't exist in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails** with a clear error. If the referenced file is a draft (e.g. `example-project.draft.mdx`), it works in dev but **fails the production build** — preventing links to unpublished content.
   204→
   205→### Clients (YAML)
   206→
   207→Client logo strip and references across the site.
   208→

   210→const clients = defineCollection({
   211→  loader: contentLoader({

   215→  schema: z.object({

   224→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   225→|---|---|---|---|

   227→| `logo` | string | yes | Path to SVG logo in `public/` (e.g. `/logos/boeing.svg`) |
   228→| `url` | string | no | Client website URL |

   289→### Handbook Groups (YAML)
   290→
   291→Visual categories shown on the handbook index page. Each handbook page references one group.
   292→
   293→```ts
   294→const handbookGroups = defineCollection({
   295→  loader: contentLoader({
   296→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   297→    base: "src/content/handbook-groups",
   298→  }),
   299→  schema: z.object({
   300→    title: z.string(),
   301→    order: z.number(),
   302→  }),
