content.content:
1→# Content Schema & Relationships
     2→
     3→Defines the "database": all content types, their fields, and how they relate to each other.
     4→
     5→## Schema Definition
     6→
     7→File: `src/content.config.ts` (at the root of `src/`, not inside `src/content/`)
     8→
     9→### Shared Schemas
    10→
    11→```ts
    12→import { defineCollection, reference, z } from "astro:content";
    13→import { glob, type Loader } from "astro/loaders";
    14→
    15→const seoSchema = z.object({
    16→  metaTitle: z.string().optional(),
    17→  metaDescription: z.string().optional(),
    18→  ogImage: z.string().optional(),
    19→});
    20→
    21→const metricSchema = z.object({
    22→  value: z.string(),
    23→  label: z.string(),
    24→});
    25→```
    26→
    27→### Content Loader
    28→
    29→All collections use a shared `contentLoader()` helper that centralizes glob patterns, ID generation, and **draft filtering**:
    30→
    31→```ts
    32→const contentLoader = ({ base, extensions }: { extensions: string[]; base: string }): Loader => {
    33→  const stripExtension = ({ entry }: { entry: string }) => {
    34→    return entry.replace(/\.(mdx?|yaml)$/, "");
    35→  };
    36→
    37→  // In development: include all files (drafts visible for preview)
    38→  // In production: exclude *.draft.* files at the glob level
    39→  const draftFilter = import.meta.env.MODE === "development" ? "**" : "!(*.draft)";
    40→  const extensionFilter = extensions.length > 1 ? `{${extensions.join(",")}}` : extensions[0];
    41→
    42→  return glob({
    43→    pattern: `**/${draftFilter}.${extensionFilter}`,
    44→    base,
    45→    generateId: stripExtension,
    46→  });
    47→};
    48→```
    49→
    50→This means:
    51→
    52→- **Development** (`pnpm dev`): all files are loaded, including `*.draft.*` files, so authors can preview drafts locally.
    53→- **Production** (`pnpm build`): files matching `*.draft.*` are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer.
    54→- **Publishing a draft** is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`.
    55→- **ID generation** strips file extensions for clean URL slugs: `example-project.mdx` → `example-project`.
    56→
    57→## Collections
    58→
    59→### Projects (MDX)
    60→
    61→Case studies. The richest content type.
    62→
    63→```ts
    64→const projects = defineCollection({
    65→  loader: contentLoader({
    66→    base: "src/content/projects",
    67→    extensions: ["mdx"],
    68→  }),
    69→  schema: z.object({
    70→    title: z.string(),
    71→    tagline: z.string().optional(),
    72→    client: z.string().optional(),
    73→    date: z.coerce.date(),
    74→    thumbnail: z.string(),
    75→    heroImage: z.string().optional(),
    76→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    77→    metrics: z.array(metricSchema).default([]),
    78→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
    79→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
    80→  }),
    81→});
    82→```
    83→
    84→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
    85→|---|---|---|---|
    86→| `title` | string | yes | Project name |
    87→| `tagline` | string | no | Short description for cards |
    88→| `client` | string | no | Client company name |
    89→| `date` | date | yes | Project date (used for sorting) |
    90→| `thumbnail` | string | yes | CDN path for listing cards |
    91→| `heroImage` | string | no | CDN path for showcase/detail header |
    92→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
    93→| `metrics` | metric[] | no | Key metrics shown on project cards (e.g. `{ value: "55%↑", label: "Conversion" }`) |
    94→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
    95→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Build-time validated slugs of related projects |
    96→
    97→### Blog (MD/MDX)
    98→
    99→Blog posts. Supports both `.md` (no components) and `.mdx` (with components).
   100→
   101→```ts
   102→const blog = defineCollection({
   103→  loader: contentLoader({
   104→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   105→    base: "src/content/blog",
   106→  }),
   107→  schema: z.object({
   108→    title: z.string(),
   109→    description: z.string().optional(),
   110→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   111→    author: z.string().optional(),
   112→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   113→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   114→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   115→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
   116→  }),
   117→});
   118→```
   119→
   120→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   121→|---|---|---|---|
   122→| `title` | string | yes | Post title |
   123→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and RSS. Falls back to title-based string if missing |
   124→| `date` | date | yes | Publication date |
   125→| `author` | string | no | Author name |
   126→| `thumbnail` | string | no | CDN path for listing cards |
   127→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
   128→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   129→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Cross-sell to project case studies |
   130→
   131→### Labs (MD/MDX)
   132→
   133→Open source projects and experiments.
   134→
   135→```ts
   136→const labs = defineCollection({
   137→  loader: contentLoader({
   138→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   139→    base: "src/content/labs",
   140→  }),
   141→  schema: z.object({
   142→    title: z.string(),
   143→    description: z.string(),
   144→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   145→    repoUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
   146→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   147→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   148→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   149→  }),
   150→});
   151→```
   152→
   153→### Pages (MDX)
   154→
   155→Miscellaneous content pages (about, services, etc.) rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.
   156→
   157→```ts
   158→const pages = defineCollection({
   159→  loader: contentLoader({
   160→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   161→    base: "src/content/pages",
   162→  }),
   163→  schema: z.object({
   164→    title: z.string(),
   165→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   166→  }),
   167→});
   168→```
   169→
   170→### Highlights (YAML)
   171→
   172→Homepage curation. Each entry references an item in another collection using a discriminated union. Astro validates every slug at build time.
   173→
   174→```ts
   175→const highlights = defineCollection({
   176→  loader: contentLoader({
   177→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   178→    base: "src/content/highlights",
   179→  }),
   180→  schema: z.object({
   181→    label: z.string().optional(),
   182→    source: z.discriminatedUnion("collection", [
   183→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("projects"), entry: reference("projects") }),
   184→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("blog"), entry: reference("blog") }),
   185→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("labs"), entry: reference("labs") }),
   186→    ]),
   187→    order: z.number(),
   188→  }),
   189→});
   190→```
   191→
   192→Example YAML:
   193→
   194→```yaml
   195→# src/content/highlights/01-cool-project.yaml
   196→order: 1
   197→label: Featured Project
   198→source:
   199→  collection: projects
   200→  entry: example-project
   201→```
   202→
   203→If `example-project` doesn't exist in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails** with a clear error. If the referenced file is a draft (e.g. `example-project.draft.mdx`), it works in dev but **fails the production build** — preventing links to unpublished content.
   204→
   205→### Clients (YAML)
   206→
   207→Client logo strip and references across the site.
   208→
   209→```ts
   210→const clients = defineCollection({
   211→  loader: contentLoader({
   212→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   213→    base: "src/content/clients",
   214→  }),
   215→  schema: z.object({
   216→    name: z.string(),
   217→    logo: z.string(),
   218→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   219→    order: z.number().default(0),
   220→  }),
   221→});
   222→```
   223→
   224→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   225→|---|---|---|---|
   226→| `name` | string | yes | Client company name |
   227→| `logo` | string | yes | Path to SVG logo in `public/` (e.g. `/logos/boeing.svg`) |
   228→| `url` | string | no | Client website URL |
   229→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   230→
   231→### Testimonials (YAML)
   232→
   233→Customer quotes for social proof sections.
   234→
   235→```ts
   236→const testimonials = defineCollection({
   237→  loader: contentLoader({
   238→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   239→    base: "src/content/testimonials",
   240→  }),
   241→  schema: z.object({
   242→    quote: z.string(),
   243→    author: z.string(),
   244→    role: z.string(),
   245→    company: z.string(),
   246→    avatar: z.string().optional(),
   247→    order: z.number().default(0),
   248→  }),
   249→});
   250→```
   251→
   252→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   253→|---|---|---|---|
   254→| `quote` | string | yes | The testimonial text |
   255→| `author` | string | yes | Person's name |
   256→| `role` | string | yes | Job title |
   257→| `company` | string | yes | Company name |
   258→| `avatar` | string | no | CDN path to author photo |
   259→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   260→
   261→### Awards (YAML)
   262→
   263→Recognition and validation. Each award references a project.
   264→
   265→```ts
   266→const awards = defineCollection({
   267→  loader: contentLoader({
   268→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   269→    base: "src/content/awards",
   270→  }),
   271→  schema: z.object({
   272→    award: z.string(),
   273→    year: z.number(),
   274→    project: reference("projects"),
   275→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   276→    order: z.number().default(0),
   277→  }),
   278→});
   279→```
   280→
   281→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   282→|---|---|---|---|
   283→| `award` | string | yes | Award name (e.g. "Red Dot", "Awwwards SOTD") |
   284→| `year` | number | yes | Year awarded |
   285→| `project` | reference | yes | Project that won — build-time validated |
   286→| `url` | string | no | External link to award page |
   287→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   288→
   289→### Handbook Groups (YAML)
   290→
   291→Visual categories shown on the handbook index page. Each handbook page references one group.
   292→
   293→```ts
   294→const handbookGroups = defineCollection({
   295→  loader: contentLoader({
   296→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   297→    base: "src/content/handbook-groups",
   298→  }),
   299→  schema: z.object({
   300→    title: z.string(),
   301→    order: z.number(),
   302→  }),
   303→});
   304→```
   305→
   306→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   307→|---|---|---|---|
   308→| `title` | string | yes | Display name shown on the index page |
   309→| `order` | number | yes | Sort order of the group on the index page |
   310→
   311→Filenames act as slugs referenced by handbook pages (e.g. `engineering.yaml` → `group: engineering`).
   312→
   313→### Handbook (MDX)
   314→
   315→Internal company documentation. File path determines both the URL and the parent-child hierarchy.
   316→
   317→```ts
   318→const handbook = defineCollection({
   319→  loader: contentLoader({
   320→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   321→    base: "src/content/handbook",
   322→  }),
   323→  schema: z.object({
   324→    title: z.string(),
   325→    description: z.string().optional(),
   326→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   327→    group: reference("handbook-groups"),
   328→    order: z.number().default(0),
   329→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   330→  }),
   331→});
   332→```
   333→
   334→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   335→|---|---|---|---|
   336→| `title` | string | yes | Page title |
   337→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and child page listings |
   338→| `date` | date | yes | Last updated date |
   339→| `group` | reference | yes | Which group this page belongs to — build-time validated |
   340→| `order` | number | no | Sort order within the group on the index. Default `0` |
   341→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   342→
   343→**File structure determines hierarchy:**
   344→
   345→```
   346→src/content/handbook/
   347→├── culture.mdx                              → /handbook/culture          (top-level)
   348→├── how-we-build-software.mdx               → /handbook/how-we-build-software  (top-level, has children)
   349→└── how-we-build-software/
   350→    ├── front-end-development.mdx           → /handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development
   351→    └── back-end-development.mdx            → /handbook/how-we-build-software/back-end-development
   352→```
   353→
   354→- A page becomes a **parent** when a sibling directory shares its name and contains MDX files.
   355→- Children inherit their parent's URL as a prefix.
   356→- Nesting beyond one level is supported but should be used sparingly.
   357→- The `group` field is still required on child pages — it controls which group they appear under in sidebar navigation.
   358→
   359→## Export
   360→
   361→All collections must be exported from `src/content.config.ts`:
   362→
   363→```ts
   364→export const collections = {
   365→  projects,
   366→  blog,
   367→  labs,
   368→  pages,
   369→  highlights,
   370→  clients,
   371→  testimonials,
   372→  awards,
   373→  "handbook-groups": handbookGroups,
   374→  handbook,
   375→};
   376→```
   377→
   378→## Draft System
   379→
   380→Drafts are managed via the **`.draft` filename suffix**, not a frontmatter field.
   381→
   382→### How It Works
   383→
   384→| File | Dev (`pnpm dev`) | Prod (`pnpm build`) |
   385→|---|---|---|
   386→| `cool-project.mdx` | ✅ Loaded | ✅ Loaded |
   387→| `cool-project.draft.mdx` | ✅ Loaded (preview) | ❌ Excluded at glob level |
   388→
   389→### Workflow
   390→
   391→1. **Create a draft:** name it `my-post.draft.mdx` (or `.draft.md`, `.draft.yaml`)
   392→2. **Preview locally:** run `pnpm dev` — drafts are included in the content layer
   393→3. **Publish:** rename `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`
   394→4. **Unpublish:** rename `my-post.mdx` → `my-post.draft.mdx`
   395→
   396→### Important: References to Drafts
   397→
   398→If a non-draft entry (e.g. a highlight or award) references a draft entry:
   399→- **In dev:** works fine — the draft is in the collection
   400→- **In prod build:** fails with a build error — the draft is excluded, so the reference is broken
   401→
   402→This is intentional. It prevents shipping pages that link to unpublished content.
   403→
   404→## How Relationships Work
   405→
   406→All cross-content references use `reference()` from Astro for compile-time validation. `reference()` returns `{ id, collection }` objects, **not** full entries. You must resolve them with `getEntry()`.
   407→
   408→### Pattern 1: Related Projects in a Case Study
   409→
   410→Frontmatter in `src/content/projects/cool-project.mdx`:
   411→
   412→```yaml
   413→---
   414→title: Cool Project
   415→client: Acme Corp
   416→date: 2024-06-01
   417→thumbnail: /images/projects/cool-project/thumb.jpg
   418→relatedProjects:
   419→  - another-project
   420→  - third-project
   421→---
   422→```
   423→
   424→If `another-project` or `third-project` don't exist as files in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails immediately**.
   425→
   426→In the page template `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`:
   427→
   428→```astro
   429→---
   430→import { getCollection, getEntry, render } from "astro:content";
   431→import { mdxComponents } from "../../components/mdx/components";
   432→
   433→export async function getStaticPaths() {
   434→  const projects = await getCollection("projects");
   435→  return projects.map((project) => ({
   436→    params: { slug: project.id },
   437→    props: { project },
   438→  }));
   439→}
   440→
   441→const { project } = Astro.props;
   442→const { Content } = await render(project);
   443→
   444→// Resolve related projects
   445→const relatedProjects = project.data.relatedProjects?.length
   446→  ? await Promise.all(project.data.relatedProjects.map((ref) => getEntry(ref)))
   447→  : [];
   448→---
   449→
   450→<Content components={mdxComponents} />
   451→```
   452→
   453→### Pattern 2: Homepage Highlights (Cross-Collection)
   454→
   455→Each highlight references an entry in another collection via `discriminatedUnion`. Resolved in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→export async function getResolvedHighlights() {
   459→  const allHighlights = await getCollection("highlights");
   460→  const sorted = allHighlights.sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
   461→
   462→  const resolved = await Promise.all(
   463→    sorted.map(async (h) => {
   464→      const entry = await getEntry(
   465→        h.data.source.collection,
   466→        h.data.source.entry.id,
   467→      );
   468→      return {
   469→        label: h.data.label,
   470→        collection: h.data.source.collection,
   471→        entry,
   472→      };
   473→    }),
   474→  );
   475→
   476→  return resolved;
   477→}
   478→```
   479→
   480→### Pattern 3: Awards with Project References
   481→
   482→Awards reference projects, resolved in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
   483→
   484→```ts
   485→export async function getAwards() {
   486→  const allAwards = await getCollection("awards");
   487→  const sorted = allAwards.sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
   488→
   489→  const resolved = await Promise.all(
   490→    sorted.map(async (a) => {
   491→      const project = await getEntry("projects", a.data.project.id);
   492→      return {
   493→        award: a.data.award,
   494→        year: a.data.year,
   495→        url: a.data.url,
   496→        project,
   497→      };
   498→    }),
   499→  );
   500→
   501→  return resolved;
   502→}
   503→```
   504→
   505→### Pattern 4: MDX Cross-Sell Component
   506→
   507→Inside any `.mdx` file:
   508→
   509→```mdx
   510→<ProjectCrossSell slug="another-project" />
   511→```
   512→
   513→The component resolves the slug with `getEntry()` and throws at build time if not found.
   514→
   515→## Collection Helper Functions
   516→
   517→All query helpers live in `src/lib/collections.ts`. These handle sorting and reference resolution. **No draft filtering is needed** — drafts are already excluded by the content loader in production.
   518→
   519→| Function | Returns |
   520→|---|---|
   521→| `getProjects()` | All projects, sorted by date desc |
   522→| `getPosts()` | All blog posts, sorted by date desc |
   523→| `getLabs()` | All labs, sorted by date desc |
   524→| `getClients()` | All clients, sorted by order asc |
   525→| `getTestimonials()` | All testimonials, sorted by order asc |
   526→| `getResolvedHighlights()` | Highlights with referenced entries resolved |
   527→| `getAwards()` | Awards with referenced projects resolved |
   528→| `getHandbookGroups()` | All handbook groups, sorted by order asc |
   529→| `getHandbookPages()` | All handbook pages, sorted by order asc |
   530→| `getTopLevelHandbookPages()` | Handbook pages without a parent |
   531→| `getHandbookChildren(parentId)` | Direct children of a handbook page |
   532→| `getHandbookParent(entry)` | Parent entry for a handbook page, or null |
   533→| `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry)` | Ancestor chain from root to the current page |
   534→| `getHandbookIndex()` | Full grouped structure for the index page |
   535→
   536→## Important Notes
   537→
   538→- **`reference()` returns `{ id, collection }` objects**, not the full entry. Resolve with `getEntry(ref)`.
   539→- **Handbook parent-child hierarchy is file-path derived.** A page at `how-we-build-software/front-end.mdx` is automatically a child of `how-we-build-software.mdx`. No `parent` field in frontmatter — the relationship is implicit in the directory structure.
   540→- **Handbook groups use `reference("handbook-groups")`**, so a typo in `group:` breaks the build, not production.
   541→- **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** No frontmatter field, no query-time filtering. The content loader handles it automatically.
   542→- **Blog supports both `.md` and `.mdx`.** Blog posts that don't need custom components can use plain `.md`.
   543→- **YAML for structured data without prose.** Highlights, clients, testimonials, awards.
   544→- **Config file location:** `src/content.config.ts` (at root of `src/`, both this path and `src/content/content.config.ts` work in Astro v5+, but we use the root path).
   545→- **MDX components are centralized:** registered once in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`, used by all slug pages.

1→# Rules & Anti-Patterns 2→ 3→## DO 4→ 5→- **Use Astro components by default.** Only reach for React when you need client-side interactivity (e.g. mobile menu toggle, form validation). Even then, prefer `client:visible` over `client:load`. 6→- **Use semantic HTML.** `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, ``. Not everything is a `
`. 7→- **Fail at build time.** If a slug reference is broken, a required field is missing, or data is invalid: throw. The build breaks, not production. 8→- **Keep pages thin.** Pages (`src/pages/`) should fetch data and delegate rendering to layout/components. No complex logic in pages. 9→- **Use TypeScript everywhere.** Type your props with `interface Props`, type your data, type your utils. No `any`. 10→- **Use `reference()` for cross-collection links.** Build-time validated. Broken slugs break the build, not production. 11→- **Use `pnpm`.** Not npm, not yarn. 12→- **Use design tokens for colors and typography.** Colors and font sizes come from `tokens.css` custom properties. No hex colors, no arbitrary font sizes. Spacing uses raw values (4px grid guideline, `rem` for vertical, `px` for horizontal when appropriate). See `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`. 13→- **Use `MediaImage` for all images in content.** Never raw `` tags. It constructs CDN URLs with responsive `srcset` and optimization parameters automatically. 14→- **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. In production builds, `.draft` files are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer. In development (`pnpm dev`), drafts are included so authors can preview them locally. Publishing is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`. No frontmatter field needed. 15→- **Use kebab-case for all filenames.** Components, pages, utilities, styles, content — everything is kebab-case (`media-image.astro`, `content-errors.ts`, `global.css`). Enforced by ESLint via `eslint-plugin-check-file`. 16→ 17→## DO NOT 18→ 19→- **Do not use `getStaticPaths` for non-dynamic routes.** Only `[slug].astro` files need it. Static pages like `index.astro` call `getCollection()` directly. 20→- **Do not add a CSS framework.** Scoped `

    43→

    87→
    88→### Metrics
    89→
    90→Key numbers/stats display.
    91→
    92→```mdx
    93→<Metrics
    94→  items={[
    95→    { value: "3x", label: "Conversion rate increase" },
    96→    { value: "40%", label: "Faster load time" },
    97→    { value: "98", label: "Lighthouse score" },
    98→  ]}
    99→/>
   100→```
   101→
   102→### ProjectCrossSell

   113→
   114→**Components to consider adding (by priority):**
   115→
   116→| Component | Justification | Priority |
   117→|---|---|---|
   118→| `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have |
   119→| `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have |
   120→| `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have |
   121→| `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have |
   122→| `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have |
   123→| `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have |
   124→
   125→## Adding New MDX Components
   126→
   127→1. Create the `.astro` file in `src/components/mdx/`
   128→2. Define `interface Props` with TypeScript
   129→3. Import and add to the `mdxComponents` map in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`
   130→4. Add usage example to this documentation
   131→5. Test with `pnpm build` to verify
   132→
   133→All slug pages import from `components.ts`, so step 3 is the only wiring needed.
   134→
   135→## Rules for MDX Components
   136→
   137→1. **Astro components only.** No React unless the component needs client interactivity.
   138→2. **Throw on bad data.** Missing props or unresolved slugs must throw with a clear error message at build time.
   139→3. **No data fetching.** All data comes from props or content collections.
   140→4. **Keep them simple.** Render what they receive. Business logic stays in page templates.
   141→5. **Document the props interface.** Every component needs a clear `interface Props`.

file.content:
# MDX Components

Custom components used inside `.mdx` content files. These are **Astro components** (not React): they run at build time with zero client JS.

## Registering Components for MDX

All MDX components are registered in a single file: `src/components/mdx/components.ts`. Every `[slug].astro` page imports from there — no duplication.

```ts
// src/components/mdx/components.ts
import MediaImage from "./media-image.astro";
import MediaVideo from "./media-video.astro";
import ComparisonBlock from "./comparison-block.astro";
import Metrics from "./metrics.astro";
import ProjectCrossSell from "./project-cross-sell.astro";

export const mdxComponents = {
  MediaImage,
  MediaVideo,
  ComparisonBlock,
  Metrics,
  ProjectCrossSell,
};
```

In your `[slug].astro` page, import and pass:

```astro
---
import { mdxComponents } from "../../components/mdx/components";

const { Content } = await render(entry);
---

<Content components={mdxComponents} />
```

## Component Catalog

### MediaImage

Renders an optimized image via the CDN with responsive `srcset`. Always use this instead of raw `![alt](url)` for captions, sizing control, and automatic image optimization.

The `src` prop takes the asset path (as returned by the asset manager). The component prepends the CDN hostname (`https://cdn.significa.co`) and appends optimization query parameters automatically.

```mdx
<MediaImage
  src="/projects/cool-project/hero.jpg"
  alt="Dashboard overview"
  width={1200}
  height={630}
  caption="The main dashboard after redesign"
/>
```

**Required props:** `src`, `alt`, `width`, `height`
**Optional:** `caption`, `eager` (for above-the-fold images), `sizes` (responsive sizes attr), `quality` (0-100, default 80)

### MediaVideo

Embeds a video served via the CDN. The `poster` image goes through the same transform pipeline as `MediaImage`.

```mdx
<MediaVideo
  src="/projects/cool-project/demo.mp4"
  poster="/projects/cool-project/demo-poster.jpg"
  caption="Prototype walkthrough"
/>
```

### ComparisonBlock

Side-by-side before/after or A/B comparison.

```mdx
<ComparisonBlock
  before={{
    src: "/images/projects/x/before.jpg",
    label: "Before redesign"
  }}
  after={{
    src: "/images/projects/x/after.jpg",
    label: "After redesign"
  }}
/>
```

### Metrics

Key numbers/stats display.

```mdx
<Metrics
  items={[
    { value: "3x", label: "Conversion rate increase" },
    { value: "40%", label: "Faster load time" },
    { value: "98", label: "Lighthouse score" },
  ]}
/>
```

### ProjectCrossSell

Card linking to another project. See `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` for the implementation pattern.

```mdx
<ProjectCrossSell slug="another-project" />
```

## Component Growth Strategy

We start with 5 components. New components are added when content demands them, not eagerly. When a content author needs a pattern that doesn't exist, that's the signal to build it.

**Components to consider adding (by priority):**

| Component | Justification | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have |
| `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have |
| `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have |
| `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have |
| `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have |
| `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have |

## Adding New MDX Components

1. Create the `.astro` file in `src/components/mdx/`
2. Define `interface Props` with TypeScript
3. Import and add to the `mdxComponents` map in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`
4. Add usage example to this documentation
5. Test with `pnpm build` to verify

All slug pages import from `components.ts`, so step 3 is the only wiring needed.

## Rules for MDX Components

1. **Astro components only.** No React unless the component needs client interactivity.
2. **Throw on bad data.** Missing props or unresolved slugs must throw with a clear error message at build time.
3. **No data fetching.** All data comes from props or content collections.
4. **Keep them simple.** Render what they receive. Business logic stays in page templates.
5. **Document the props interface.** Every component needs a clear `interface Props`.

content.content: 1→# Media & Assets 2→ 3→## Architecture 4→ 5→Media files are managed through Significa's internal asset manager (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads files to **S3**. file.content: # Media & Assets ## Architecture Media files are managed through Significa's internal asset manager (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads files to **S3**. A self-hosted image transform service on **Fly.io** handles on-the-fly image optimization via **sharp**, fronted by **Cloudflare** for edge caching. - **Asset Manager** (`assets.significa.co`): uploads images/videos to S3, returns URLs - **CDN** (`cdn.significa.co`): Cloudflare edge cache → Fly.io transform service → S3 - **Image transforms**: sharp-based, triggered via URL query parameters Content authors reference media by their S3 path. The `MediaImage` component constructs optimized CDN URLs with transform parameters automatically. ``` Author uploads via asset manager -> File stored in S3 -> Referenced in MDX as path (e.g. /projects/cool-project/hero.jpg) -> MediaImage builds CDN URL with transforms -> Cloudflare edge serves cached version (or Fly transforms on cache miss) ``` ## CDN Hostname ``` https://cdn.significa.co ``` All media URLs are constructed by prepending this hostname to the asset path. ## Image Transform Service The transform service runs on Fly.io (`assets.significa.co`) with sharp. It transforms images on-the-fly via query parameters. Transformed images are cached in two layers: S3 (persistent, survives deploys) and Cloudflare edge (global, automatic). ### URL Pattern ``` https://cdn.significa.co/?width=800&quality=80&format=webp ``` ### Available Parameters | Parameter | Values | Default | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `width` | integer (px) | original | Resize to width, maintain aspect ratio | | `height` | integer (px) | original | Resize to height, maintain aspect ratio | | `quality` | 0-100 | 80 | Compression level | | `format` | webp, avif, jpeg, png | auto-negotiated | Force output format | When no `format` is specified, the service auto-negotiates from the `Accept` header: AVIF → WebP → original format. This gives every browser the best format it supports without clients needing to know. ### Caching **Layer 1 — S3 transform cache (persistent):** Transformed images are stored back to S3 under a `_transforms/` prefix, keyed by a hash of (original path + params). Sharp runs exactly once per unique combination. **Layer 2 — Cloudflare edge cache (global):** Responses include `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`. After the first request, Cloudflare serves directly from the edge — never hits Fly again until eviction. ### Common Transform Recipes ``` # Thumbnail (400px wide, optimized) ?width=400&quality=80 # Hero image (full width, WebP) ?width=1920&quality=85&format=webp # Square avatar (use CSS object-fit for cropping) ?width=200&quality=80 ``` ## Referencing Media in Content ### In frontmatter ```yaml thumbnail: /projects/cool-project/thumb.jpg ``` ### In MDX body ```mdx ``` The `MediaImage` component prepends the CDN hostname and appends optimization parameters automatically. Content authors only need to provide the asset path as returned by the asset manager. ## MediaImage Component Behavior `MediaImage` constructs responsive image URLs using the transform service: 1. Takes the `src` path (e.g. `/projects/cool-project/hero.jpg`) 2. Prepends the CDN hostname: `https://cdn.significa.co/projects/cool-project/hero.jpg` 3. Generates `srcset` with multiple widths for responsive loading 4. Appends `quality` and lets the service auto-negotiate format (WebP/AVIF when supported) Example output (without caption): ```html ``` When a `caption` is provided, the output wraps in `

content.content: 1→# Significa Website: Team Discussion & Exploration 2→ 3→> Senior team review of the Significa website rebuild plan. 4→> 4 agents, 4 perspectives, 1 synthesis. 5→> Date: 2026-02-09 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Participants 10→ 11→| Role | Focus | Experience | 12→|---|---|---| 13→| **Technical Architect** | Architecture, build, deploy, performance | 15+ years | 14→| **UX/UI Designer** | Storytelling, components, IA, interactions | 12+ years | 15→| **Marketing/Content Director** | Lead gen, SEO, workflow, content strategy | 15+ years | 16→| **Devil's Advocate** | Stress-testing every decision | 15+ years | 17→ 18→--- 19→ 20→## Consensus: The Plan Is Solid 21→ 22→All 4 participants agree: the technical foundation is well-designed. Astro, static output, Content Collections with Zod, MDX. No one recommended changing the framework or fundamental approach. 23→ 24→Verdict: **ship it, but fix these gaps first.** 25→ 26→--- 27→ 28→## Team Decisions (Post-Discussion) 29→ 30→After reviewing all 4 perspectives, the following decisions were made: 31→ 32→1. **Astro v6 (latest stable).** We go with the latest stable version to avoid near-future breaking changes. No pinning to v5. 33→2. **Media in `public/` for now.** External CDN/image service (ImageKit, Cloudinary, or similar with S3) will be evaluated later. Not blocking for development. 34→3. **No CMS. Marketing uses git + AI.** The marketing team will have the project set up locally. MDX is learnable. AI assistance fills any gaps. Good documentation and content templates are key. 35→4. **Components: start lean, grow with content.** The 5 starting components are sufficient. New components are added when content demands them, not eagerly pre-built. 36→5. **Conversion architecture evolves with data.** CTAs and conversion points will be designed and tested against real metrics. Marketing owns this. 37→6. **SEO infra from inception.** RSS, sitemap, canonical URLs, robots.txt, 404 page, and SEO component should be set up from the start. 38→7. **CSS token system: no magic values.** Tokens evolve with the design but the rule is absolute: no hardcoded pixels, no hex colors, no arbitrary spacing. Agents must follow this strictly. 39→8. **Slug collision check: included.** Maximize compile-time validations. `reference()` over plain slugs, always. Non-technical content editors need the build to catch their mistakes. 40→9. **`reference()` is non-negotiable.** The Astro lock-in is worth the build-time validation. Runtime errors are unacceptable for a site managed by non-developers. 41→ 42→--- 43→ 44→## 5 Critical Gaps (Unanimous or Near-Unanimous) 45→ 46→### 1. Media Pipeline Is Underspecified 47→ 48→**Raised by:** All 4 participants. 49→ 50→The plan says "CDN handles it" but doesn't define the actual pipeline. Cloudflare R2 is object storage, not an image CDN. 51→ 52→**What's missing:** 53→- URL patterns for responsive images (`srcset`, format=webp/avif) 54→- Upload workflow (Figma to CDN to MDX) 55→- `width` and `height` as required props on `MediaImage` (prevents CLS) 56→- `loading="eager"` + `fetchpriority="high"` for hero images (LCP) 57→- Cloudflare Image Resizing or Workers for transformation 58→- Local development experience with CDN images 59→ 60→**Tech Architect's solution:** 61→ 62→```astro 63→--- 64→// MediaImage with responsive images via Cloudflare Image Resizing 65→interface Props { 66→ src: string; 67→ alt: string; 68→ width: number; 69→ height: number; 70→ eager?: boolean; 71→ sizes?: string; 72→} 73→ 74→const { src, alt, width, height, eager = false, sizes = '100vw' } = Astro.props; 75→const cdnBase = 'https://media.significa.co'; 76→const transforms = (w: number) => 77→ `${cdnBase}/cdn-cgi/image/width=${w},format=auto,quality=80/${src}`; 78→--- 79→ 80→ 81→ 85→ 94→ 95→``` 96→ 97→**Recommendation:** Define the image pipeline before writing any content. Build a CLI tool or script that uploads to R2 and generates the MDX snippet. 98→ 99→**Team Decision (Updated):** Media is managed via the internal asset manager app (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads to S3. A self-hosted image transform service on Fly.io serves media through `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare edge cache → Fly.io → S3) with sharp-based image transforms (resize, quality, format conversion via URL query parameters). See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` for the full technical reference. 100→ 101→--- 102→ 103→### 2. Content Workflow Without CMS: Realistic But Limited 104→ 105→**Raised by:** Marketing, Devil's Advocate, Tech Architect. 106→ 107→Git + MDX works if 1-2 technical people control the content. The breaking point comes when non-technical team members need to publish. 108→ 109→**Devil's Advocate scenario:** 110→> Tuesday afternoon. A marketing manager wants to fix a typo. They need to: open the repo, find the .mdx file, edit without breaking YAML, commit to a branch, open a PR, wait for build, get it merged, wait for deployment. In a CMS: click, edit, save. 30 seconds. 111→ 112→**When it works:** 113→- 1-2 people write content AND are comfortable with git 114→- Publishing cadence is low (2-4 posts/month) 115→- No external contributors 116→ 117→**When it breaks:** 118→- Non-technical team members need to contribute 119→- Publishing frequency increases 120→- Blog posts sit in PRs because the dev who merges is busy 121→ 122→**Recommendation:** Don't add CMS now, but: 123→1. Create MDX skeleton templates for each content type (copy-paste-edit) 124→2. Document "create new blog post" in 5 steps 125→3. Assign one content owner (not "the team") 126→4. Document Decap CMS as the Phase 2 escape hatch (git-backed, zero lock-in) 127→ 128→**Marketing Director's phased approach:** 129→1. **Phase 1 (now):** File-based MDX. Ship the site. 130→2. **Phase 2 (6-12 months):** Decap CMS or Tina CMS (git-backed visual editor) 131→3. **Phase 3 (if needed):** Headless CMS (Sanity, Contentful, Payload) 132→ 133→**Team Decision:** No CMS. The marketing team will have the project set up on their computers with AI assistance. Changing markdown files is not rocket science. Even with some learning curve, the team should be sufficiently familiar within a week or two. AI fills any remaining gaps, given correct skills and documentation in-project. 134→ 135→--- 136→ 137→### 3. MDX Components Insufficient for Case Studies 138→ 139→**Raised by:** UX/UI (strongly), Marketing, Devil's Advocate. 140→ 141→5 components produce linear, monotonous case studies. The UX/UI Designer was unequivocal: the gap between "portfolio site" and "design studio experience" is in the component toolkit. 142→ 143→**Current 5 components:** 144→ 145→| Component | Verdict | 146→|---|---| 147→| MediaImage | Essential. Needs `size` variant and `eager` prop. | 148→| MediaVideo | Essential. Needs autoplay-on-scroll for showreels. | 149→| ComparisonBlock | Good. Limited use but high impact. | 150→| Metrics | Important. Needs careful design. | 151→| ProjectCrossSell | Necessary. Should support blog/labs too. | 152→ 153→**What a case study looks like with 5 components:** 154→``` 155→Title (markdown) 156→Intro paragraph (markdown) 157→ 158→More text (markdown) 159→ 160→ 161→ 162→Results paragraph (markdown) 163→ 164→``` 165→This is a **linear, single-column scroll**. No rhythm, no visual chapters, no surprise. 166→ 167→**What a compelling case study needs:** 168→``` 169→[Hero: full-bleed image/video, title overlay] 170→[Brief: 2-3 sentences, large type] 171→[Challenge with client quote] 172→[Process: timeline/steps] 173→[Design exploration: image grid] 174→[Key decision: callout block] 175→[Solution: full-bleed dark section with device frames] 176→[Details: comparison, color palette, type specimen] 177→[Results: metrics with context] 178→[Impact: client testimonial] 179→[Next: related project cross-sell] 180→``` 181→ 182→**Components to add (by priority):** 183→ 184→| Component | Justification | Priority | 185→|---|---|---| 186→| `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have | 187→| `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have | 188→| `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have | 189→| `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have | 190→| `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have | 191→| `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have | 192→| `ProcessTimeline` | Visualize design process | Should-have | 193→| `ContentCrossSell` | Generic version of ProjectCrossSell (blog/labs too) | Should-have | 194→| `ColorPalette` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | 195→| `TypeSpecimen` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | 196→| `Embed` | Figma prototypes, CodeSandbox | Nice-to-have | 197→ 198→**Devil's Advocate concern:** Every new component requires a code change and deployment. Document the component addition process so it's a 15-minute task, not a research project. 199→ 200→**UX/UI insight:** Consider using `.md` for blog posts (no custom components needed) and `.mdx` only for case studies. Saves MDX compilation overhead on content that doesn't need it. 201→ 202→**Team Decision:** The 5 starting components are a starting point. New components will be created as content needs arise. We won't eagerly build components that might not be needed. Start simple and lean, grow with the content. 203→ 204→--- 205→ 206→### 4. Conversion Architecture Is Nonexistent 207→ 208→**Raised by:** Marketing (strongly), UX/UI. 209→ 210→The plan has zero conversion points. After reading a brilliant case study, the visitor encounters nothing saying "let's work together." 211→ 212→**What's missing:** 213→- `CTA` component embeddable in MDX (headline, body, button, link) 214→- Contact form (Formspree, Netlify Forms, or similar) 215→- Newsletter signup (Buttondown, Resend) 216→- Contextual CTAs at the end of case studies and blog posts 217→- Strategic CTA placement on homepage 218→ 219→**Marketing Director's math:** 220→> 5,000 monthly visitors at 1% contact rate = 50 leads. With proper conversion architecture (CTAs, newsletter, social proof) = 2-3% = 100-150 leads. For a studio, that's predictable pipeline vs. relying on referrals. 221→ 222→**Recommended CTA placements:** 223→- **Case studies:** "Want results like these? Let's talk." at the bottom 224→- **Blog posts:** Newsletter signup mid-article and at bottom 225→- **Labs:** "Built by Significa. See our client work." crosslink 226→- **Homepage:** Primary CTA above the fold, secondary after work section 227→ 228→**Team Decision:** The final structure will have conversion points, but this is something to try and test against real data and metrics. Not a worry for the initial build. Marketing people own this and will iterate based on performance. 229→ 230→--- 231→ 232→### 5. SEO and Technical Infrastructure Missing 233→ 234→**Raised by:** Marketing, Tech Architect. 235→ 236→Elements required for launch day: 237→ 238→| Item | Status | Priority | Effort | 239→|---|---|---|---| 240→| RSS Feed (`@astrojs/rss`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 241→| Sitemap (`@astrojs/sitemap`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 242→| 404 page | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 243→| Canonical URLs | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 244→| Robots.txt | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | 245→| `` component (OG, Twitter, meta) | Partial | Must-have | Medium | 246→| JSON-LD structured data | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | 247→| Tag archive pages | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | 248→| View Transitions | Mentioned, not planned | Should-have | Medium | 249→| Pagefind (search) | Not mentioned | Nice-to-have | Low | 250→ 251→**Tech Architect's SEO component:** 252→ 253→```astro 254→--- 255→interface Props { 256→ title: string; 257→ description: string; 258→ ogImage?: string; 259→ type?: 'website' | 'article'; 260→ publishedAt?: Date; 261→} 262→ 263→const { 264→ title, 265→ description, 266→ ogImage = 'https://media.significa.co/og-default.jpg', 267→ type = 'website', 268→ publishedAt 269→} = Astro.props; 270→ 271→const canonicalURL = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site); 272→--- 273→ 274→{title} 275→ 276→ 277→ 278→ 279→ 280→ 281→ 282→ 283→ 284→ 285→ 286→{type === 'article' && publishedAt && ( 287→ 288→)} 289→``` 290→ 291→--- 292→ 293→## Secondary Gaps (Important but Not Blockers) 294→ 295→### Content Types Missing 296→ 297→**Team/People collection** (raised by UX/UI, Marketing): 298→- Name, role, photo, bio, social links 299→- Feeds into: author cards on blog, project credits, about page 300→- A design studio sells people. Without this, the studio feels faceless. 301→ 302→**Testimonials** as structured data (raised by Marketing, UX/UI): 303→- Client name, role, company, quote, associated project 304→- Embeddable in case studies AND aggregatable on homepage 305→- Single highest-ROI missing content type per Marketing Director 306→ 307→**Careers collection** (raised by Marketing): 308→- Title, department, location, type, description, requirements 309→- Enables Google Jobs structured data 310→- Static "careers page" is insufficient if attracting talent is a goal 311→ 312→### Analytics (Raised by Marketing, Tech) 313→ 314→Nothing mentioned in the plan. Unanimous recommendation: 315→- **Plausible or Fathom** (privacy-first, no GDPR cookie banner needed) 316→- **Google Search Console** (free, non-negotiable for SEO) 317→- Track: page views, traffic sources, conversions, content performance 318→ 319→### Version Pinning: Astro v5 Now, v6 When Stable 320→ 321→**Team Decision:** Use latest stable Astro. Currently on **v5** — Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs). Upgrade to v6 as soon as it reaches stable. Until then, keep dependencies up to date within the v5 range. 322→ 323→### CSS Token System (Raised by Devil's Advocate, UX/UI) 324→ 325→**Team Decision:** Confirmed needed. The token system will be documented and developed alongside the design. No magic numbers and no magic colors must be enforced by all agents. The system grows with the project but the rule is absolute from day one. 326→ 327→### Slug Collision Check (Raised by Tech Architect) 328→ 329→**Team Decision:** Include this. We should include as many compile-time validations as possible. A website managed by non-tech and marketing people should error as soon as possible instead of relying on runtime errors. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→const reserved = ['blog', 'projects', 'labs', 'index']; 333→for (const page of pages) { 334→ const topSegment = page.id.split('/')[0]; 335→ if (reserved.includes(topSegment)) { 336→ throw new Error(`Page "${page.id}" collides with reserved route /${topSegment}.`); 337→ } 338→} 339→``` 340→ 341→This philosophy extends to all content validation: use `reference()` instead of plain slugs, require fields that must exist, throw on missing data. Build-time errors are features, not annoyances. 342→ 343→--- 344→ 345→## Plan Strengths (Full Consensus) 346→ 347→What all 4 participants praised: 348→ 349→1. **Build-time validation** with `reference()` and Zod: broken references fail the build, not production 350→2. **Zero JS by default**: performance is excellent out of the box 351→3. **Highlights with discriminatedUnion**: elegant, extensible homepage curation 352→4. **Documentation quality**: clear, opinionated, well-organized (rare for a project plan) 353→5. **MDX components as Astro (not React)**: correct decision, zero client JS overhead 354→6. **Media on CDN, not in git**: repo stays lean, deploys stay fast 355→7. **Content Collections as the "database"**: typed, validated, portable content 356→ 357→--- 358→ 359→## Devil's Advocate: Report Card 360→ 361→| Aspect | Grade | Note | 362→|---|---|---| 363→| Framework choice (Astro) | B+ | Good fit, but version uncertainty is real | 364→| Content strategy (MDX in git) | B- | Works for dev teams, risky for mixed teams | 365→| Schema design | A- | Well-structured, good references and validation | 366→| Component architecture | B | Solid start, needs growth strategy | 367→| CSS approach | B- | Works at small scale, needs token system | 368→| Media strategy | C+ | Too vague. "CDN handles it" isn't a plan | 369→| Build/deploy | B | Fine for now, needs monitoring | 370→| Documentation | A- | Clear, opinionated, well-organized | 371→| Migration risk | B | Content is portable, glue code is not | 372→| Team scalability | C+ | Works for devs, walls off everyone else | 373→ 374→--- 375→ 376→## Interactions and Islands (UX/UI Perspective) 377→ 378→### Where Islands Are Required 379→1. **Mobile navigation**: `client:load` (immediate) 380→2. **ComparisonBlock slider**: `client:visible` (in viewport) 381→3. **Project filtering**: `client:load` (tag-based, immediate results) 382→4. **Image lightbox/zoom**: `client:visible` (portfolio detail viewing) 383→5. **Contact form**: `client:load` (validation + submission) 384→6. **Video player controls**: `client:visible` (CDN-hosted showreels) 385→ 386→### Where Islands Are NOT Needed 387→- Scroll animations: CSS `@scroll-timeline` or Intersection Observer via `

Create MDX skeleton templates for each content type (copy-paste-edit) 2. Document "create new blog post" in 5 steps 3. Assign one content owner (not "the team") 4. Document Decap CMS as the Phase 2 escape hatch (git-backed, zero lock-in) **Marketing Director's phased approach:** 1. **Phase 1 (now):** File-based MDX. Ship the site. 2. **Phase 2 (6-12 months):** Decap CMS or Tina CMS (git-backed visual editor) 3. **Phase 3 (if needed):** Headless CMS (Sanity, Contentful, Payload) **Team Decision:** No CMS. The marketing team will have the project set up on their computers with AI assistance. Changing markdown files is not rocket science. Even with some learning curve, the team should be sufficiently familiar within a week or two. AI fills any remaining gaps, given correct skills and documentation in-project. --- ### 3. MDX Components Insufficient for Case Studies **Raised by:** UX/UI (strongly), Marketing, Devil's Advocate. 5 components produce linear, monotonous case studies. The UX/UI Designer was unequivocal: the gap between "portfolio site" and "design studio experience" is in the component toolkit. **Current 5 components:** | Component | Verdict | |---|---| | MediaImage | Essential. Needs `size` variant and `eager` prop. | | MediaVideo | Essential. Needs autoplay-on-scroll for showreels. | | ComparisonBlock | Good. Limited use but high impact. | | Metrics | Important. Needs careful design. | | ProjectCrossSell | Necessary. Should support blog/labs too. | **What a case study looks like with 5 components:** ``` Title (markdown) Intro paragraph (markdown) More text (markdown) Results paragraph (markdown) ``` This is a **linear, single-column scroll**. No rhythm, no visual chapters, no surprise. **What a compelling case study needs:** ``` [Hero: full-bleed image/video, title overlay] [Brief: 2-3 sentences, large type] [Challenge with client quote] [Process: timeline/steps] [Design exploration: image grid] [Key decision: callout block] [Solution: full-bleed dark section with device frames] [Details: comparison, color palette, type specimen] [Results: metrics with context] [Impact: client testimonial] [Next: related project cross-sell] ``` **Components to add (by priority):** | Component | Justification | Priority | |---|---|---| | `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have | | `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have | | `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have | | `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have | | `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have | | `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have | | `ProcessTimeline` | Visualize design process | Should-have | | `ContentCrossSell` | Generic version of ProjectCrossSell (blog/labs too) | Should-have | | `ColorPalette` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | | `TypeSpecimen` | For branding projects | Nice-to-have | | `Embed` | Figma prototypes, CodeSandbox | Nice-to-have | **Devil's Advocate concern:** Every new component requires a code change and deployment. Document the component addition process so it's a 15-minute task, not a research project. **UX/UI insight:** Consider using `.md` for blog posts (no custom components needed) and `.mdx` only for case studies. Saves MDX compilation overhead on content that doesn't need it. **Team Decision:** The 5 starting components are a starting point. New components will be created as content needs arise. We won't eagerly build components that might not be needed. Start simple and lean, grow with the content. --- ### 4. Conversion Architecture Is Nonexistent **Raised by:** Marketing (strongly), UX/UI. The plan has zero conversion points. After reading a brilliant case study, the visitor encounters nothing saying "let's work together." **What's missing:** - `CTA` component embeddable in MDX (headline, body, button, link) - Contact form (Formspree, Netlify Forms, or similar) - Newsletter signup (Buttondown, Resend) - Contextual CTAs at the end of case studies and blog posts - Strategic CTA placement on homepage **Marketing Director's math:** > 5,000 monthly visitors at 1% contact rate = 50 leads. With proper conversion architecture (CTAs, newsletter, social proof) = 2-3% = 100-150 leads. For a studio, that's predictable pipeline vs. relying on referrals. **Recommended CTA placements:** - **Case studies:** "Want results like these? Let's talk." at the bottom - **Blog posts:** Newsletter signup mid-article and at bottom - **Labs:** "Built by Significa. See our client work." crosslink - **Homepage:** Primary CTA above the fold, secondary after work section **Team Decision:** The final structure will have conversion points, but this is something to try and test against real data and metrics. Not a worry for the initial build. Marketing people own this and will iterate based on performance. --- ### 5. SEO and Technical Infrastructure Missing **Raised by:** Marketing, Tech Architect. Elements required for launch day: | Item | Status | Priority | Effort | |---|---|---|---| | RSS Feed (`@astrojs/rss`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | Sitemap (`@astrojs/sitemap`) | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | 404 page | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | Canonical URLs | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | Robots.txt | Not mentioned | Must-have | Low | | `` component (OG, Twitter, meta) | Partial | Must-have | Medium | | JSON-LD structured data | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | | Tag archive pages | Not mentioned | Should-have | Medium | | View Transitions | Mentioned, not planned | Should-have | Medium | | Pagefind (search) | Not mentioned | Nice-to-have | Low | **Tech Architect's SEO component:** ```astro --- interface Props { title: string; description: string; ogImage?: string; type?: 'website' | 'article'; publishedAt?: Date; } const { title, description, ogImage = 'https://media.significa.co/og-default.jpg', type = 'website', publishedAt } = Astro.props; const canonicalURL = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site); --- {title} {type === 'article' && publishedAt && ( )} ``` --- ## Secondary Gaps (Important but Not Blockers) ### Content Types Missing **Team/People collection** (raised by UX/UI, Marketing): - Name, role, photo, bio, social links - Feeds into: author cards on blog, project credits, about page - A design studio sells people. Without this, the studio feels faceless. **Testimonials** as structured data (raised by Marketing, UX/UI): - Client name, role, company, quote, associated project - Embeddable in case studies AND aggregatable on homepage - Single highest-ROI missing content type per Marketing Director **Careers collection** (raised by Marketing): - Title, department, location, type, description, requirements - Enables Google Jobs structured data - Static "careers page" is insufficient if attracting talent is a goal ### Analytics (Raised by Marketing, Tech) Nothing mentioned in the plan. Unanimous recommendation: - **Plausible or Fathom** (privacy-first, no GDPR cookie banner needed) - **Google Search Console** (free, non-negotiable for SEO) - Track: page views, traffic sources, conversions, content performance ### Version Pinning: Astro v5 Now, v6 When Stable **Team Decision:** Use latest stable Astro. Currently on **v5** — Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs). Upgrade to v6 as soon as it reaches stable. Until then, keep dependencies up to date within the v5 range. ### CSS Token System (Raised by Devil's Advocate, UX/UI) **Team Decision:** Confirmed needed. The token system will be documented and developed alongside the design. No magic numbers and no magic colors must be enforced by all agents. The system grows with the project but the rule is absolute from day one. ### Slug Collision Check (Raised by Tech Architect) **Team Decision:** Include this. We should include as many compile-time validations as possible. A website managed by non-tech and marketing people should error as soon as possible instead of relying on runtime errors. ```typescript const reserved = ['blog', 'projects', 'labs', 'index']; for (const page of pages) { const topSegment = page.id.split('/')[0]; if (reserved.includes(topSegment)) { throw new Error(`Page "${page.id}" collides with reserved route /${topSegment}.`); } } ``` This philosophy extends to all content validation: use `reference()` instead of plain slugs, require fields that must exist, throw on missing data. Build-time errors are features, not annoyances. --- ## Plan Strengths (Full Consensus) What all 4 participants praised: 1. **Build-time validation** with `reference()` and Zod: broken references fail the build, not production 2. **Zero JS by default**: performance is excellent out of the box 3. **Highlights with discriminatedUnion**: elegant, extensible homepage curation 4. **Documentation quality**: clear, opinionated, well-organized (rare for a project plan) 5. **MDX components as Astro (not React)**: correct decision, zero client JS overhead 6. **Media on CDN, not in git**: repo stays lean, deploys stay fast 7. **Content Collections as the "database"**: typed, validated, portable content --- ## Devil's Advocate: Report Card | Aspect | Grade | Note | |---|---|---| | Framework choice (Astro) | B+ | Good fit, but version uncertainty is real | | Content strategy (MDX in git) | B- | Works for dev teams, risky for mixed teams | | Schema design | A- | Well-structured, good references and validation | | Component architecture | B | Solid start, needs growth strategy | | CSS approach | B- | Works at small scale, needs token system | | Media strategy | C+ | Too vague. "CDN handles it" isn't a plan | | Build/deploy | B | Fine for now, needs monitoring | | Documentation | A- | Clear, opinionated, well-organized | | Migration risk | B | Content is portable, glue code is not | | Team scalability | C+ | Works for devs, walls off everyone else | --- ## Interactions and Islands (UX/UI Perspective) ### Where Islands Are Required 1. **Mobile navigation**: `client:load` (immediate) 2. **ComparisonBlock slider**: `client:visible` (in viewport) 3. **Project filtering**: `client:load` (tag-based, immediate results) 4. **Image lightbox/zoom**: `client:visible` (portfolio detail viewing) 5. **Contact form**: `client:load` (validation + submission) 6. **Video player controls**: `client:visible` (CDN-hosted showreels) ### Where Islands Are NOT Needed - Scroll animations: CSS `@scroll-timeline` or Intersection Observer via `
