content.content:
1→# Parent-Child Page Structure
     2→
     3→This document explains how to create hierarchical page relationships in the handbook and pages collections.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## How It Works
     8→
     9→The parent-child structure is based on **file system hierarchy**. No frontmatter field is needed — the directory structure determines the relationship.
    10→
    11→### File System Rules
    12→
    13→```
    14→src/content/handbook/
    15→├── mission-and-values.mdx              → /handbook/mission-and-values (parent)
    16→└── mission-and-values/
    17→    ├── code-of-ethics.mdx              → /handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics (child)
    18→    └── diversity-and-inclusion.mdx     → /handbook/mission-and-values/diversity-and-inclusion (child)
    19→```
    20→
    21→**Key principle:** A page's ID is its file path (without extension). Children have IDs that start with their parent's ID + `/`.
    22→
    23→- Parent page: `mission-and-values` (from `mission-and-values.mdx`)
    24→- Child page: `mission-and-values/code-of-ethics` (from `mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`)
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Creating a Parent-Child Relationship
    29→
    30→### Step 1: Create the Parent Page
    31→
    32→Create a top-level `.mdx` file:
    33→
    34→```mdx
    35→---
    36→title: Mission and Values
    37→description: Our mission and the core values that guide everything we do.
    38→date: 2024-09-06
    39→group: knowing-significa
    40→order: 3
    41→---
    42→
    43→## Content of the parent page...
    44→```
    45→
    46→This becomes `/handbook/mission-and-values`.
    47→
    48→### Step 2: Create a Directory for Children
    49→
    50→Create a directory with the **exact same name** as the parent file (without extension):
    51→
    52→```bash
    53→mkdir src/content/handbook/mission-and-values
    54→```
    55→
    56→### Step 3: Add Child Pages
    57→
    58→Create `.mdx` files inside the directory:
    59→
    60→```mdx
    61→---
    62→title: Code of Ethics
    63→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
    64→date: 2024-01-15
    65→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
    66→group: knowing-significa
    67→order: 1
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Content of the child page...
    71→```
    72→
    73→This becomes `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`.
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## Helper Functions
    78→
    79→These functions in `src/lib/collections.ts` handle parent-child relationships:
    80→
    81→### `getHandbookChildren(parentId: string)`
    82→
    83→Returns all direct children of a parent page.
    84→
    85→```ts
    86→const children = await getHandbookChildren("mission-and-values");
    87→// Returns: [code-of-ethics, diversity-and-inclusion]
    88→```
    89→
    90→### `getHandbookParent(entry: HandbookEntry)`
    91→
    92→Returns the parent page of a child, or `null` for top-level pages.
    93→
    94→```ts
    95→const parent = await getHandbookParent(codeOfEthicsEntry);
    96→// Returns: mission-and-values entry
    97→```
    98→
    99→### `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry: HandbookEntry)`
   100→
   101→Returns the full breadcrumb trail from root to the current page (exclusive).
   102→
   103→```ts
   104→const breadcrumbs = await getHandbookBreadcrumbs(codeOfEthicsEntry);
   105→// Returns: [mission-and-values]
   106→```
   107→
   108→### `getHandbookIndex()`
   109→
   110→Returns the full grouped structure with children attached. Used by the index page.
   111→
   112→```ts
   113→const groups = await getHandbookIndex();
   114→// Returns: [
   115→//   {
   116→//     group: { id: "knowing-significa", data: { title: "Knowing Significa" } },
   117→//     pages: [
   118→//       {
   119→//         page: mission-and-values,
   120→//         children: [code-of-ethics, diversity-and-inclusion]
   121→//       }
   122→//     ]
   123→//   }
   124→// ]
   125→```
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→## UI Behavior
   130→
   131→### Index Page (`/handbook`)
   132→
   133→- Shows top-level pages grouped by `group`
   134→- If a parent has children, they appear indented below it
   135→- Clicking a parent or child navigates to that page
   136→
   137→### Detail Page (`/handbook/[...slug]`)
   138→
   139→**Parent pages:**
   140→- Show their content
   141→- Display an "In this section" box listing all children at the bottom
   142→
   143→**Child pages:**
   144→- Show breadcrumbs at the top (Handbook › Parent › Child)
   145→- Show their content
   146→- If they have children, display "In this section" box
   147→
   148→**Sidebar navigation:**
   149→- Shows full hierarchy with collapsible sections
   150→- Children only visible when parent is active or current
   151→- Active page highlighted in primary color
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## Nesting Depth
   156→
   157→The system supports **unlimited nesting depth**:
   158→
   159→```
   160→how-we-build-software.mdx                          (parent)
   161→how-we-build-software/
   162→  front-end-development.mdx                        (child of how-we-build-software)
   163→  front-end-development/
   164→    react-best-practices.mdx                       (child of front-end-development)
   165→    react-best-practices/
   166→      hooks-and-state-management.mdx               (child of react-best-practices)
   167→```
   168→
   169→However, **2-3 levels maximum is recommended** for usability. Deeper nesting becomes hard to navigate.
   170→
   171→---
   172→
   173→## Build-Time Validation
   174→
   175→The system validates relationships at build time:
   176→
   177→1. **No orphaned directories** — If `mission-and-values/` exists, `mission-and-values.mdx` must exist as the parent.
   178→2. **No ID collisions** — File names must be unique within their directory level.
   179→3. **Group references** — All pages must reference a valid `handbook-groups` entry (validated via `reference()`).
   180→
   181→If validation fails, **the build breaks**. This prevents broken links in production.
   182→
   183→---
   184→
   185→## Common Patterns
   186→
   187→### Creating a Section with Multiple Children
   188→
   189→```
   190→culture.mdx                      → /handbook/culture
   191→culture/
   192→  diversity.mdx                  → /handbook/culture/diversity
   193→  remote-work.mdx                → /handbook/culture/remote-work
   194→  team-rituals.mdx               → /handbook/culture/team-rituals
   195→```
   196→
   197→### Standalone Pages (No Children)
   198→
   199→Just create the `.mdx` file without a matching directory:
   200→
   201→```
   202→changelog.mdx                    → /handbook/changelog (no children)
   203→```
   204→
   205→### Adding Children to an Existing Page
   206→
   207→1. Create the directory: `mkdir src/content/handbook/existing-page`
   208→2. Add child files inside the directory
   209→3. The parent page automatically displays them in "In this section"
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Frontmatter Reference
   214→
   215→All pages use the same schema, whether parent or child:
   216→
   217→```yaml
   218→title: string                    # Required. Page title
   219→description: string              # Optional. Short description
   220→date: Date                       # Required. Last updated date (YYYY-MM-DD)
   221→coverImage: string               # Optional. Cover image (full URL)
   222→group: reference("handbook-groups")  # Required. Which group it belongs to
   223→order: number                    # Optional. Sort order within group (default: 0)
   224→seo:                             # Optional. SEO overrides
   225→  metaTitle: string
   226→  metaDescription: string
   227→  ogImage: string
   228→```
   229→
   230→**Note:** There is no `parent` field. The relationship is determined by file location.
   231→
   232→---
   233→
   234→## Extending to Other Collections
   235→
   236→This pattern can be applied to any collection. For example, if you wanted parent-child pages in the `pages` collection:
   237→
   238→1. Update `content.config.ts` to allow nested file structure
   239→2. Create helper functions similar to `getHandbookChildren()` in `src/lib/collections.ts`
   240→3. Update `src/pages/[...slug].astro` to handle children
   241→4. Add UI for displaying child pages
   242→
   243→The handbook implementation can serve as a reference.
   244→
   245→---
   246→
   247→## Examples
   248→
   249→### Example 1: Mission and Values → Code of Ethics
   250→
   251→**Parent:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values.mdx`
   252→- URL: `/handbook/mission-and-values`
   253→- Group: `knowing-significa`
   254→
   255→**Child:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
   256→- URL: `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`
   257→- Group: `knowing-significa` (must match parent's group)
   258→
   259→### Example 2: How We Build Software → Front-End Development
   260→
   261→**Parent:** `src/content/handbook/how-we-build-software.mdx`
   262→- URL: `/handbook/how-we-build-software`
   263→- Group: `engineering`
   264→
   265→**Child:** `src/content/handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development.mdx`
   266→- URL: `/handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development`
   267→- Group: `engineering` (must match parent's group)
   268→
   269→---
   270→
   271→## Gotchas
   272→
   273→1. **Directory name must match file name exactly** (case-sensitive, including hyphens)
   274→   - ✅ `mission-and-values.mdx` + `mission-and-values/`
   275→   - ❌ `mission-and-values.mdx` + `mission_and_values/`
   276→
   277→2. **Children must belong to the same group as their parent** — Otherwise they won't appear correctly in the navigation.
   278→
   279→3. **The `.draft` suffix works for parent and child pages** — `mission-and-values.draft.mdx` or `code-of-ethics.draft.mdx` are excluded in production.
   280→
   281→4. **Changing a parent's filename breaks children's URLs** — Renaming `mission-and-values.mdx` to `values.mdx` requires renaming the directory and all child IDs.
   282→
   283→5. **Cover images are served from CDN** — Use paths like `/handbook/my-image.jpg` which resolve to `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`. The system automatically generates responsive srcsets.
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## Cover Images
   288→
   289→You can add an optional cover image to any handbook page (parent or child).
   290→
   291→### Adding a Cover Image
   292→
   293→Add the `coverImage` field to your frontmatter with a full URL:
   294→
   295→```yaml
   296→---
   297→title: Code of Ethics
   298→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
   299→date: 2024-01-15
   300→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
   301→group: knowing-significa
   302→order: 1
   303→---
   304→```
   305→
   306→### URL Format
   307→
   308→The `coverImage` field accepts **full URLs**. You can use:
   309→
   310→- **CDN URLs:** `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`
   311→- **External URLs:** `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz`
   312→- **Any public image URL**
   313→
   314→The system handles all URL formats correctly:
   315→- CDN URLs get automatic optimization parameters
   316→- External URLs are used as-is
   317→- No need to worry about paths vs URLs
   318→
   319→### Image Guidelines
   320→
   321→- **URL format:** Full URL (e.g., `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`)
   322→- **Recommended dimensions:** 1200×630px or larger (16:9 or 2:1 aspect ratio)
   323→- **File format:** JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics with transparency
   324→- **File size:** Optimize before uploading (CDN images will be further optimized)
   325→- **Alt text:** Cover images are decorative, so no alt text is added
   326→
   327→### Display Behavior
   328→
   329→- Cover image appears between the page header and content
   330→- Full-width within the content area (max 840px on desktop)
   331→- Rounded corners (8px border-radius)
   332→- Responsive srcset generated automatically
   333→- Loaded eagerly with high priority (optimized for LCP)
   334→
   335→### Example
   336→
   337→**Code of Ethics page** with cover:
   338→```
   339→/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics
   340→```
   341→
   342→The cover image displays prominently at the top, setting the visual tone for the ethical guidelines content.
   343→
   344→### URL Examples
   345→
   346→```yaml
   347→# Significa CDN (recommended)
   348→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/ethics-cover.jpg
   349→
   350→# External service
   351→coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1234567890
   352→
   353→# Any public URL
   354→coverImage: https://example.com/images/cover.jpg
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→## Summary
   360→
   361→- **Parent-child relationships are defined by file structure**, not frontmatter.
   362→- **Create a directory matching the parent filename** to add children.
   363→- **Helper functions in `src/lib/collections.ts`** handle queries and navigation.
   364→- **The handbook implementation** is the reference for this pattern.
   365→- **Build-time validation** ensures no broken relationships make it to production.
   366→
   367→This approach keeps content management simple: just move files around. No need to update IDs or references manually.

file.content:
# Parent-Child Page Structure

This document explains how to create hierarchical page relationships in the handbook and pages collections.

---

## How It Works

The parent-child structure is based on **file system hierarchy**. No frontmatter field is needed — the directory structure determines the relationship.

### File System Rules

```
src/content/handbook/
├── mission-and-values.mdx              → /handbook/mission-and-values (parent)
└── mission-and-values/
    ├── code-of-ethics.mdx              → /handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics (child)
    └── diversity-and-inclusion.mdx     → /handbook/mission-and-values/diversity-and-inclusion (child)
```

**Key principle:** A page's ID is its file path (without extension). Children have IDs that start with their parent's ID + `/`.

- Parent page: `mission-and-values` (from `mission-and-values.mdx`)
- Child page: `mission-and-values/code-of-ethics` (from `mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`)

---

## Creating a Parent-Child Relationship

### Step 1: Create the Parent Page

Create a top-level `.mdx` file:

```mdx
---
title: Mission and Values
description: Our mission and the core values that guide everything we do.
date: 2024-09-06
group: knowing-significa
order: 3
---

## Content of the parent page...
```

This becomes `/handbook/mission-and-values`.

### Step 2: Create a Directory for Children

Create a directory with the **exact same name** as the parent file (without extension):

```bash
mkdir src/content/handbook/mission-and-values
```

### Step 3: Add Child Pages

Create `.mdx` files inside the directory:

```mdx
---
title: Code of Ethics
description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
date: 2024-01-15
coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
group: knowing-significa
order: 1
---

## Content of the child page...
```

This becomes `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`.

---

## Helper Functions

These functions in `src/lib/collections.ts` handle parent-child relationships:

### `getHandbookChildren(parentId: string)`

Returns all direct children of a parent page.

```ts
const children = await getHandbookChildren("mission-and-values");
// Returns: [code-of-ethics, diversity-and-inclusion]
```

### `getHandbookParent(entry: HandbookEntry)`

Returns the parent page of a child, or `null` for top-level pages.

```ts
const parent = await getHandbookParent(codeOfEthicsEntry);
// Returns: mission-and-values entry
```

### `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry: HandbookEntry)`

Returns the full breadcrumb trail from root to the current page (exclusive).

```ts
const breadcrumbs = await getHandbookBreadcrumbs(codeOfEthicsEntry);
// Returns: [mission-and-values]
```

### `getHandbookIndex()`

Returns the full grouped structure with children attached. Used by the index page.

```ts
const groups = await getHandbookIndex();
// Returns: [
//   {
//     group: { id: "knowing-significa", data: { title: "Knowing Significa" } },
//     pages: [
//       {
//         page: mission-and-values,
//         children: [code-of-ethics, diversity-and-inclusion]
//       }
//     ]
//   }
// ]
```

---

## UI Behavior

### Index Page (`/handbook`)

- Shows top-level pages grouped by `group`
- If a parent has children, they appear indented below it
- Clicking a parent or child navigates to that page

### Detail Page (`/handbook/[...slug]`)

**Parent pages:**
- Show their content
- Display an "In this section" box listing all children at the bottom

**Child pages:**
- Show breadcrumbs at the top (Handbook › Parent › Child)
- Show their content
- If they have children, display "In this section" box

**Sidebar navigation:**
- Shows full hierarchy with collapsible sections
- Children only visible when parent is active or current
- Active page highlighted in primary color

---

## Nesting Depth

The system supports **unlimited nesting depth**:

```
how-we-build-software.mdx                          (parent)
how-we-build-software/
  front-end-development.mdx                        (child of how-we-build-software)
  front-end-development/
    react-best-practices.mdx                       (child of front-end-development)
    react-best-practices/
      hooks-and-state-management.mdx               (child of react-best-practices)
```

However, **2-3 levels maximum is recommended** for usability. Deeper nesting becomes hard to navigate.

---

## Build-Time Validation

The system validates relationships at build time:

1. **No orphaned directories** — If `mission-and-values/` exists, `mission-and-values.mdx` must exist as the parent.
2. **No ID collisions** — File names must be unique within their directory level.
3. **Group references** — All pages must reference a valid `handbook-groups` entry (validated via `reference()`).

If validation fails, **the build breaks**. This prevents broken links in production.

---

## Common Patterns

### Creating a Section with Multiple Children

```
culture.mdx                      → /handbook/culture
culture/
  diversity.mdx                  → /handbook/culture/diversity
  remote-work.mdx                → /handbook/culture/remote-work
  team-rituals.mdx               → /handbook/culture/team-rituals
```

### Standalone Pages (No Children)

Just create the `.mdx` file without a matching directory:

```
changelog.mdx                    → /handbook/changelog (no children)
```

### Adding Children to an Existing Page

1. Create the directory: `mkdir src/content/handbook/existing-page`
2. Add child files inside the directory
3. The parent page automatically displays them in "In this section"

---

## Frontmatter Reference

All pages use the same schema, whether parent or child:

```yaml
title: string                    # Required. Page title
description: string              # Optional. Short description
date: Date                       # Required. Last updated date (YYYY-MM-DD)
coverImage: string               # Optional. Cover image (full URL)
group: reference("handbook-groups")  # Required. Which group it belongs to
order: number                    # Optional. Sort order within group (default: 0)
seo:                             # Optional. SEO overrides
  metaTitle: string
  metaDescription: string
  ogImage: string
```

**Note:** There is no `parent` field. The relationship is determined by file location.

---

## Extending to Other Collections

This pattern can be applied to any collection. For example, if you wanted parent-child pages in the `pages` collection:

1. Update `content.config.ts` to allow nested file structure
2. Create helper functions similar to `getHandbookChildren()` in `src/lib/collections.ts`
3. Update `src/pages/[...slug].astro` to handle children
4. Add UI for displaying child pages

The handbook implementation can serve as a reference.

---

## Examples

### Example 1: Mission and Values → Code of Ethics

**Parent:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values.mdx`
- URL: `/handbook/mission-and-values`
- Group: `knowing-significa`

**Child:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
- URL: `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`
- Group: `knowing-significa` (must match parent's group)

### Example 2: How We Build Software → Front-End Development

**Parent:** `src/content/handbook/how-we-build-software.mdx`
- URL: `/handbook/how-we-build-software`
- Group: `engineering`

**Child:** `src/content/handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development.mdx`
- URL: `/handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development`
- Group: `engineering` (must match parent's group)

---

## Gotchas

1. **Directory name must match file name exactly** (case-sensitive, including hyphens)
   - ✅ `mission-and-values.mdx` + `mission-and-values/`
   - ❌ `mission-and-values.mdx` + `mission_and_values/`

2. **Children must belong to the same group as their parent** — Otherwise they won't appear correctly in the navigation.

3. **The `.draft` suffix works for parent and child pages** — `mission-and-values.draft.mdx` or `code-of-ethics.draft.mdx` are excluded in production.

4. **Changing a parent's filename breaks children's URLs** — Renaming `mission-and-values.mdx` to `values.mdx` requires renaming the directory and all child IDs.

5. **Cover images are served from CDN** — Use paths like `/handbook/my-image.jpg` which resolve to `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`. The system automatically generates responsive srcsets.

---

## Cover Images

You can add an optional cover image to any handbook page (parent or child).

### Adding a Cover Image

Add the `coverImage` field to your frontmatter with a full URL:

```yaml
---
title: Code of Ethics
description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
date: 2024-01-15
coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
group: knowing-significa
order: 1
---
```

### URL Format

The `coverImage` field accepts **full URLs**. You can use:

- **CDN URLs:** `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`
- **External URLs:** `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz`
- **Any public image URL**

The system handles all URL formats correctly:
- CDN URLs get automatic optimization parameters
- External URLs are used as-is
- No need to worry about paths vs URLs

### Image Guidelines

- **URL format:** Full URL (e.g., `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/my-image.jpg`)
- **Recommended dimensions:** 1200×630px or larger (16:9 or 2:1 aspect ratio)
- **File format:** JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics with transparency
- **File size:** Optimize before uploading (CDN images will be further optimized)
- **Alt text:** Cover images are decorative, so no alt text is added

### Display Behavior

- Cover image appears between the page header and content
- Full-width within the content area (max 840px on desktop)
- Rounded corners (8px border-radius)
- Responsive srcset generated automatically
- Loaded eagerly with high priority (optimized for LCP)

### Example

**Code of Ethics page** with cover:
```
/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics
```

The cover image displays prominently at the top, setting the visual tone for the ethical guidelines content.

### URL Examples

```yaml
# Significa CDN (recommended)
coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/ethics-cover.jpg

# External service
coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1234567890

# Any public URL
coverImage: https://example.com/images/cover.jpg
```

---

## Summary

- **Parent-child relationships are defined by file structure**, not frontmatter.
- **Create a directory matching the parent filename** to add children.
- **Helper functions in `src/lib/collections.ts`** handle queries and navigation.
- **The handbook implementation** is the reference for this pattern.
- **Build-time validation** ensures no broken relationships make it to production.

This approach keeps content management simple: just move files around. No need to update IDs or references manually.

content.content:
1→# Blog Post Template & Guidelines
     2→
     3→This document explains the structure, content, and best practices for creating blog posts on the Significa website.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Quick Start
     8→
     9→1. Create a new `.mdx` or `.md` file in `src/content/blog/`
    10→2. Use kebab-case for the filename: `designing-complex-products.mdx`
    11→3. Copy the frontmatter template below
    12→4. Write your content using MDX components
    13→5. Add images via CDN (`https://cdn.significa.co/blog/...`)
    14→6. Reference related projects using `relatedProjects` array
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Frontmatter Template
    19→
    20→```yaml
    21→---
    22→title: Your Post Title Here
    23→description: A compelling one-sentence summary that appears in previews and meta descriptions
    24→date: 2026-01-22
    25→author: Author Name
    26→thumbnail: https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/hero.jpg
    27→tags:
    28→  - design
    29→  - guide
    30→  - case-study
    31→relatedProjects:
    32→  - project-slug
    33→seo:
    34→  metaTitle: Your Post Title — Significa
    35→  metaDescription: Extended description for search engines (150-160 characters)
    36→  ogImage: https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/og-image.jpg
    37→---
    38→```
    39→
    40→### Frontmatter Fields
    41→
    42→| Field | Required | Type | Description |
    43→|---|---|---|---|
    44→| `title` | Yes | string | Post title (50-60 characters ideal) |
    45→| `description` | No | string | One-sentence summary for previews |
    46→| `date` | Yes | date | Publication date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
    47→| `author` | No | string | Author's full name |
    48→| `thumbnail` | No | string | CDN path to hero image (16:9 ratio, 1600x900px) |
    49→| `tags` | No | array | Content categories (lowercase, hyphenated) |
    50→| `relatedProjects` | No | array | Project slugs (validated at build time) |
    51→| `seo.metaTitle` | No | string | Override default page title |
    52→| `seo.metaDescription` | No | string | Override default description |
    53→| `seo.ogImage` | No | string | Social sharing image (1200x630px) |
    54→
    55→### Common Tags
    56→
    57→- `design` - Design process, UI/UX, visual design
    58→- `development` - Engineering, code, technical
    59→- `strategy` - Product strategy, planning
    60→- `guide` - How-to articles, tutorials
    61→- `case-study` - Project retrospectives
    62→- `team` - Culture, hiring, team posts
    63→- `learning` - Lessons learned, insights
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## Content Structure
    68→
    69→### Opening Hook
    70→
    71→Start with a compelling hook that establishes context and creates curiosity:
    72→
    73→```mdx
    74→I recently designed an insurance management product. My knowledge of insurance? Zero.
    75→
    76→I could barely tell you the difference between premiums and brokers, let alone design a platform that finance professionals would use daily to reconcile thousands of payments. But here's the thing: designers rarely start projects as domain experts, and we don't need to be.
    77→```
    78→
    79→**Why this works:**
    80→- Vulnerable opening (relatable)
    81→- Specific problem stated
    82→- Promise of insight/solution
    83→
    84→### Project Cross-Sell (Optional)
    85→
    86→Link to a related project early in the post:
    87→
    88→```mdx
    89→<ProjectCrossSell
    90→  project="mishmash"
    91→  title="Streamlining insurance payments"
    92→  description="Before you uncover all the insights from this article, discover the project behind it."
    93→/>
    94→```
    95→
    96→**Best practices:**
    97→- Place after intro, before first H2
    98→- Use when post relates to a specific project
    99→- Keep description focused on the project, not the post
   100→
   101→### Body Structure
   102→
   103→Use clear hierarchy with H2 and H3 headings:
   104→
   105→```mdx
   106→## Main Section Heading
   107→
   108→Opening paragraph for the section.
   109→
   110→### Subsection (if needed)
   111→
   112→More specific content.
   113→```
   114→
   115→**Heading guidelines:**
   116→- H2 for main sections (actionable, clear)
   117→- H3 for subsections (rare, use sparingly)
   118→- No H4 or deeper (simplify instead)
   119→- Keep headings short (3-7 words)
   120→- Use sentence case, not title case
   121→
   122→### Visual Content
   123→
   124→Use MDX components to add rich media:
   125→
   126→#### Images
   127→
   128→```mdx
   129→<MediaImage
   130→  src="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/image-name.jpg"
   131→  alt="Descriptive alt text for accessibility"
   132→  width={1260}
   133→  height={840}
   134→  caption="Optional caption explaining the image"
   135→/>
   136→```
   137→
   138→**Image specifications:**
   139→- Format: JPG for photos, PNG for screenshots/diagrams
   140→- Max width: 1600px (for retina displays)
   141→- Aspect ratio: 3:2 or 16:9 preferred
   142→- File size: <500KB per image
   143→- CDN path: `https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg`
   144→
   145→#### Video
   146→
   147→```mdx
   148→<MediaVideo
   149→  src="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/video-name.mp4"
   150→  width={1260}
   151→  height={840}
   152→  poster="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/video-poster.jpg"
   153→  caption="Optional video caption"
   154→/>
   155→```
   156→
   157→#### Comparison Blocks
   158→
   159→```mdx
   160→<ComparisonBlock
   161→  before={{
   162→    src: "https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/before.jpg",
   163→    label: "Before — Immediate confirmation dialog",
   164→  }}
   165→  after={{
   166→    src: "https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/after.jpg",
   167→    label: "After — Undo toast with 10s grace period",
   168→  }}
   169→/>
   170→```
   171→
   172→**Use for:**
   173→- Before/after redesigns
   174→- Problem/solution comparisons
   175→- Old vs new approach
   176→
   177→#### Metrics
   178→
   179→```mdx
   180→<Metrics
   181→  items={[
   182→    { value: "+12%", label: "Engagement increase" },
   183→    { value: "3x", label: "Faster task completion" },
   184→    { value: "−28%", label: "Support tickets reduced" },
   185→  ]}
   186→/>
   187→```
   188→
   189→**Best practices:**
   190→- 3-5 metrics maximum
   191→- Use real data when possible
   192→- Include context in labels
   193→- Format consistently (+/−/x)
   194→
   195→### Quotes
   196→
   197→Use blockquotes for testimonials or key insights:
   198→
   199→```mdx
   200→> "From the outside, it might not seem like the most exciting area, but I was genuinely surprised by how intricate and intellectually stimulating it is. Insurance payments involve a huge amount of nuance, edge cases, and interconnected rules."
   201→>
   202→> **Julieta Frade** — Front-end developer
   203→```
   204→
   205→**Formatting:**
   206→- Use `>` for blockquotes
   207→- Add attribution on a new line
   208→- Bold the name with `**Name**`
   209→- Use em dash (—) before role/title
   210→
   211→### Lists
   212→
   213→Use lists for clarity:
   214→
   215→**Bullet lists:**
   216→```mdx
   217→- **Choose what to "buy"** → Select which premiums to process
   218→- **Review your cart** → Double-check selections
   219→- **Complete checkout** → Process the payout
   220→```
   221→
   222→**Numbered lists:**
   223→```mdx
   224→1. Create a domain model early
   225→2. Run structured workshops
   226→3. Keep feedback continuous
   227→4. Validate with real users
   228→```
   229→
   230→**Best practices:**
   231→- Bold key terms at the start
   232→- Keep items parallel in structure
   233→- Use 3-7 items (never just 2)
   234→- Add context after em dash (→) if needed
   235→
   236→### Closing
   237→
   238→End with a clear takeaway:
   239→
   240→```mdx
   241→## Lessons learned.
   242→
   243→Every project is a crash course in something new. You don't need to become an industry expert to design a great product, but you do need to collaborate effectively, ask the right questions, simplify complexity when you can, and validate your assumptions with real users.
   244→
   245→The best work doesn't come from designers working in isolation or clients dictating solutions. They come from genuine collaboration where both sides bring what they know best and build something better together.
   246→
   247→That's what made this project work… and that's what I'll carry into the next unfamiliar industry I encounter.
   248→```
   249→
   250→**Why this works:**
   251→- Summarizes key insights
   252→- Actionable takeaway
   253→- Personal reflection
   254→- Forward-looking
   255→
   256→---
   257→
   258→## Writing Style Guide
   259→
   260→### Voice & Tone
   261→
   262→- **Conversational but professional** - Write like you're explaining to a colleague
   263→- **First person for personal posts** - "I designed..." not "We designed..."
   264→- **Active voice** - "We tested the prototype" not "The prototype was tested"
   265→- **Specific over vague** - "Reduced support tickets by 28%" not "Improved efficiency"
   266→
   267→### Sentence Structure
   268→
   269→- **Vary sentence length** - Mix short punchy sentences with longer explanatory ones
   270→- **One idea per sentence** - Complex ideas deserve their own space
   271→- **Start strong** - Put the main point early in the sentence
   272→- **Cut filler words** - "really", "very", "just", "actually" rarely add value
   273→
   274→### Paragraphs
   275→
   276→- **2-4 sentences per paragraph** - Easier to scan online
   277→- **One idea per paragraph** - New point = new paragraph
   278→- **Add breathing room** - White space improves readability
   279→
   280→### Common Mistakes
   281→
   282→❌ **Don't:**
   283→- Start sentences with "So..."
   284→- Overuse em dashes (—) for dramatic effect
   285→- Write in abstract generalities
   286→- Use corporate jargon
   287→- Apologize for opinions ("In my humble opinion...")
   288→
   289→✅ **Do:**
   290→- Use concrete examples
   291→- Show your work (screenshots, diagrams)
   292→- Tell stories
   293→- Share failures alongside wins
   294→- Link to related work
   295→
   296→---
   297→
   298→## Technical Considerations
   299→
   300→### MDX vs MD
   301→
   302→- Use `.mdx` if you need MDX components (MediaImage, Metrics, etc.)
   303→- Use `.md` for simple text-only posts
   304→- Both support frontmatter and markdown syntax
   305→
   306→### Links
   307→
   308→**Internal links:**
   309→```mdx
   310→[related project](/projects/mishmash)
   311→[our services](/services)
   312→```
   313→
   314→**External links:**
   315→```mdx
   316→[external resource](https://example.com)
   317→```
   318→
   319→**Project references:**
   320→```yaml
   321→relatedProjects:
   322→  - mishmash
   323→  - planit-app
   324→```
   325→
   326→Build validates these at compile time. Typos break the build.
   327→
   328→### Images via CDN
   329→
   330→All images must be served from `https://cdn.significa.co`:
   331→
   332→```
   333→https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg
   334→```
   335→
   336→**Folder structure:**
   337→```
   338→cdn.significa.co/
   339→└── blog/
   340→    └── designing-complex-products/
   341→        ├── hero.jpg
   342→        ├── london-workshop.jpg
   343→        ├── domain-model.jpg
   344→        └── og-image.jpg
   345→```
   346→
   347→**Image optimization:**
   348→- Use CDN's automatic resizing
   349→- Specify `width` and `height` attributes
   350→- Always include descriptive `alt` text
   351→- Use `loading="lazy"` for below-fold images
   352→
   353→### Reading Time
   354→
   355→Automatically calculated based on word count (200 words per minute).
   356→
   357→### URL Structure
   358→
   359→Posts live at `/blog/{filename-without-extension}`:
   360→
   361→```
   362→src/content/blog/designing-complex-products.mdx
   363→→ https://significa.co/blog/designing-complex-products
   364→```
   365→
   366→---
   367→
   368→## SEO Best Practices
   369→
   370→### Title Optimization
   371→
   372→- 50-60 characters (display limit in search)
   373→- Include primary keyword
   374→- Front-load important words
   375→- Add brand suffix: "— Significa"
   376→
   377→### Description
   378→
   379→- 150-160 characters (search result snippet)
   380→- Include secondary keywords
   381→- Add call-to-action
   382→- Match article tone
   383→
   384→### OG Image
   385→
   386→Social sharing image specifications:
   387→
   388→- Size: 1200x630px (Facebook/LinkedIn ratio)
   389→- Format: JPG (better compression)
   390→- Text overlay: Large, readable from thumbnail
   391→- Include Significa branding
   392→
   393→### Internal Linking
   394→
   395→- Link to 2-3 related projects or posts
   396→- Use descriptive anchor text
   397→- Add related projects in frontmatter
   398→- Link naturally within content
   399→
   400→---
   401→
   402→## Publishing Checklist
   403→
   404→Before publishing, verify:
   405→
   406→- [ ] Frontmatter complete and valid
   407→- [ ] All images uploaded to CDN
   408→- [ ] Alt text added to all images
   409→- [ ] Links work (no 404s)
   410→- [ ] Related projects valid
   411→- [ ] Tags appropriate
   412→- [ ] Spelling and grammar checked
   413→- [ ] Code examples formatted
   414→- [ ] Build passes (`pnpm build`)
   415→- [ ] Preview looks correct
   416→
   417→---
   418→
   419→## Example Post Structures
   420→
   421→### Case Study Post
   422→
   423→1. Opening hook (the challenge)
   424→2. Project context
   425→3. ProjectCrossSell component
   426→4. Main insight #1 with visuals
   427→5. Main insight #2 with visuals
   428→6. Main insight #3 with visuals
   429→7. Results/metrics
   430→8. Lessons learned
   431→9. Related projects (automatic footer)
   432→
   433→### Guide/Tutorial Post
   434→
   435→1. Opening hook (why this matters)
   436→2. Problem statement
   437→3. Step 1 with examples
   438→4. Step 2 with examples
   439→5. Step 3 with examples
   440→6. Common mistakes section
   441→7. Conclusion with takeaway
   442→8. Related resources
   443→
   444→### Team/Culture Post
   445→
   446→1. Personal anecdote opening
   447→2. Context/background
   448→3. Main story/insight
   449→4. Supporting examples
   450→5. Broader lesson
   451→6. Personal reflection
   452→7. Call to action
   453→
   454→---
   455→
   456→## Getting Help
   457→
   458→- **Content questions:** Ask the content team
   459→- **Technical issues:** Check `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`
   460→- **Design tokens:** See `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`
   461→- **SEO guidelines:** Review `docs/09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md`
   462→
   463→---
   464→
   465→## Real Example
   466→
   467→See `src/content/blog/designing-complex-products.mdx` for a complete, production-ready example that demonstrates:
   468→
   469→- Effective frontmatter structure
   470→- Compelling opening hook
   471→- Strategic use of MDX components
   472→- Clear heading hierarchy
   473→- Quotes and testimonials
   474→- Strong closing with takeaways
   475→- Related project references

# Blog Post Template & Guidelines

This document explains the structure, content, and best practices for creating blog posts on the Significa website.

---

## Quick Start

1. Create a new `.mdx` or `.md` file in `src/content/blog/`
2. Use kebab-case for the filename: `designing-complex-products.mdx`
3. Copy the frontmatter template below
4. Write your content using MDX components
5. Add images via CDN (`https://cdn.significa.co/blog/...`)
6. Reference related projects using `relatedProjects` array

---

## Frontmatter Template

```yaml
---
title: Your Post Title Here
description: A compelling one-sentence summary that appears in previews and meta descriptions
date: 2026-01-22
author: Author Name
thumbnail: https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/hero.jpg
tags:
  - design
  - guide
  - case-study
relatedProjects:
  - project-slug
seo:
  metaTitle: Your Post Title — Significa
  metaDescription: Extended description for search engines (150-160 characters)
  ogImage: https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/og-image.jpg
---
```

### Frontmatter Fields

| Field | Required | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | Yes | string | Post title (50-60 characters ideal) |
| `description` | No | string | One-sentence summary for previews |
| `date` | Yes | date | Publication date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| `author` | No | string | Author's full name |
| `thumbnail` | No | string | CDN path to hero image (16:9 ratio, 1600x900px) |
| `tags` | No | array | Content categories (lowercase, hyphenated) |
| `relatedProjects` | No | array | Project slugs (validated at build time) |
| `seo.metaTitle` | No | string | Override default page title |
| `seo.metaDescription` | No | string | Override default description |
| `seo.ogImage` | No | string | Social sharing image (1200x630px) |

### Common Tags

- `design` - Design process, UI/UX, visual design
- `development` - Engineering, code, technical
- `strategy` - Product strategy, planning
- `guide` - How-to articles, tutorials
- `case-study` - Project retrospectives
- `team` - Culture, hiring, team posts
- `learning` - Lessons learned, insights

---

## Content Structure

### Opening Hook

Start with a compelling hook that establishes context and creates curiosity:

```mdx
I recently designed an insurance management product. My knowledge of insurance? Zero.

I could barely tell you the difference between premiums and brokers, let alone design a platform that finance professionals would use daily to reconcile thousands of payments. But here's the thing: designers rarely start projects as domain experts, and we don't need to be.
```

**Why this works:**
- Vulnerable opening (relatable)
- Specific problem stated
- Promise of insight/solution

### Project Cross-Sell (Optional)

Link to a related project early in the post:

```mdx
<ProjectCrossSell
  project="mishmash"
  title="Streamlining insurance payments"
  description="Before you uncover all the insights from this article, discover the project behind it."
/>
```

**Best practices:**
- Place after intro, before first H2
- Use when post relates to a specific project
- Keep description focused on the project, not the post

### Body Structure

Use clear hierarchy with H2 and H3 headings:

```mdx
## Main Section Heading

Opening paragraph for the section.

### Subsection (if needed)

More specific content.
```

**Heading guidelines:**
- H2 for main sections (actionable, clear)
- H3 for subsections (rare, use sparingly)
- No H4 or deeper (simplify instead)
- Keep headings short (3-7 words)
- Use sentence case, not title case

### Visual Content

Use MDX components to add rich media:

#### Images

```mdx
<MediaImage
  src="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/image-name.jpg"
  alt="Descriptive alt text for accessibility"
  width={1260}
  height={840}
  caption="Optional caption explaining the image"
/>
```

**Image specifications:**
- Format: JPG for photos, PNG for screenshots/diagrams
- Max width: 1600px (for retina displays)
- Aspect ratio: 3:2 or 16:9 preferred
- File size: <500KB per image
- CDN path: `https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg`

#### Video

```mdx
<MediaVideo
  src="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/video-name.mp4"
  width={1260}
  height={840}
  poster="https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/video-poster.jpg"
  caption="Optional video caption"
/>
```

#### Comparison Blocks

```mdx
<ComparisonBlock
  before={{
    src: "https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/before.jpg",
    label: "Before — Immediate confirmation dialog",
  }}
  after={{
    src: "https://cdn.significa.co/blog/your-post/after.jpg",
    label: "After — Undo toast with 10s grace period",
  }}
/>
```

**Use for:**
- Before/after redesigns
- Problem/solution comparisons
- Old vs new approach

#### Metrics

```mdx
<Metrics
  items={[
    { value: "+12%", label: "Engagement increase" },
    { value: "3x", label: "Faster task completion" },
    { value: "−28%", label: "Support tickets reduced" },
  ]}
/>
```

**Best practices:**
- 3-5 metrics maximum
- Use real data when possible
- Include context in labels
- Format consistently (+/−/x)

### Quotes

Use blockquotes for testimonials or key insights:

```mdx
> "From the outside, it might not seem like the most exciting area, but I was genuinely surprised by how intricate and intellectually stimulating it is. Insurance payments involve a huge amount of nuance, edge cases, and interconnected rules."
>
> **Julieta Frade** — Front-end developer
```

**Formatting:**
- Use `>` for blockquotes
- Add attribution on a new line
- Bold the name with `**Name**`
- Use em dash (—) before role/title

### Lists

Use lists for clarity:

**Bullet lists:**
```mdx
- **Choose what to "buy"** → Select which premiums to process
- **Review your cart** → Double-check selections
- **Complete checkout** → Process the payout
```

**Numbered lists:**
```mdx
1. Create a domain model early
2. Run structured workshops
3. Keep feedback continuous
4. Validate with real users
```

**Best practices:**
- Bold key terms at the start
- Keep items parallel in structure
- Use 3-7 items (never just 2)
- Add context after em dash (→) if needed

### Closing

End with a clear takeaway:

```mdx
## Lessons learned.

Every project is a crash course in something new. You don't need to become an industry expert to design a great product, but you do need to collaborate effectively, ask the right questions, simplify complexity when you can, and validate your assumptions with real users.

The best work doesn't come from designers working in isolation or clients dictating solutions. They come from genuine collaboration where both sides bring what they know best and build something better together.

That's what made this project work… and that's what I'll carry into the next unfamiliar industry I encounter.
```

**Why this works:**
- Summarizes key insights
- Actionable takeaway
- Personal reflection
- Forward-looking

---

## Writing Style Guide

### Voice & Tone

- **Conversational but professional** - Write like you're explaining to a colleague
- **First person for personal posts** - "I designed..." not "We designed..."
- **Active voice** - "We tested the prototype" not "The prototype was tested"
- **Specific over vague** - "Reduced support tickets by 28%" not "Improved efficiency"

### Sentence Structure

- **Vary sentence length** - Mix short punchy sentences with longer explanatory ones
- **One idea per sentence** - Complex ideas deserve their own space
- **Start strong** - Put the main point early in the sentence
- **Cut filler words** - "really", "very", "just", "actually" rarely add value

### Paragraphs

- **2-4 sentences per paragraph** - Easier to scan online
- **One idea per paragraph** - New point = new paragraph
- **Add breathing room** - White space improves readability

### Common Mistakes

❌ **Don't:**
- Start sentences with "So..."
- Overuse em dashes (—) for dramatic effect
- Write in abstract generalities
- Use corporate jargon
- Apologize for opinions ("In my humble opinion...")

✅ **Do:**
- Use concrete examples
- Show your work (screenshots, diagrams)
- Tell stories
- Share failures alongside wins
- Link to related work

---

## Technical Considerations

### MDX vs MD

- Use `.mdx` if you need MDX components (MediaImage, Metrics, etc.)
- Use `.md` for simple text-only posts
- Both support frontmatter and markdown syntax

### Links

**Internal links:**
```mdx
[related project](/projects/mishmash)
[our services](/services)
```

**External links:**
```mdx
[external resource](https://example.com)
```

**Project references:**
```yaml
relatedProjects:
  - mishmash
  - planit-app
```

Build validates these at compile time. Typos break the build.

### Images via CDN

All images must be served from `https://cdn.significa.co`:

```
https://cdn.significa.co/blog/{post-slug}/{image-name}.jpg
```

**Folder structure:**
```
cdn.significa.co/
└── blog/
    └── designing-complex-products/
        ├── hero.jpg
        ├── london-workshop.jpg
        ├── domain-model.jpg
        └── og-image.jpg
```

**Image optimization:**
- Use CDN's automatic resizing
- Specify `width` and `height` attributes
- Always include descriptive `alt` text
- Use `loading="lazy"` for below-fold images

### Reading Time

Automatically calculated based on word count (200 words per minute).

### URL Structure

Posts live at `/blog/{filename-without-extension}`:

```
src/content/blog/designing-complex-products.mdx
→ https://significa.co/blog/designing-complex-products
```

---

## SEO Best Practices

### Title Optimization

- 50-60 characters (display limit in search)
- Include primary keyword
- Front-load important words
- Add brand suffix: "— Significa"

### Description

- 150-160 characters (search result snippet)
- Include secondary keywords
- Add call-to-action
- Match article tone

### OG Image

Social sharing image specifications:

- Size: 1200x630px (Facebook/LinkedIn ratio)
- Format: JPG (better compression)
- Text overlay: Large, readable from thumbnail
- Include Significa branding

### Internal Linking

- Link to 2-3 related projects or posts
- Use descriptive anchor text
- Add related projects in frontmatter
- Link naturally within content

---

## Publishing Checklist

Before publishing, verify:

- [ ] Frontmatter complete and valid
- [ ] All images uploaded to CDN
- [ ] Alt text added to all images
- [ ] Links work (no 404s)
- [ ] Related projects valid
- [ ] Tags appropriate
- [ ] Spelling and grammar checked
- [ ] Code examples formatted
- [ ] Build passes (`pnpm build`)
- [ ] Preview looks correct

---

## Example Post Structures

### Case Study Post

1. Opening hook (the challenge)
2. Project context
3. ProjectCrossSell component
4. Main insight #1 with visuals
5. Main insight #2 with visuals
6. Main insight #3 with visuals
7. Results/metrics
8. Lessons learned
9. Related projects (automatic footer)

### Guide/Tutorial Post

1. Opening hook (why this matters)
2. Problem statement
3. Step 1 with examples
4. Step 2 with examples
5. Step 3 with examples
6. Common mistakes section
7. Conclusion with takeaway
8. Related resources

### Team/Culture Post

1. Personal anecdote opening
2. Context/background
3. Main story/insight
4. Supporting examples
5. Broader lesson
6. Personal reflection
7. Call to action

---

## Getting Help

- **Content questions:** Ask the content team
- **Technical issues:** Check `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`
- **Design tokens:** See `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`
- **SEO guidelines:** Review `docs/09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md`

---

## Real Example

See `src/content/blog/designing-complex-products.mdx` for a complete, production-ready example that demonstrates:

- Effective frontmatter structure
- Compelling opening hook
- Strategic use of MDX components
- Clear heading hierarchy
- Quotes and testimonials
- Strong closing with takeaways
- Related project references

Animate appearance 214→ 215→**Progressive enhancement:** 216→- Works without JavaScript (all posts visible) 217→- Enhances experience when JS available 218→- Respects View Transitions API 219→ 220→**Code:** 221→```javascript 222→function initTagFilter() { 223→ const buttons = document.querySelectorAll(".tag-button"); `, ``, `

To change: 460→ 461→```astro 462→// Example: Feature specific post 463→const featuredPost = allPosts.find(p => p.data.featured); 464→ 465→// Example: Feature random post 466→const featuredPost = allPosts[Math.floor(Math.random() * allPosts.length)]; 467→``` 468→ 469→### Add More Filters 470→ 471→Beyond tags, you could add: 472→ 473→```astro 474→// Author filter 475→const authors = [...new Set(allPosts.map(p => p.data.author))]; 476→ 477→// Year filter 478→const years = [...new Set(allPosts.map(p => p.data.date.getFullYear()))]; 479→ 480→// Category filter (if you add categories) 481→const categories = [...new Set(allPosts.flatMap(p => p.data.categories))]; 482→``` 483→ 484→### Modify Card Layout 485→ 486→Change grid columns: 487→ 488→```css 489→/* 4 columns on large screens */ 490→grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr)); 491→ 492→/* 2 columns max */ 493→grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(400px, 1fr)); 494→``` 495→ 496→### Add Pagination 497→ 498→For many posts: 499→ 500→```astro 501→const POSTS_PER_PAGE = 12; 502→const page = Number(Astro.url.searchParams.get('page')) || 1; 503→const paginatedPosts = recentPosts.slice( 504→ (page - 1) * POSTS_PER_PAGE, 505→ page * POSTS_PER_PAGE 506→); 507→``` 508→ 509→--- 510→ 511→## Best Practices 512→ 513→### Image Specifications 514→ 515→**Featured post:** 516→- Size: 1200x675px (16:9) 517→- Format: JPG 518→- Quality: 85% 519→- Loading: eager 520→ 521→**Grid cards:** 522→- Size: 600x400px (3:2) 523→- Format: JPG 524→- Quality: 80% 525→- Loading: lazy 526→ 527→### Tag Naming 528→ 529→- **lowercase** - "design" not "Design" 530→- **hyphenated** - "case-study" not "case study" 531→- **specific** - "ui-design" not just "design" 532→- **consistent** - Use same tags across posts 533→ 534→### Writing Card Content 535→ 536→**Titles:** 537→- 50-70 characters ideal 538→- Front-load important words 539→- No clickbait 540→ 541→**Descriptions:** 542→- 100-150 characters 543→- Complete sentence 544→- Entice without spoiling 545→ 546→--- 547→ 548→## Analytics Recommendations 549→ 550→Track these events: 551→ 552→1. **Featured post clicks** - Measure effectiveness 553→2. **Tag filter usage** - Which topics are popular 554→3. **Card clicks** - Which posts get attention 555→4. **Scroll depth** - How far users browse 556→5. **Newsletter CTA clicks** - Conversion rate 557→ 558→--- 559→ 560→## Future Enhancements 561→ 562→Consider adding: 563→ 564→1. **Search functionality** - Full-text search 565→2. **Load more button** - Instead of pagination 566→3. **Related posts** - "You might also like..." 567→4. **Author pages** - Filter by author 568→5. **RSS feed** - Already exists at `/blog/rss.xml` 569→6. **Reading progress** - Show how much user has read 570→7. **Share buttons** - Social sharing 571→8. **Bookmarking** - Save for later 572→9. **Comments** - Discussion system 573→10. **View count** - Popular posts indicator 574→ 575→--- 576→ 577→## Troubleshooting 578→ 579→### Tags not filtering 580→ 581→Check: 582→1. Posts have `tags` in frontmatter 583→2. JavaScript is enabled 584→3. `data-tags` attribute is valid JSON 585→4. Console for errors 586→ 587→### Cards have different heights 588→ 589→Ensure: 590→1. `.card-link { height: 100%; }` 591→2. `.card-description { flex-grow: 1; }` 592→3. `.card-tags { margin-top: auto; }` 593→ 594→### Featured post not showing 595→ 596→Verify: 597→1. At least one post exists 598→2. Post has all required fields 599→3. Array is not empty: `allPosts.length > 0` 600→ 601→### Images not loading 602→ 603→Check: 604→1. CDN path is correct 605→2. Image exists at path 606→3. CDN service is running 607→4. No CORS issues 608→ 609→--- 610→ 611→## Related Documentation 612→ 613→- **Blog post template:** `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` 614→- **MDX components:** `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` 615→- **Design tokens:** `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` 616→- **SEO guidelines:** `docs/09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` 617→ 618→--- 619→ 620→This blog index provides a modern, performant, and user-friendly way to discover and filter blog content while maintaining the design system and accessibility standards.

# Blog Index Page Documentation This document explains the structure, features, and implementation of the blog index page at `/blog`. --- ## Overview The blog index page serves as the main entry point for all blog content. It features: - **Hero section** with headline and description - **Featured post** (most recent) with large visual treatment - **Tag filtering** (client-side JavaScript enhancement) - **Blog grid** with card-based layout - **Newsletter CTA** for engagement --- ## Page Structure ### 1. Blog Header ```astro

We think things through.

Insights on design, development, and building products...

``` **Purpose:** - Sets the tone and context - Clearly communicates what the blog is about - Simple, centered layout **Styling:** - Large heading (--text-4xl) - Centered text - Generous padding (8rem top) - Neutral background --- ### 2. Featured Post The most recent blog post gets special treatment: **Features:** - Large two-column layout (image + content) - "Featured" label badge - Date and reading time - Full description - Tag badges - Hover effects **Layout:** - Desktop: 1fr 1fr grid (50/50 split) - Tablet/Mobile: Stacks vertically - Image on left, content on right (desktop) **Visual treatment:** - Larger font sizes - Border on hover - Image zoom on hover - Clear hierarchy --- ### 3. Tag Filter Client-side filtering system for blog posts: **Features:** - "All" button (default active) - Individual tag buttons - Active state styling - Smooth fade-in animation when filtering **How it works:** 1. Extract all unique tags from posts 2. Render as buttons 3. JavaScript listens for clicks 4. Filters cards by matching `data-tags` attribute 5. Animates filtered results **Implementation:** ```javascript // Cards have data-tags attribute
// Filter on button click if (selectedTag === "all" || cardTags.includes(selectedTag)) { card.style.display = ""; } else { card.style.display = "none"; } ``` --- ### 4. Blog Grid Card-based layout for all non-featured posts: **Features:** - Responsive grid (auto-fill, minmax 320px) - Thumbnail image (600x400px) - Date and reading time - Title and description - Tag badges - Hover effects **Card anatomy:** ``` ┌─────────────────┐ │ │ │ Image │ │ │ ├─────────────────┤ │ Date • 5 min │ │ │ │ Post Title │ │ │ │ Description... │ │ │ │ [tag] [tag] │ └─────────────────┘ ``` **Behavior:** - Border color change on hover - Image scales 1.05x on hover - Entire card is clickable - Smooth transitions --- ### 5. Newsletter CTA Bottom section encouraging engagement: **Features:** - Centered layout - Clear headline and description - Primary CTA button - Alternative background color **Can be customized to:** - Link to newsletter signup - Link to RSS feed - Link to contact form - Any engagement action --- ## Data Processing ### Post Fetching ```astro const allPosts = await getPosts(); // Sorted by date desc const featuredPost = allPosts[0]; // Most recent const recentPosts = allPosts.slice(1); // Rest ``` ### Tag Extraction ```astro const allTags = [ ...new Set(allPosts.flatMap((post) => post.data.tags || [])) ].sort(); ``` **Result:** Unique, sorted array of all tags used across posts. ### Reading Time Calculation ```typescript function calculateReadingTime(post): number { const wordCount = post.body?.split(/\s+/).length ?? 0; return wordCount > 0 ? Math.ceil(wordCount / 200) : 5; } ``` **Formula:** Word count ÷ 200 words per minute (industry standard). ### Date Formatting ```typescript function formatDate(date: Date): string { return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric", }); } ``` **Output:** "January 22, 2026" --- ## JavaScript Functionality ### Tag Filtering **Purpose:** Filter posts without page reload. **How it works:** 1. Store tags in `data-tags` attribute as JSON 2. Button click updates active state 3. Parse each card's tags 4. Show/hide based on match 5. Animate appearance **Progressive enhancement:** - Works without JavaScript (all posts visible) - Enhances experience when JS available - Respects View Transitions API **Code:** ```javascript function initTagFilter() { const buttons = document.querySelectorAll(".tag-button"); const cards = document.querySelectorAll(".blog-card"); buttons.forEach((button) => { button.addEventListener("click", () => { const selectedTag = button.dataset.tag; // Update active state buttons.forEach((btn) => btn.classList.remove("active")); button.classList.add("active"); // Filter cards cards.forEach((card) => { const cardTags = JSON.parse(card.dataset.tags); if (selectedTag === "all" || cardTags.includes(selectedTag)) { card.style.display = ""; card.style.animation = "fadeIn 0.3s ease-out"; } else { card.style.display = "none"; } }); }); }); } // Run on load and after navigation initTagFilter(); document.addEventListener("astro:after-swap", initTagFilter); ``` --- ## Styling Details ### Layout Widths - **Header/CTA:** `--max-width-narrow` (centered, readable) - **Featured post:** `--max-width` (wider for visual impact) - **Blog grid:** `--max-width` (wider for cards) ### Grid Behavior ```css .blog-grid-inner { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr)); gap: 2rem; } ``` **Result:** - Desktop: 3 columns - Tablet: 2 columns - Mobile: 1 column - Automatic, responsive ### Card Heights Cards have flexible height: - `.card-link { height: 100%; }` - Fill parent - `.card-content { flex-grow: 1; }` - Expand content - `.card-description { flex-grow: 1; }` - Push tags to bottom - `.card-tags { margin-top: auto; }` - Stick to bottom **Result:** Aligned cards even with varying content lengths. ### Color Usage - **Surface:** Main background (white/dark) - **Surface-alt:** Alternating sections, cards - **Border:** Subtle borders on cards, tags - **Text:** Primary content - **Text-muted:** Secondary info (dates, descriptions) All from design token system. --- ## Responsive Design ### Breakpoints **Featured Post (968px):** ```css @media (max-width: 968px) { .featured-link { grid-template-columns: 1fr; /* Stack vertically */ } .featured-image { height: 300px; /* Fixed height for stacked layout */ } } ``` **Mobile (768px):** ```css @media (max-width: 768px) { .blog-title { font-size: var(--text-3xl); /* Smaller heading */ } } ``` **Grid:** - Auto-adjusts based on `minmax(320px, 1fr)` - No explicit breakpoints needed - Fluid, responsive by default --- ## SEO Optimization ### Page Metadata ```astro ``` ### Structured Content - Semantic HTML (`
`, ``, `
`) - Proper heading hierarchy (H1 → H2) - Alt text on all images - Descriptive link text ### Internal Linking - Each card links to full post - Related posts visible in grid - Tag-based organization - Clear navigation --- ## Performance Considerations ### Image Loading **Featured post:** ```astro loading="eager" // Above fold, load immediately ``` **Grid cards:** ```astro loading="lazy" // Below fold, lazy load ``` ### CDN Usage All images served via CDN with optimization: ```astro src={cdnUrl(post.data.thumbnail, { width: 600, quality: 80 })} ``` **Benefits:** - Automatic resizing - Format optimization - Caching - Fast delivery ### JavaScript - **Minimal:** ~40 lines for filtering - **Progressive:** Works without JS - **Efficient:** Event delegation, no loops - **Cached:** Static, changes rarely --- ## Accessibility ### Keyboard Navigation - All interactive elements focusable - Clear focus states - Logical tab order ### Screen Readers - Semantic HTML throughout - `` elements with `datetime` attribute - Alt text on all images - Clear link purposes ### Color Contrast - Text meets WCAG AA standards - Sufficient contrast ratios - No color-only indicators --- ## Content Requirements ### For Posts to Appear Correctly **Required:** - `title` - Display in cards - `date` - Sorting and display **Recommended:** - `thumbnail` - Visual interest (600x400px minimum) - `description` - Preview text - `tags` - Categorization and filtering - `author` - Attribution **Optional:** - `seo.*` - Override defaults ### Missing Content Handling **No thumbnail:** - Card still displays with placeholder background - Content fills available space - No broken image **No description:** - Card shows title and meta only - Still looks complete **No tags:** - No tag badges shown - Card still functional --- ## Customization Guide ### Change Featured Post Logic Currently shows most recent. To change: ```astro // Example: Feature specific post const featuredPost = allPosts.find(p => p.data.featured); // Example: Feature random post const featuredPost = allPosts[Math.floor(Math.random() * allPosts.length)]; ``` ### Add More Filters Beyond tags, you could add: ```astro // Author filter const authors = [...new Set(allPosts.map(p => p.data.author))]; // Year filter const years = [...new Set(allPosts.map(p => p.data.date.getFullYear()))]; // Category filter (if you add categories) const categories = [...new Set(allPosts.flatMap(p => p.data.categories))]; ``` ### Modify Card Layout Change grid columns: ```css /* 4 columns on large screens */ grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr)); /* 2 columns max */ grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(400px, 1fr)); ``` ### Add Pagination For many posts: ```astro const POSTS_PER_PAGE = 12; const page = Number(Astro.url.searchParams.get('page')) || 1; const paginatedPosts = recentPosts.slice( (page - 1) * POSTS_PER_PAGE, page * POSTS_PER_PAGE ); ``` --- ## Best Practices ### Image Specifications **Featured post:** - Size: 1200x675px (16:9) - Format: JPG - Quality: 85% - Loading: eager **Grid cards:** - Size: 600x400px (3:2) - Format: JPG - Quality: 80% - Loading: lazy ### Tag Naming - **lowercase** - "design" not "Design" - **hyphenated** - "case-study" not "case study" - **specific** - "ui-design" not just "design" - **consistent** - Use same tags across posts ### Writing Card Content **Titles:** - 50-70 characters ideal - Front-load important words - No clickbait **Descriptions:** - 100-150 characters - Complete sentence - Entice without spoiling --- ## Analytics Recommendations Track these events: 1. **Featured post clicks** - Measure effectiveness 2. **Tag filter usage** - Which topics are popular 3. **Card clicks** - Which posts get attention 4. **Scroll depth** - How far users browse 5. **Newsletter CTA clicks** - Conversion rate --- ## Future Enhancements Consider adding: 1. **Search functionality** - Full-text search 2. **Load more button** - Instead of pagination 3. **Related posts** - "You might also like..." 4. **Author pages** - Filter by author 5. **RSS feed** - Already exists at `/blog/rss.xml` 6. **Reading progress** - Show how much user has read 7. **Share buttons** - Social sharing 8. **Bookmarking** - Save for later 9. **Comments** - Discussion system 10. **View count** - Popular posts indicator --- ## Troubleshooting ### Tags not filtering Check: 1. Posts have `tags` in frontmatter 2. JavaScript is enabled 3. `data-tags` attribute is valid JSON 4. Console for errors ### Cards have different heights Ensure: 1. `.card-link { height: 100%; }` 2. `.card-description { flex-grow: 1; }` 3. `.card-tags { margin-top: auto; }` ### Featured post not showing Verify: 1. At least one post exists 2. Post has all required fields 3. Array is not empty: `allPosts.length > 0` ### Images not loading Check: 1. CDN path is correct 2. Image exists at path 3. CDN service is running 4. No CORS issues --- ## Related Documentation - **Blog post template:** `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` - **MDX components:** `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` - **Design tokens:** `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` - **SEO guidelines:** `docs/09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` --- This blog index provides a modern, performant, and user-friendly way to discover and filter blog content while maintaining the design system and accessibility standards.

# SEO & Accessibility

## SEO Checklist

Every page must have:

- [ ] `<title>` tag (unique per page)
- [ ] `<meta name="description">` (unique, under 160 chars)
- [ ] `<link rel="canonical">` (absolute URL)
- [ ] Open Graph tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:type`, `og:url`)
- [ ] Twitter card tags (`twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`)
- [ ] Proper heading hierarchy (`h1` > `h2` > `h3`, never skip levels)
- [ ] `lang` attribute on `<html>`

### Site-Level SEO (Include From Inception)

These should be set up from the start of the project, not bolted on before launch:

- [ ] `@astrojs/sitemap` configured with `site` in `astro.config.mjs`
- [ ] RSS feed at `/blog/rss.xml` using `@astrojs/rss`
- [ ] `robots.txt` in `public/`
- [ ] JSON-LD structured data (`Organization`, `Article`, `BreadcrumbList`)
- [ ] `404.astro` page
- [ ] Canonical URLs in every page head
- [ ] Reusable `<SEO>` component in Base layout

## Accessibility

Non-negotiable baseline:

1. **Alt text on every image.** `alt` prop is required on `MediaImage`. Caption is not alt text.
2. **WCAG AA contrast.** 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text. Test with real content.
3. **Keyboard navigation.** All interactive elements (lightbox, slider, nav, filters) must work with keyboard.
4. **Reduced motion.** `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all non-essential animations.
5. **Semantic HTML.** `<article>` for entries, `<nav>` for navigation, `<main>` for content, proper heading hierarchy.
6. **Skip link.** First focusable element: "Skip to content."
7. **Focus management.** On View Transition navigation, focus moves to main content.

# Significa Website Significa's company website. Static site built with Astro v5, MDX for rich content, TypeScript strict. No CMS, no database, no server. Content lives in git as typed MDX/YAML files. Marketing team manages content directly with AI assistance. **Live site:** significa.co **Stack:** Astro v5 (latest stable), MDX, TypeScript strict, Content Collections with Zod, React (islands only) **Deploy:** Cloudflare Pages (static output to `dist/`) --- ## Commands ```bash pnpm dev # Development server with HMR pnpm build # Build static site to dist/ pnpm preview # Preview built site locally pnpm check # TypeScript + content validation (astro check) pnpm lint # Full lint pipeline: Prettier + type check + ESLint pnpm lint:check # ESLint only pnpm lint:fix # ESLint auto-fix pnpm format # Prettier auto-format src/ pnpm format:check # Prettier check (no write) ``` Before pushing: `pnpm lint && pnpm build` --- ## Critical Rules These always apply, regardless of the task. 1. **Static site. No exceptions.** No API routes, no server-side logic, no `fetch()` for content. Everything renders at build time. 2. **Astro components first.** React only for interactive islands with explicit `client:*` directives. If it can be done without JS, do it without JS. 3. **Content Collections are the database.** Use `getCollection()` and `getEntry()` from `astro:content`. Never `import.meta.glob`. Never raw file reads. 4. **References use `reference()`.** Cross-collection links must use Astro's `reference()` for build-time validation. Broken slugs break the build, not production. 5. **Fail at build time. Always.** The site is managed by marketing and non-technical people. Runtime errors are unacceptable. Use `reference()`, require fields, throw on invalid data. 6. **TypeScript strict.** Type all props with `interface Props`. No `any`. 7. **Semantic HTML.** `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, ``. Not everything is a `
`. 8. **No CSS framework.** Scoped `

# Significa.co - Project Memory

## Quick Reference
- **Stack:** Astro v5, MDX, TypeScript strict, React (islands only), Cloudflare Pages
- **Media:** S3 + Bunny CDN (`significa.b-cdn.net`) with Optimizer query params
- **Styles:** Scoped CSS + global tokens (OKLCH colors, 4px grid spacing, fluid clamp() typography)
- **Content:** 8 collections with Zod schemas, `reference()` for cross-links, filename-based drafts
- **Zero:** No CMS, no server, no CSS framework, no test framework, no dark mode

## Architecture
See [architecture.md](architecture.md) for full details.

## Design Tokens
See [design-tokens.md](design-tokens.md) for the complete token inventory.

## Content System
See [content-system.md](content-system.md) for schemas, collections, and patterns.

## Components
See [components.md](components.md) for the full component catalog.

## Routing
See [routing.md](routing.md) for all routes and data flow.

## Known Quirks
- `.prettierrc.mjs` has `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` configured but Tailwind is not used (vestigial)
- `draft: z.boolean()` exists in some schemas but is deprecated; filename convention (`*.draft.mdx`) is the source of truth
- No React components currently in use despite `@astrojs/react` being configured
- RSS endpoint exists at `/blog/rss.xml` but `@astrojs/rss` is not in the integrations array (works as API route)

1→# Content System
     2→
     3→## Collections (8 total)
     4→
     5→### MDX Collections
     6→| Collection | Extensions | Required Fields | References |
     7→|---|---|---|---|
     8→| projects | .mdx | title, date, thumbnail | relatedProjects → projects[] |
     9→| blog | .md, .mdx | title, date | relatedProjects → projects[] |
    10→| labs | .md, .mdx | title, description, date | none |
    11→| pages | .mdx | title | none |
    12→
    13→### YAML Collections
    14→| Collection | Required Fields | References |
    15→|---|---|---|
    16→| highlights | source (discriminated union), order | → projects/blog/labs |
    17→| clients | name, logo, order | none |
    18→| testimonials | quote, author, role, company, order | none |
    19→| awards | award, year, project, order | → projects |
    20→
    21→## Shared Schemas
    22→- `seoSchema`: metaTitle?, metaDescription?, ogImage? (used in projects, blog, labs, pages)
    23→- `metricSchema`: value (string), label (string) (used in projects)
    24→
    25→## Content Loader (`contentLoader()`)
    26→- Uses `glob()` with smart draft filtering
    27→- Dev: includes `*.draft.*` files
    28→- Prod: excludes `*.draft.*` at glob level
    29→- ID generation: strips file extension for clean URL slugs
    30→
    31→## Draft System
    32→- Filename-based: `my-post.draft.mdx`
    33→- Publish = rename: remove `.draft` from filename
    34→- References to drafts break production build (correct behavior)
    35→- `draft: z.boolean()` in schemas is deprecated, filename is source of truth
    36→
    37→## Reference Pattern
    38→```typescript
    39→// Schema: reference() for build-time validation
    40→relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([])
    41→
    42→// Returns stub: { id: string, collection: string }
    43→// Resolve with: await getEntry(ref)
    44→```
    45→
    46→## Highlights Discriminated Union
    47→```typescript
    48→source: z.discriminatedUnion("collection", [
    49→  z.object({ collection: z.literal("projects"), entry: reference("projects") }),
    50→  z.object({ collection: z.literal("blog"), entry: reference("blog") }),
    51→  z.object({ collection: z.literal("labs"), entry: reference("labs") }),
    52→])
    53→```
    54→
    55→## Collection Helpers (`src/lib/collections.ts`)
    56→- `getProjects()` - sorted by date DESC
    57→- `getPosts()` - sorted by date DESC
    58→- `getLabs()` - sorted by date DESC
    59→- `getClients()` - sorted by order ASC
    60→- `getTestimonials()` - sorted by order ASC
    61→- `getResolvedHighlights()` - highlights with full referenced entries
    62→- `getAwards()` - awards with resolved project references
    63→
    64→## CDN Integration (`src/lib/cdn.ts`)
    65→- `CDN_HOSTNAME`: `https://significa.b-cdn.net`
    66→- `cdnUrl(src, options)`: builds full URL with Optimizer params
    67→- `cdnSrcset(src, options)`: generates responsive srcset (640, 960, 1280, 1920)
    68→- `DEFAULT_QUALITY`: 80
    69→- Content authors use relative paths; components prepend CDN hostname
    70→
    71→## Build-Time Errors (`src/lib/content-errors.ts`)
    72→```typescript
    73→contentError(component, file, message) // throws with "[Component] in file: message\nFix the content and rebuild."
    74→```
    75→

| highlights | source (discriminated union), order | → projects/blog/labs |
| clients | name, logo, order | none |
| testimonials | quote, author, role, company, order | none |
| awards | award, year, project, order | → projects |

## Shared Schemas
- `seoSchema`: metaTitle?, metaDescription?, ogImage? (used in projects, blog, labs, pages)
- `metricSchema`: value (string), label (string) (used in projects)

## Content Loader (`contentLoader()`)
- Uses `glob()` with smart draft filtering
- Dev: includes `*.draft.*` files
- Prod: excludes `*.draft.*` at glob level
- ID generation: strips file extension for clean URL slugs

## Draft System
- Filename-based: `my-post.draft.mdx`
- Publish = rename: remove `.draft` from filename
- References to drafts break production build (correct behavior)
- `draft: z.boolean()` in schemas is deprecated, filename is source of truth

## Reference Pattern
```typescript
// Schema: reference() for build-time validation
relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([])

// Returns stub: { id: string, collection: string }
// Resolve with: await getEntry(ref)
```

## Highlights Discriminated Union
```typescript
source: z.discriminatedUnion("collection", [
  z.object({ collection: z.literal("projects"), entry: reference("projects") }),
  z.object({ collection: z.literal("blog"), entry: reference("blog") }),
  z.object({ collection: z.literal("labs"), entry: reference("labs") }),
])
```

## Collection Helpers (`src/lib/collections.ts`)
- `getProjects()` - sorted by date DESC
- `getPosts()` - sorted by date DESC
- `getLabs()` - sorted by date DESC
- `getClients()` - sorted by order ASC
- `getTestimonials()` - sorted by order ASC
- `getResolvedHighlights()` - highlights with full referenced entries
- `getAwards()` - awards with resolved project references

## CDN Integration (`src/lib/cdn.ts`)
- `CDN_HOSTNAME`: `https://significa.b-cdn.net`
- `cdnUrl(src, options)`: builds full URL with Optimizer params
- `cdnSrcset(src, options)`: generates responsive srcset (640, 960, 1280, 1920)
- `DEFAULT_QUALITY`: 80
- Content authors use relative paths; components prepend CDN hostname

## Build-Time Errors (`src/lib/content-errors.ts`)
```typescript
contentError(component, file, message) // throws with "[Component] in file: message\nFix the content and rebuild."
```

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Consolidate 21 documentation files into 7 domain-scoped reference docs, update CLAUDE.md and memory/, eliminating ~75% token waste while preserving all project-specific information.

**Architecture:** Two-layer docs system. Layer 1: docs/ has 7 reference files (one per domain). Layer 2: memory/ has dense LLM-optimized summaries. CLAUDE.md is the entry point with routing table, documentation conventions, and model routing rules.

**Tech Stack:** Markdown files only. No code changes.

---

### Task 1: Create new 01-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/01-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` (new consolidated version)

**Step 1: Write the new file**

- Old `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` (schemas, helpers, drafts, relationships)
- Old `05-RULES.md` (collision check, contentError, common mistakes)
- Old `06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md` (2 project-specific gotchas)

Structure:
```
# Content Schema & Validation

## Config Location
- src/content.config.ts (root of src/, not src/content/)

## Content Loader
- contentLoader() code with draftFilter
- Draft filtering: glob-level, not query-time
- ID generation: strips extensions

## Collections
- Shared schemas (seoSchema, metricSchema) - code block
- All 10 collection definitions with Zod schemas - code blocks
- Handbook hierarchy explanation (brief, link to 04-HANDBOOK.md)
- Export block

## Draft System
- .draft filename suffix
- Dev vs prod behavior table
